            <div class="md-content" data-md-component="content">
              <article class="md-content__inner md-typeset">
                
                  


  
  


<div align="center">
<img src="circom-logo-black.png" width="460px" align="center"/>
</div>

<p><a href="https://t.me/iden3io"><img alt="Chat on Telegram" src="https://img.shields.io/badge/@iden3-2CA5E0.svg?style=flat-square&amp;logo=telegram&amp;label=Telegram" /></a>
<a href="https://iden3.io/circom"><img alt="Website" src="https://img.shields.io/website?up_color=blue&amp;up_message=circom&amp;url=https%3A%2F%2Fiden3.io%2Fcircom" /></a>
<a href="https://github.com/iden3/circom"><img alt="GitHub repo" src="https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/iden3/circom?color=blue" /></a>
<img alt="Issues" src="https://img.shields.io/github/issues-raw/iden3/circom?color=blue" />
<img alt="GitHub top language" src="https://img.shields.io/github/languages/top/iden3/circom" />
<img alt="Contributors" src="https://img.shields.io/github/contributors-anon/iden3/circom?color=blue" /></p>
<hr />
<h1 id="start-here"><div align="center"><b><a href="https://iden3.github.io/circom/getting-started/installation/">Start here</a></b></div></h1>
<hr />
<h1 id="important-deprecation-note">&#9888; Important deprecation note</h1>
<p>Current <code>circom</code> is a compiler written in Rust. The old <code>circom</code> compiler written in Javascript will be frozen, but it can still be downloaded from the <a href="https://github.com/iden3/circom_old">old circom repository</a>.</p>
<hr />
<h1 id="about-the-circom-ecosystem">About the circom ecosystem</h1>
<p>The circom compiler and its ecosystem of tools allows you to create, test and create zero knowledge proofs for your circuits.</p>
<h2 id="circom">circom</h2>
<p><code>circom</code> is a compiler written in Rust for compiling circuits written in the <code>circom</code> language.
The compiler outputs the representation of the circuit as constraints and everything needed to compute different ZK proofs. </p>
<h2 id="circomlib">circomlib</h2>
<p>With <code>circom</code>, it is possible to create large circuits by combining smaller generic circuits called <code>templates</code>. The <code>circomlib</code> is a library of <code>circom</code> templates that contains hundreds of circuits such as comparators, hash functions, digital signatures, binary and decimal convertors and many more. You can also create your custom templates, but before start coding, we recommend you to take a look at our already created templates.</p>
<p>The package already contains tests for circuits available in the <code>circomlib</code>.
The package also installs the npm packages <code>circomlibjs</code>, <code>circom_tester</code> and <code>ffjavascript</code> as dependencies.</p>
<h2 id="circomlibjs">circomlibjs</h2>
<p><code>circomlibjs</code> is a Javascript library that provides programs to compute the witness of several circuits of <code>circomlib</code>.
This library is used to check that the witness computed 
using the wasm or c code generated by <code>circom</code> from many circuits in the <code>circomlib</code> match the ones generated by the corresponding Javascript program in <code>circomlibjs</code>.</p>
<p>The package includes these programs in the src directory. In the 
test directory it includes its own tests. In the tools directory it includes programs to precompute some needed parameters.</p>
<h2 id="circom_tester">circom_tester</h2>
<p>The <code>circomtester</code> is a npm package that provides tools for testing <code>circom</code> circuits.</p>
<h2 id="ffjavascript">ffjavascript</h2>
<p>The <code>ffjavascript</code> is a npm package with Javascript code to perform finite field operations in Javascript. </p>
<h2 id="snarkjs">snarkjs</h2>
<p><code>snarkjs</code> is a npm package that contains code to generate and validate ZK proofs from the artifacts produced by <code>circom</code>. </p>
<h1 id="visual-summary">Visual summary <a id="visual-summary"></a></h1>
<p><img alt="" src="https://gblobscdn.gitbook.com/assets%2F-MDt-cjMfCLyy351MraT%2F-ME35kSLplV3Z39JJsLE%2F-ME37Q2MlDc67k0-jzQS%2Fcircomsnarkjs.png?alt=media&amp;token=4b1b1c11-a1d4-4048-8c3a-0c7b02f4930a" /></p>












                
              </article>

<style id="wp-block-spacer-inline-css">
.wp-block-spacer{clear:both}
/*# sourceURL=https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/blocks/spacer/style.min.css */
</style>
<style id="wp-block-post-date-inline-css">
.wp-block-post-date{box-sizing:border-box}
/*# sourceURL=https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/blocks/post-date/style.min.css */
</style>
<style id="wp-block-post-terms-inline-css">
.wp-block-post-terms{box-sizing:border-box}.wp-block-post-terms .wp-block-post-terms__separator{white-space:pre-wrap}
/*# sourceURL=https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/blocks/post-terms/style.min.css */
</style>
<style id="wp-block-post-author-inline-css">
.wp-block-post-author{box-sizing:border-box;display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap}.wp-block-post-author__byline{font-size:.5em;margin-bottom:0;margin-top:0;width:100%}.wp-block-post-author__avatar{margin-right:1em}.wp-block-post-author__bio{font-size:.7em;margin-bottom:.7em}.wp-block-post-author__content{flex-basis:0;flex-grow:1}.wp-block-post-author__name{margin:0}
/*# sourceURL=https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/blocks/post-author/style.min.css */
</style>
<style id="wp-block-columns-inline-css">
.wp-block-columns{box-sizing:border-box;display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap!important}@media (min-width:782px){.wp-block-columns{flex-wrap:nowrap!important}}.wp-block-columns{align-items:normal!important}.wp-block-columns.are-vertically-aligned-top{align-items:flex-start}.wp-block-columns.are-vertically-aligned-center{align-items:center}.wp-block-columns.are-vertically-aligned-bottom{align-items:flex-end}@media (max-width:781px){.wp-block-columns:not(.is-not-stacked-on-mobile)>.wp-block-column{flex-basis:100%!important}}@media (min-width:782px){.wp-block-columns:not(.is-not-stacked-on-mobile)>.wp-block-column{flex-basis:0;flex-grow:1}.wp-block-columns:not(.is-not-stacked-on-mobile)>.wp-block-column[style*=flex-basis]{flex-grow:0}}.wp-block-columns.is-not-stacked-on-mobile{flex-wrap:nowrap!important}.wp-block-columns.is-not-stacked-on-mobile>.wp-block-column{flex-basis:0;flex-grow:1}.wp-block-columns.is-not-stacked-on-mobile>.wp-block-column[style*=flex-basis]{flex-grow:0}:where(.wp-block-columns){margin-bottom:1.75em}:where(.wp-block-columns.has-background){padding:1.25em 2.375em}.wp-block-column{flex-grow:1;min-width:0;overflow-wrap:break-word;word-break:break-word}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-top{align-self:flex-start}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-center{align-self:center}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-bottom{align-self:flex-end}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-stretch{align-self:stretch}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-bottom,.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-center,.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-top{width:100%}
/*# sourceURL=https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/blocks/columns/style.min.css */
</style>
<style id="wp-emoji-styles-inline-css">

	img.wp-smiley, img.emoji {
		display: inline !important;
		border: none !important;
		box-shadow: none !important;
		height: 1em !important;
		width: 1em !important;
		margin: 0 0.07em !important;
		vertical-align: -0.1em !important;
		background: none !important;
		padding: 0 !important;
	}
/*# sourceURL=wp-emoji-styles-inline-css */
</style>

}.has-global-padding > .alignfull { margin-right: calc(var(--wp--style--root--padding-right) * -1); margin-left: calc(var(--wp--style--root--padding-left) * -1); }.has-global-padding :where(:not(.alignfull.is-layout-flow) > .has-global-padding:not(.wp-block-block, .alignfull)) { padding-right: 0; padding-left: 0; }.has-global-padding :where(:not(.alignfull.is-layout-flow) > .has-global-padding:not(.wp-block-block, .alignfull)) > .alignfull { margin-left: 0; margin-right: 0; }.wp-site-blocks > .alignleft { float: left; margin-right: 2em; }.wp-site-blocks > .alignright { float: right; margin-left: 2em; }.wp-site-blocks > .aligncenter { justify-content: center; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; }:where(.wp-site-blocks) > * { margin-block-start: 1.5rem; margin-block-end: 0; }:where(.wp-site-blocks) > :first-child { margin-block-start: 0; }:where(.wp-site-blocks) > :last-child { margin-block-end: 0; }:root { --wp--style--block-gap: 1.5rem; }:root :where(.is-layout-constrained) > *{margin-block-start: 1.5rem;margin-block-end: 0;}:root :where(.is-layout-flex){gap: 1.5rem;}:root :where(.is-layout-grid){gap: 1.5rem;}.is-layout-flow > .alignleft{float: left;margin-inline-start: 0;margin-inline-end: 2em;}.is-layout-flow > .alignright{float: right;margin-inline-start: 2em;margin-inline-end: 0;}.is-layout-flow > .aligncenter{margin-left: auto !important;margin-right: auto !important;}.is-layout-constrained > .alignleft{float: left;margin-inline-start: 0;margin-inline-end: 2em;}.is-layout-constrained > <main id="wp--skip-link--target" class="wp-block-group is-layout-flow wp-block-group-is-layout-flow" style="margin-top:var(--wp--preset--spacing--50)">
<div class="wp-block-group has-global-padding is-layout-constrained wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained"><h1 style="margin-bottom:var(--wp--preset--spacing--40)" class="wp-block-post-title has-large-font-size">Circom Groth16 on Stellar</h1>

<figure class="wp-block-post-featured-image"><img width="1645" height="700" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Circom-x-Stellar.png" class="has-border-color wp-post-image lazyload" alt="Circom x Stellar" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 1645px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 1645/700;border-color:#212121;border-radius:33px;border-width:6px;object-fit:cover;" decoding="async" data-srcset="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Circom-x-Stellar.png 1645w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Circom-x-Stellar-300x128.png 300w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Circom-x-Stellar-1024x436.png 1024w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Circom-x-Stellar-768x327.png 768w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Circom-x-Stellar-1536x654.png 1536w" data-sizes="(max-width: 1645px) 100vw, 1645px" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" /></figure></div>


<div class="entry-content wp-block-post-content has-global-padding is-layout-constrained wp-block-post-content-is-layout-constrained">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph">This walkthrough shows how to take a Circom Groth16 proof and verify it This walkthrough shows how to take a Circom Groth16 proof and verify it inside a Stellar smart contract.</p>



<div class="video-wrapper" onclick="if (!window.__cfRLUnblockHandlers) return false; window.open('https://www.youtube.com/c/JamesBachini?sub_confirmation=1');" data-cf-modified-863158123ef3f65ba78de5ad-="">
	<div class="video-header">
		<div class="video-channel">James On YouTube</div>
		<img decoding="async" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/04/youtubeSubscribeButton.png" class="video-button lazyload" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 500px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 500/111;" />
	</div>
	<!-- Copy & Pasted from YouTube -->
	<iframe data-src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/cNyKOl_uLiI?rel=0&amp;hd=1" allowfullscreen="" width="560" height="349" frameborder="0" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" class="lazyload" data-load-mode="1"></iframe>
</div>
<div class="text-center">Watch On YouTube: <a href="https://youtu.be/cNyKOl_uLiI" target="_blank" rel="noopener">https://youtu.be/cNyKOl_uLiI</a> | <a href="https://www.youtube.com/c/JamesBachini?sub_confirmation=1" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Subscribe</a> </div>




<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The circuit is intentionally trivial (<em>a * b = c</em>). </p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Given two private inputs<em> A &amp; B</em> let&#8217;s check they multiply correctly to a public output <em>C</em>.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Note all the code for this is open source on Github and you can clone it and follow along if you wish:<br><a href="https://github.com/jamesbachini/CircomStellar" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">https://github.com/jamesbachini/CircomStellar</a></p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Here is what that looks like in a Circom circuit:</p>



<pre class="wp-block-code"><code>pragma circom 2.0.0;

template Multiplier2 () {    
   signal input a;  
   signal input b;  
   signal output c;  
   c &lt;== a * b;  
}

component main = Multiplier2();</code></pre>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">So we are creating a new circuit using the template keyword, defining some input and ouput signals and then setting the constraint.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">There is a more detailed description of this circuit here: <a href="https://docs.circom.io/getting-started/writing-circuits/">https://docs.circom.io/getting-started/writing-circuits/</a></p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">This circuit lives in a .circom file inside a circuits/ directory.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">We can then pass our signals into a javascript library called snarkjs to generate a proof. This will output a zero-knowledge Groth16 proof along with the public ouput and a verification key. These can be passed to a Stellar Groth16 verifier contract to see if the proof is valid.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">There is a frontend React site and backend shell script to demonstrate this in the repo.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Let&#8217;s look at the backend first as it&#8217;s a little cleaner to understand.</p>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Server-side Verification</h2>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">To run it locally you&#8217;ll need <a href="https://rust-lang.org/">Rust</a>, <a href="https://nodejs.org/">Node</a> and <a href="https://github.com/stellar/stellar-cli">Stellar Cli</a> installed.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">You can then clone the repo and run the demo.sh script. If you are using windows then I&#8217;d recommend running it in <a href="https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/install">WSL</a>.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-code"><code>git clone https://github.com/jamesbachini/CircomStellar.git
cd CircomStellar
./demo.sh</code></pre>



<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><a href="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-4.png"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="552" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-4-1024x552.png" alt="Circom verifier Rust" class="wp-image-8167 lazyload" data-srcset="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-4-1024x552.png 1024w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-4-300x162.png 300w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-4-768x414.png 768w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-4-1536x828.png 1536w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-4-2048x1104.png 2048w" data-sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 1024px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 1024/552;" /></a></figure>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Let&#8217;s take a look at that demo script to see what is happening here: <a href="https://github.com/jamesbachini/CircomStellar/blob/main/demo.sh">https://github.com/jamesbachini/CircomStellar/blob/main/demo.sh</a></p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">So we use npm to install snarkjs, build and deploy the Stellar Groth16 verifier contract, then generates a proof from a precomputed witness using <em>snarkjs groth16 prove</em> and export the corresponding verification key.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Before touching the chain, it verifies the proof locally with <em>snarkjs groth16 verify</em> to isolate proving correctness from integration errors. Then we convet the JSON verification key, proof, and public inputs into canonical hex encoded byte arrays that match the contract&#8217;s expected layout.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Those bytes are then passed to the verifier contract. The script asserts that the on-chain result is true, effectively proving that a*b=c on Stellar testnet.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Now let&#8217;s take a look at doing this in the frontend.</p>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Frontend Verification</h2>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The frontend follows a very similar process using snarkjs to create a proof. We then use the <a href="https://www.npmjs.com/package/@stellar/stellar-sdk">@stellar/stellar-sdk</a> to send this through to the contract.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">It provides a visually useful demo of what is going on.</p>



<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><a href="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-5.png"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="600" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-5-1024x600.png" alt="Circom frontend verifier snarkjs" class="wp-image-8168 lazyload" data-srcset="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-5-1024x600.png 1024w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-5-300x176.png 300w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-5-768x450.png 768w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-5-1536x899.png 1536w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-5-2048x1199.png 2048w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-5.png 1920w" data-sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 1024px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 1024/600;" /></a></figure>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">You can run this locally by cloning the repo as before then moving into the frontend, installing dependencies, editing the .env file (get keys and testnet funds from <a href="https://lab.stellar.org">https://lab.stellar.org</a>) and running <em>npm run dev</em></p>



<pre class="wp-block-code"><code>git clone https://github.com/jamesbachini/CircomStellar.git
cd CircomStellar/frontend
npm install
mv .env.example .env
nano .env
npm run dev</code></pre>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">You should then get a local dev server fired up and you can open a browser on localhost:5173</p>



<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><a href="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-6.png"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="218" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-6-1024x218.png" alt="" class="wp-image-8169 lazyload" data-srcset="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-6-1024x218.png 1024w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-6-300x64.png 300w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-6-768x164.png 768w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-6.png 1482w" data-sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 1024px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 1024/218;" /></a></figure>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">I hope you enjoyed this tutorial and it shed some light on how we can combine Circom circuits with Stellar smart contracts.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">For more information check out the following resources:</p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><a href="https://docs.circom.io/">https://docs.circom.io/</a></li>



<li><a href="https://developers.stellar.org/">https://developers.stellar.org/</a></li>
</ul>
</div>


<div class="wp-block-group has-global-padding is-layout-constrained wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained">
<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Get <a rel="noreferrer noopener" href="https://bachini.substack.com" target="_blank">The Blockchain Sector Newsletter</a>, binge the <a rel="noreferrer noopener" href="https://www.youtube.com/c/JamesBachini?sub_confirmation=1" target="_blank">YouTube</a> channel and connect with me on <a href="https://twitter.com/intent/follow?screen_name=james_bachini" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Twitter</a></p>

<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><em>The Blockchain Sector</em> newsletter goes out a few times a month when there is breaking news or interesting developments to discuss. All the content I produce is free, if you&#8217;d like to help please share this content on social media.</p>



<p class="has-text-align-center wp-block-paragraph">Thank you.</p>



<figure class="wp-block-image aligncenter size-full is-resized"><a href="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/signature_grey.png"><img data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/signature_grey.png" alt="James Bachini" class="wp-image-5039 lazyload" width="150" height="68" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 150px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 150/68;" /></a></figure>



<p class="has-text-color has-small-font-size wp-block-paragraph" style="color:#828689"><strong>Disclaimer</strong>: Not a financial advisor, not financial advice. The content I create is to document my journey and for educational and entertainment purposes only. It is not under any circumstances investment advice. I am not an investment or trading professional and am learning myself while still making plenty of mistakes along the way. Any code published is experimental and not production ready to be used for financial transactions. Do your own research and do not play with funds you do not want to lose. </p>
</div>

<!-- Substack Overlay -->
<script type="863158123ef3f65ba78de5ad-text/javascript">
const showOverlay = function() {
  document.body.innerHTML += `<div id="overlay-substack" style='display: block; z-index: 9999999; position: fixed; left: 0; right: 0; top: 0; bottom: 0; background: #111111; text-align: center;'><div style='width: 100%; text-align: right;'><div id='overlay-close' style='font-size: 26px; color: #555; cursor: pointer; padding: 20px;'>X</div></div><h1>Get The Latest Insider Blockchain News</h1><iframe data-src='https://bachini.substack.com/embed' width='300' height='320' style='border:1px solid #EEE; background:white; margin: 10px auto;' frameborder='0' scrolling='no' src='data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==' class='lazyload' data-load-mode='1'></iframe><div style='width: 320px; text-align: left; margin: 10px auto; line-height: 200%;'>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f4f0')} Be First To Hear Breaking News<br>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f50d')} Explainers For DeFi Protocols<br>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f4c8')} Independent Market Commentary<br>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f393')} Web3 & Solidity Dev Tutorials<br>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f4b0')} Free Newsletter, For Everyone<br><div style="font-size: 0.9em; width: 100%; text-align: center;"><a href="https://bachini.substack.com" target="_blank">https://bachini.substack.com</a></div></div></div>`;
  document.getElementById('overlay-close').onclick = function() {
    document.getElementById('overlay-substack').style.display = 'none';
  };
}
const myScrollFunc = function() {
  const y = window.scrollY;
  const lastOverlay = localStorage.lastOverlay || 0;
  const ts = new Date().getTime(); 
  if (y >= 3000 && lastOverlay < ts -  604800000) {
    localStorage.setItem('lastOverlay', ts);
    if (localStorage.getItem('lastOverlay') == ts) {
      showOverlay();
    }
  }
};
setTimeout(function() {
  window.addEventListener('scroll', myScrollFunc);
}, 30000);
</script>
<!-- End Substack Overlay -->

<script type="863158123ef3f65ba78de5ad-text/javascript">
window.lastScrollTime = false;
window.addEventListener('scroll', function() {
	window.lastScrollTime = new Date().getTime();
});
setTimeout(async function() {
	if (gtag && window.lastScrollTime) {
		gtag('event','timedScroll', {
			'event_category': 'blog'
		});
	}
}, 35000);
</script>

<style>
.single-post img {
  max-width: 100%;
}
.single-post a {
  word-break: break-all;
}
a, a:visited, a:active {
    color: #EEE !important;
}
a:hover {
    color: #FFF !important;
}
.wp-block-navigation__submenu-container {
    background: #111111EE !important;
}
.wp-block-navigation__responsive-container-content {
    background: #111111EE !important;
}
.table td, .table tr, .table th {
  padding: 5px;
  border: 1px solid #444;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #444 !important;
}
.table-center td, .table-center th {
  text-align: center !important;
}
.table  th {
  font-weight: 400;
  background: #23282d;
  color: #FFF00F;
}
.table tr {
  background: #111;
}
.table tr:nth-child(even) {
  background: #000;
}
.video-wrapper {
  position: relative;
  padding-top: 25px;
  width: 100%;
  padding-bottom: 380px;
}
.video-wrapper iframe {
  position: absolute;
  top: 80px;
  left: 0;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: 400px;
}
.video-header {
  background: #000;
  color: #EEE;
  width: 100%;
  height: 60px;
}
.video-channel {
  font-size: 1.3em;
  font-weight: bold;
  float: left;
  margin-left: 10px;
  margin-top: 15px;
}
.video-button {
  float: right;
  height: 40px;
  margin-right: 10px;
  margin-top: 10px;
  max-width: 33%;
  cursor: pointer
}
.entry-inner {
  word-wrap: break-word;
}
.responsive-image {
  max-width: 100%;
  max-height: 100%;
}  
.text-small {
  font-size: 0.8em;
}
.screenshot {
  border: 3px solid #444;
  border-radius: 5px;
  box-shadow: 0 0 5px #222;
  -webkit-box-shadow: 0 0 5px #222;
  -moz-box-shadow: 0 0 5px #222;
  box-sizing: border-box;
  -moz-box-sizing: border-box;
  -webkit-box-sizing: border-box;
}
.standout {
  background: #000;
  border-left: 5px solid #FFF00F;
  padding: 10px 10px 10px 20px;
  font-size: 1.1em;
}
.bullet {
  float: left;
  font-weight: bold;
  border-radius: 99px;
  background: #FFF00F;
  width: 27px;
  height: 27px;
  text-align: center;
  margin-right: 10px;
  color: #FFF;
}
code {
    background: #070707;
    color: #CCC;
    border: 3px solid #444;
    border-radius: 5px;
    padding: 10px;
    font-size: 0.8em;
}
.console {
  background: #000 !important;
  color: #6F0;
  border-radius: 3px;
  padding: 2px 4px;
}
.console-error {
  color: #F44;
}
.console code {
  background: #000 !important;
  color: #6F0;
}
.wp-block-post-title {
  font-weight: bold;
}
.wp-block-post-title, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 {
  background-image: linear-gradient(to right, #FFF00F, #f85050);
  color: transparent;
  background-clip: text;
  background: -webkit-linear-gradient(left, #FFF00F, #f85050);
  -webkit-background-clip: text;
  -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent;
}
@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
  .mobile-hidden {
    display: none;
  }
}
</style><script data-wp-router-options="{&quot;loadOnClientNavigation&quot;:true}" fetchpriority="low" id="@wordpress/block-library/navigation/view-js-module" src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/js/dist/script-modules/block-library/navigation/view.min.js?ver=96a846e1d7b789c39ab9" type="863158123ef3f65ba78de5ad-module"></script>

Tokens on Stellar — A Smart-Contract Approach | Meridian 2025","lengthSeconds":"1250","keywords":["stellar","stellar.org","cryptocurrency","payment protocol","bitcoin","xlm","blockchain","stellar development foundation","lumens","SDF","smart contracts","payment rails"],"channelId":"UC4BrVpvKK0r2zP9xVFQcPSA","isOwnerViewing":false,"shortDescription":"This talk presents Confidential Tokens on Stellar - a token extension enabling private, verifiable value transfer on a public ledger. Using zero-knowledge cryptography, balances and amounts remain hidden while view keys allow selective disclosure to specific third parties. Through a live demo, we invite developers, asset issuers, and ecosystem partners to think together about what privacy and compliance should look like in a decentralized, permissionless setting.\n\nJay Tokens on Stellar — A Smart-Contract Approach | Meridian 2025"},"description":{"simpleText":"This talk presents Confidential Tokens on Stellar - a token extension enabling private, verifiable value transfer on a public ledger. Using zero-knowledge cryptography, balances and amounts remain hidden while view keys allow selective disclosure to specific third parties. Through a live demo, we invite developers, asset issuers, and ecosystem partners to think together about what privacy and compliance should look like in a decentralized, permissionless setting.\n\nJay talk presents Confidential Tokens on Stellar - a token extension enabling private, verifiable value transfer on a public ledger. Using zero-knowledge cryptography, balances and amounts remain hidden while view keys allow selective disclosure to specific third parties. private, verifiable value transfer on a public ledger. Using zero-knowledge cryptography, balances and amounts remain hidden while view keys allow selective disclosure to specific third parties. /* Ensure all elements inherit the color from its parent */
body * {
    color: inherit;
}

a,
.w-input,
.w-select,
.w-tab-link,
.w-nav-link,
.w-slider-arrow-left,
.w-slider-arrow-right,
.w-dropdown-btn,
.w-dropdown-toggle,
.w-dropdown-link {
  color: inherit;
  text-decoration: inherit;
  font-size: inherit;
}

/* Focus state style for keyboard navigation for the focusable elements */
*[tabindex]:focus-visible,
  input[type="file"]:focus-visible {
   outline: 0.125rem solid #4d65ff;
   outline-offset: 0.125rem;
}

/* Get rid of top margin on first element in any rich text element */
.w-richtext > :not(div):first-child, .w-richtext > div:first-child > :first-child {
  margin-top: 0 !important;
}

/* Get rid of bottom margin on last element in any rich text element */
.w-richtext>:last-child, .w-richtext ol li:last-child, .w-richtext ul li:last-child {
	margin-bottom: 0 !important;
}

/* Prevent all click and hover interaction with an element */
.pointer-events-off {
	pointer-events: none;
}

/* Enables all click and hover interaction with an element */
.pointer-events-on {
  pointer-events: auto;
}

/* Create a class of .div-square which maintains a 1:1 dimension of a div */
.div-square::after {
	content: "";
	display: block;
	padding-bottom: 100%;
}

/* Make sure containers never lose their center alignment */
.container-medium,.container-small, .container-large {
	margin-right: auto !important;
  margin-left: auto !important;
}


/* Apply "..." after 3 lines of text */
.text-style-3lines {
	display: -webkit-box;
	overflow: hidden;
	-webkit-line-clamp: 3;
	-webkit-box-orient: vertical;
}

/* Apply "..." after 2 lines of text */
.text-style-2lines {
	display: -webkit-box;
	overflow: hidden;
	-webkit-line-clamp: 2;
	-webkit-box-orient: vertical;
}

/* Adds inline flex display */
.display-inlineflex {
  display: inline-flex;
}

/* These classes are never overwritten */
.hide {
  display: none !important;
}

@media screen and (max-width: 991px) {
    .hide, .hide-tablet {
        display: none !important;
    }
}
  @media screen and (max-width: 767px) {
    .hide-mobile-landscape{
      display: none !important;
    }
}
  @media screen and (max-width: 479px) {
    .hide-mobile{
      display: none !important;
    }
}
 
.margin-0 {
  margin: 0rem !important;
}

}

/* Apply "..." at 100% width */

confidentiality</p></div></div></div></div><div id="w-node-_724bd5d2-bb70-d113-c101-60479747c9bf-cd96ee1d"><p blocks-name="paragraph-3" class="number">[06]</p><div blocks-name="layout396_card-3" class="layout396_card"><div blocks-name="layout396_card-content-3" blocks-slot-children="ST265" class="layout396_card-content"><div blocks-slot-children="ST265" blocks-name="layout396_card-content-top-3" class="layout396_card-content-top"><p blocks-name="paragraph-4" class="text-align-center text-weight-medium">Fully onchain</p></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="w-embed"><style>
.rotate {
  animation: rotation 10s infinite linear;
}

@keyframes rotation {
  from {
    transform: rotate(0deg);
  }
  to {
    transform: rotate(359deg);
  }
}
</style></div></div></header><header class="section_header19"><div class="padding-global"><div class="container-medium"><div class="padding-section-large"><div class="header19_component"><div class="w-layout-grid header19_content"><div id="w-node-a1ad2c51-797d-0f7a-c133-046f71739a15-cd96ee1d" class="header19_image-wrapper"><div data-w-id="cd7be1dd-d480-214d-18a7-4c11f84a3290" data-animation-type="lottie" data-src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/67b5199018e2c328cd96edc0/67b7c913457186c9fc7a975e_Flow%201%20(7).json" data-loop="1" data-direction="1" data-autoplay="1" data-is-ix2-target="0" data-renderer="svg" data-default-duration="0" data-duration="2.2" data-loading="eager"></div></div><div id="w-node-a1ad2c51-797d-0f7a-c133-046f71739a17-cd96ee1d" class="header19_content-right"><p class="heading-style-h4">Blockchains are poised to achieve mainstream adoption, but are missing one key feature: confidentiality. <span class="text-span">The goal of the Confidential Token Association is to push forward onchain confidentiality by leveraging recent advances in encryption to build a common token standard.</span></p></div></div></div></div></div></div></header><section class="section_layout395"><div class="padding-global"><div class="container-medium"><div class="padding-section-large"><div class="layout395_component"><div class="margin-bottom margin-xxlarge"><div class="text-align-center"><div class="max-width-large align-center"><div class="margin-bottom margin-small"><h2 class="heading-style-h2">What is the confidential token standard?</h2></div><p class="text-size-medium">The confidential token standard is a framework for empowering token standards such as ERC20 with confidentiality. Using encryption-based approaches, the confidential token standard enables users to hide the amount transferred between addresses while keeping the flow of funds visible.</p></div></div></div><div class="w-layout-grid layout395_grid-list"><div id="w-node-_71fba3a5-c86c-4464-0dba-7d1fa8c1d3d4-cd96ee1d" class="w-layout-grid layout395_row"><div class="layout395_card card1"><div id="w-node-_71fba3a5-c86c-4464-0dba-7d1fa8c1d3d6-cd96ee1d" class="layout395_card-image-wrapper"><div class="s_container"><img loading="lazy" src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/67b5199018e2c328cd96edc0/67b52302bda01a3920991acc_a.svg" alt="" class="layout395_card-image"/></div></div><div class="layout395_card-content"><div class="layout395_card-content-top"><div class="margin-bottom margin-xsmall"><h3 class="heading-style-h4">Payment</h3></div><p>Payments and transfers represent a huge adoption opportunity for crypto, but without confidentiality, simple use cases such as sending money to a friend, remittance, or executing payroll onchain will not reach the mainstream.</p></div></div></div><div class="layout395_card card2"><div id="w-node-_71fba3a5-c86c-4464-0dba-7d1fa8c1d3e9-cd96ee1d" class="layout395_card-image-wrapper"><div class="s_container"><img loading="lazy" src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/67b5199018e2c328cd96edc0/67b5232903005f1584dd843d_b.svg" alt="" class="layout395_card-image"/></div></div><div class="layout395_card-content"><div class="layout395_card-content-top"><div class="margin-bottom margin-xsmall"><h3 class="heading-style-h4">DeFi</h3></div><p>With every transaction visible onchain, DeFi users require a way to keep transaction details confidential for basic privacy reasons as well as to protect against MEV. Builders need a way to supercharge their apps with confidentiality without sacrificing composability.</p></div></div></div><div class="layout395_card card3"><div id="w-node-_71fba3a5-c86c-4464-0dba-7d1fa8c1d3fc-cd96ee1d" class="layout395_card-image-wrapper"><div class="s_container"><img loading="lazy" src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/67b5199018e2c328cd96edc0/67b5235272c5887ba3762ecd_c.svg" alt="" class="layout395_card-image"/></div></div><div class="layout395_card-content"><div class="layout395_card-content-top"><div class="margin-bottom margin-xsmall"><h3 class="heading-style-h4">RWAs</h3></div><p>Financial institutions are ready to bring their assets onchain, but are limited by confidentiality concerns. The confidential token standard can be deployed to provide programmable privacy, compliance, and transfer rules to RWA tokens, enabling assets to be brought onchain safely.</p></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></section><section id="members" class="section_logo1"><div class="padding-global"><div class="container-medium"><div class="padding-section-medium"><div class="logo1_component"><div class="logo1_content"><div class="margin-bottom margin-medium"><div class="text-align-center"><div class="max-width-large class="padding-section-large"><div class="faq4_component"><div class="margin-bottom margin-large"><div class="text-align-center"><div class="max-width-large align-center"><div class="margin-bottom margin-small"><h2 class="heading-style-h2">FAQs</h2></div></div></div></div><div class="max-width-large align-center"><div class="w-layout-grid faq4_list"><div class="faq4_accordion"><div data-w-id="df629537-c65d-ca45-148d-10deb45083d4" class="faq4_question"><div class="text-size-medium text-weight-bold">Does it cost anything to join?</div><div class="faq4_icon-wrappper"><div class="icon-embed-small w-embed"><svg width="100%" height="100%" viewBox="0 0 32 32" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<path d="M25.3333 15.667V16.3336C25.3333 16.7018 25.0349 17.0003 24.6667 17.0003H17V24.667C17 25.0351 16.7015 25.3336 16.3333 25.3336H15.6667C15.2985 25.3336 15 25.0351 15 24.667V17.0003H7.3333C6.96511 17.0003 6.66663 16.7018 6.66663 16.3336V15.667C6.66663 15.2988 6.96511 15.0003 7.3333 15.0003H15V7.33365C15 6.96546 15.2985 6.66699 15.6667 6.66699H16.3333C16.7015 6.66699 17 6.96546 17 7.33365V15.0003H24.6667C25.0349 15.0003 25.3333 15.2988 25.3333 15.667Z" fill="currentColor"/>
</svg></div></div></div><div style="width:100%;height:0px" class="faq4_answer"><div class="margin-bottom margin-small"><p>No—joining the association is free. Simply get in touch.</p></div></div></div><div class="faq4_accordion"><div data-w-id="df629537-c65d-ca45-148d-10deb45083de" class="faq4_question"><div class="text-size-medium text-weight-bold">What is the time commitment?</div><div class="faq4_icon-wrappper"><div class="icon-embed-small w-embed"><svg width="100%" height="100%" viewBox="0 0 32 32" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<path d="M25.3333 15.667V16.3336C25.3333 16.7018 25.0349 17.0003 24.6667 17.0003H17V24.667C17 25.0351 16.7015 25.3336 16.3333 25.3336H15.6667C15.2985 25.3336 15 25.0351 15 24.667V17.0003H7.3333C6.96511 17.0003 6.66663 16.7018 6.66663 16.3336V15.667C6.66663 15.2988 6.96511 15.0003 7.3333 15.0003H15V7.33365C15 6.96546 15.2985 6.66699 15.6667 6.66699H16.3333C16.7015 6.66699 17 6.96546 17 7.33365V15.0003H24.6667C25.0349 15.0003 25.3333 15.2988 25.3333 15.667Z" fill="currentColor"/>
</svg></div></div></div><div style="width:100%;height:0px" class="faq4_answer"><div class="margin-bottom margin-small"><p>You must be able to set aside time to join a monthly virtual meeting to talk through association updates.</p></div></div></div><div class="faq4_accordion"><div data-w-id="df629537-c65d-ca45-148d-10deb45083e8" class="faq4_question"><div class="text-size-medium text-weight-bold">What’s the process for joining?</div><div class="faq4_icon-wrappper"><div class="icon-embed-small w-embed"><svg width="100%" height="100%" viewBox="0 0 32 32" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<path d="M25.3333 15.667V16.3336C25.3333 16.7018 25.0349 17.0003 24.6667 17.0003H17V24.667C17 25.0351 16.7015 25.3336 16.3333 25.3336H15.6667C15.2985 25.3336 15 25.0351 15 24.667V17.0003H7.3333C6.96511 17.0003 6.66663 16.7018 6.66663 16.3336V15.667C6.66663 15.2988 6.96511 15.0003 7.3333 15.0003H15V7.33365C15 6.96546 15.2985 6.66699 15.6667 6.66699H16.3333C16.7015 6.66699 17 6.96546 17 7.33365V15.0003H24.6667C25.0349 15.0003 25.3333 15.2988 25.3333 15.667Z" fill="currentColor"/>
</svg></div></div></div><div style="width:100%;height:0px" class="faq4_answer"><div class="margin-bottom margin-small"><p>Get in touch.</p></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></section><footer class="footer6_component"><div class="padding-global"><div class="container-medium"><div class="padding-vertical padding-medium"><div class="padding-top"><div class="footer6_bottom-wrapper"><div id="w-node-_97858210-b4fe-27b1-403f-11cccd28b5dd-cd96ee1d" class="footer6_credit-text text-color-secondary">Confidential Token Association</div><div blocks-name="footer13_social-list" blocks-slot-children="ST232" class="w-layout-grid footer13_social-list"><a blocks-name="footer13_social-link-3" href="https://x.com/ConfTokenAssoc" target="_blank" class="footer13_social-link w-inline-block"><div class="icon-embed-xsmall w-embed"><svg width="100%" height="100%" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">

<style id="wp-block-spacer-inline-css">
.wp-block-spacer{clear:both}
/*# sourceURL=https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/blocks/spacer/style.min.css */
</style>
<style id="wp-block-post-date-inline-css">
.wp-block-post-date{box-sizing:border-box}
/*# sourceURL=https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/blocks/post-date/style.min.css */
</style>
<style id="wp-block-post-terms-inline-css">
.wp-block-post-terms{box-sizing:border-box}.wp-block-post-terms .wp-block-post-terms__separator{white-space:pre-wrap}
/*# sourceURL=https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/blocks/post-terms/style.min.css */
</style>
<style id="wp-block-post-author-inline-css">
.wp-block-post-author{box-sizing:border-box;display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap}.wp-block-post-author__byline{font-size:.5em;margin-bottom:0;margin-top:0;width:100%}.wp-block-post-author__avatar{margin-right:1em}.wp-block-post-author__bio{font-size:.7em;margin-bottom:.7em}.wp-block-post-author__content{flex-basis:0;flex-grow:1}.wp-block-post-author__name{margin:0}
/*# sourceURL=https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/blocks/post-author/style.min.css */
</style>
<style id="wp-block-columns-inline-css">
.wp-block-columns{box-sizing:border-box;display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap!important}@media (min-width:782px){.wp-block-columns{flex-wrap:nowrap!important}}.wp-block-columns{align-items:normal!important}.wp-block-columns.are-vertically-aligned-top{align-items:flex-start}.wp-block-columns.are-vertically-aligned-center{align-items:center}.wp-block-columns.are-vertically-aligned-bottom{align-items:flex-end}@media (max-width:781px){.wp-block-columns:not(.is-not-stacked-on-mobile)>.wp-block-column{flex-basis:100%!important}}@media (min-width:782px){.wp-block-columns:not(.is-not-stacked-on-mobile)>.wp-block-column{flex-basis:0;flex-grow:1}.wp-block-columns:not(.is-not-stacked-on-mobile)>.wp-block-column[style*=flex-basis]{flex-grow:0}}.wp-block-columns.is-not-stacked-on-mobile{flex-wrap:nowrap!important}.wp-block-columns.is-not-stacked-on-mobile>.wp-block-column{flex-basis:0;flex-grow:1}.wp-block-columns.is-not-stacked-on-mobile>.wp-block-column[style*=flex-basis]{flex-grow:0}:where(.wp-block-columns){margin-bottom:1.75em}:where(.wp-block-columns.has-background){padding:1.25em 2.375em}.wp-block-column{flex-grow:1;min-width:0;overflow-wrap:break-word;word-break:break-word}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-top{align-self:flex-start}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-center{align-self:center}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-bottom{align-self:flex-end}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-stretch{align-self:stretch}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-bottom,.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-center,.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-top{width:100%}
/*# sourceURL=https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/blocks/columns/style.min.css */
</style>
<style id="wp-emoji-styles-inline-css">

	img.wp-smiley, img.emoji {
		display: inline !important;
		border: none !important;
		box-shadow: none !important;
		height: 1em !important;
		width: 1em !important;
		margin: 0 0.07em !important;
		vertical-align: -0.1em !important;
		background: none !important;
		padding: 0 !important;
	}
/*# sourceURL=wp-emoji-styles-inline-css */
</style>

:where([style*=border-top-color]){border-top-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-right-color]){border-right-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-bottom-color]){border-bottom-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-left-color]){border-left-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-width]){border-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-top-width]){border-top-style:solid}html padding-bottom: var(--wp--style--root--padding-bottom); }.has-global-padding { padding-right: var(--wp--style--root--padding-right); padding-left: var(--wp--style--root--padding-left); }.has-global-padding > .alignfull { margin-right: calc(var(--wp--style--root--padding-right) * -1); margin-left: calc(var(--wp--style--root--padding-left) * -1); }.has-global-padding :where(:not(.alignfull.is-layout-flow) > .has-global-padding:not(.wp-block-block, .alignfull)) { padding-right: 0; padding-left: 0; }.has-global-padding :where(:not(.alignfull.is-layout-flow) > .has-global-padding:not(.wp-block-block, .alignfull)) > .alignfull { margin-left: 0; margin-right: 0; }.wp-site-blocks > .alignleft { float: left; margin-right: 2em; }.wp-site-blocks > .alignright { float: right; margin-left: 2em; }.wp-site-blocks > .aligncenter { justify-content: center; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; }:where(.wp-site-blocks) > * { margin-block-start: 1.5rem; margin-block-end: 0; }:where(.wp-site-blocks) > :first-child { margin-block-start: 0; }:where(.wp-site-blocks) > :last-child { margin-block-end: 0; }:root { --wp--style--block-gap: 1.5rem; }:root :where(.is-layout-flow) > :first-child{margin-block-start: 0;}:root :where(.is-layout-flow) > :last-child{margin-block-end: 0;}:root :where(.is-layout-flow) > *{margin-block-start: :where(.is-layout-constrained) > *{margin-block-start: 1.5rem;margin-block-end: 0;}:root :where(.is-layout-flex){gap: 1.5rem;}:root :where(.is-layout-grid){gap: 1.5rem;}.is-layout-flow > <main id="wp--skip-link--target" class="wp-block-group is-layout-flow wp-block-group-is-layout-flow" style="margin-top:var(--wp--preset--spacing--50)">
<div class="wp-block-group has-global-padding is-layout-constrained wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained"><h1 style="margin-bottom:var(--wp--preset--spacing--40)" class="wp-block-post-title has-large-font-size">How To Verify Noir Ultrahonk Circuits In A Stellar Contract</h1>

<figure class="wp-block-post-featured-image"><img width="1645" height="700" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/noirStellar.png" class="has-border-color wp-post-image lazyload" alt="Noir Lang on Stellar" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 1645px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 1645/700;border-color:#212121;border-radius:33px;border-width:6px;object-fit:cover;" decoding="async" data-srcset="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/noirStellar.png 1645w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/noirStellar-300x128.png 300w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/noirStellar-1024x436.png 1024w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/noirStellar-768x327.png 768w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/noirStellar-1536x654.png 1536w" data-sizes="(max-width: 1645px) 100vw, 1645px" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" /></figure></div>


<div class="entry-content wp-block-post-content has-global-padding is-layout-constrained wp-block-post-content-is-layout-constrained">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph">This guide walks through integrating Noir zero knowledge circuits with smart contracts on Stellar. You will:</p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>Build a Noir circuit</li>



<li>Generate UltraHonk proofs in Node on a backend</li>



<li>Generate proofs directly in the browser with React</li>



<li>Verify proofs inside a Stellar smart contract</li>
</ul>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">All code is open-source: <a href="https://github.com/jamesbachini/Noirlang-Experiments/">https://github.com/jamesbachini/Noirlang-Experiments/</a></p>



<div class="video-wrapper" onclick="if (!window.__cfRLUnblockHandlers) return false; window.open('https://www.youtube.com/c/JamesBachini?sub_confirmation=1');" data-cf-modified-a794ff430a4e6afb4eb0f1f3-="">
	<div class="video-header">
		<div class="video-channel">James On YouTube</div>
		<img decoding="async" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/04/youtubeSubscribeButton.png" class="video-button lazyload" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 500px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 500/111;" />
	</div>
	<!-- Copy & Pasted from YouTube -->
	<iframe data-src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/AHBSZzemy9E?rel=0&amp;hd=1" allowfullscreen="" width="560" height="349" frameborder="0" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" class="lazyload" data-load-mode="1"></iframe>
</div>
<div class="text-center">Watch On YouTube: <a href="https://youtu.be/AHBSZzemy9E" target="_blank" rel="noopener">https://youtu.be/AHBSZzemy9E</a> | <a href="https://www.youtube.com/c/JamesBachini?sub_confirmation=1" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Subscribe</a> </div>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The integration follows a clean separation of responsibilities.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Noir defines the constraint system &#8211; the private logic you want to prove. Barretenberg (<em>bb.js</em>) generates a verification key and proofs from that circuit.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">A Stellar smart contract verifies those proofs on chain. Optionally, a React frontend can generate proofs client side using WebAssembly and submit them to the contract.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">In practical terms, the flow looks like this:<br>Noir circuit &gt; BB.js &gt; Stellar verifier contract</p>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Setup</h2>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">You&#8217;ll need <a href="https://rust-lang.org/">Rust</a>, <a href="https://www.docker.com/">Docker</a>, <a href="https://crates.io/crates/stellar-cli">Stellar-cli</a> all installed, ideally on a unix based console.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">On-chain verification of Ultrahonk proofs is computationally expensive and even simple circuits are right on the borderline of CPU instruction limits for on-chain transactions currently.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">This is being worked on and an update is expected in Protocol 26. Until then I would recommend deploying to a local network environment using stellar/quickstart with the flag <em>&#8211;limits unlimited</em>. So let&#8217;s go ahead and set that up.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-code console"><code>sudo dockerd &amp;
git clone https://github.com/jamesbachini/Noirlang-Experiments
cd Noirlang-Experiments
docker run -d -p 8000:8000 stellar/quickstart \
  --local \
  --limits unlimited \
  --enable core,rpc,lab,horizon,friendbot
stellar network add local \
  --rpc-url http://localhost:8000/soroban/rpc \
  --network-passphrase "Standalone Network ; February 2017"
stellar network use local
stellar keys generate --global alice
stellar keys fund alice --network local</code></pre>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">This command sequence starts the Docker daemon, clones the Noir Stellar integration repository, launches a local <a href="https://github.com/stellar/quickstart">stellar/quickstart</a> node with unlimited execution limits (required for CPU intensive UltraHonk verification), configures the Stellar CLI to use the local Soroban RPC endpoint, generates a test keypair, and funds it via the built in friendbot.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The result is a fully configured local development environment ready for deploying and testing Noir generated zero knowledge proofs against a Stellar verifier contract without hitting network budget constraints.</p>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Noir Circuit</h2>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The example circuit lives in <a href="https://github.com/jamesbachini/Noirlang-Experiments/blob/main/private_limit_orders/src/main.nr">private_limit_orders/src/main.nr</a>. It models a simple but useful pattern: a private limit order.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-code"><code>fn main(my_limit_price: u64, market_price: pub u64) {
    assert(my_limit_price &gt; market_price);
}</code></pre>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The circuit takes a private input (my_limit_price) and a public input (market_price) and enforces the constraint that the private value is greater than the public one. In other words, the prover demonstrates that their hidden limit price satisfies the trade condition without revealing it.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">This minimal constraint illustrates how Noir can encode financial logic while preserving confidentiality. The same pattern can be extended to hidden game moves, sealed bids, eligibility checks, or confidential scoring systems.</p>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Backend Proof Generation</h2>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The repository includes a script that orchestrates the entire proving and verification lifecycle:</p>



<pre class="wp-block-code"><code>./test-local.sh</code></pre>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The script compiles and executes the Noir circuit using nargo, producing the witness data required for proof generation.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Then it invokes @aztec/bb.js to generate an UltraHonk verification key and a proof bundle containing both proof data and public inputs. The script splits this bundle into raw byte files suitable for contract submission.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">It builds the Stellar UltraHonk verifier contract and deploys it to the local network. During deployment, the verification key bytes are passed to initialise the contract. Finally, the script calls the verify_proof function, first in simulation mode and then on chain, confirming that the proof verifies successfully.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">If all steps complete without error, you will see the deployed contract ID and confirmation that verification succeeded on chain.</p>



<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><a href="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-7.png"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="472" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-7-1024x472.png" alt="verify ultrahonk on stellar" class="wp-image-8174 lazyload" data-srcset="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-7-1024x472.png 1024w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-7-300x138.png 300w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-7-768x354.png 768w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-7-1536x708.png 1536w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-7-2048x944.png 2048w" data-sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 1024px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 1024/472;" /></a></figure>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">If you encounter funding errors during deployment or invocation, open <em>http://localhost:8000/lab</em>, paste your account address into the account funding interface, and retry the script.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The script passes raw binary proof files directly to the Stellar CLI using file path flags. If you prefer to test manually in a contract explorer such as <a href="https://soropg.com">SoroPG</a> on a local network, you’ll need to convert those byte files to hex.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">From the <em>private_limit_orders/target</em> directory:</p>



<pre class="wp-block-code console"><code>xxd -p -c 999999 public_inputs | tr -d '\n' &gt; public_inputs.hex
xxd -p -c 999999 proof | tr -d '\n' &gt; proof.hex</code></pre>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">These hex strings can be pasted into the verify_proof contract call on the <a href="https://soropg.com/">SoroPG contract explorer</a>. A valid proof returns OK; modifying inputs to violate the constraint will result in a contract error.</p>



<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><a href="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-8-scaled.png"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="482" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-8-1024x482.png" alt="SoroPG Contract Explorer" class="wp-image-8175 lazyload" data-srcset="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-8-1024x482.png 1024w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-8-300x141.png 300w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-8-768x361.png 768w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-8-1536x723.png 1536w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-8-2048x964.png 2048w" data-sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 1024px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 1024/482;" /></a></figure>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Frontend Proof Generator</h2>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The repository also includes a React based prover UI in <a href="https://github.com/jamesbachini/Noirlang-Experiments/tree/main/private_limit_orders/prover-ui">private_limit_orders/prover ui</a>. This demonstrates client side proof generation using WebAssembly.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Start the UI with:</p>



<pre class="wp-block-code console"><code>cd private_limit_orders/prover-ui
npm install
npm run dev</code></pre>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Open the development server in your browser. The interface allows you to enter a limit price and market price, generate a proof entirely in the browser, and copy hex encoded proof data suitable for submission to the Stellar verifier.</p>



<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><a href="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/hero_proverui-scaled.png"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="512" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/hero_proverui-1024x512.png" alt="" class="wp-image-8176 lazyload" data-srcset="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/hero_proverui-1024x512.png 1024w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/hero_proverui-300x150.png 300w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/hero_proverui-768x384.png 768w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/hero_proverui-1536x768.png 1536w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/hero_proverui-2048x1025.png 2048w" data-sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 1024px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 1024/512;" /></a></figure>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">This UI uses the same bb.js library that we were using in the node.js backend.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">This workflow demonstrates a complete zero knowledge pipeline on Stellar: private logic expressed in Noir, UltraHonk proof generation via bb.js, and on chain verification inside a Stellar contract.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">With this foundation, developers can build privacy preserving financial tools, competitive games with hidden state, sealed bid auctions, and many other applications that require verifiable computation without revealing sensitive data.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The combination of Noir&#8217;s expressive constraint language and Stellar&#8217;s programmable smart contracts opens the door to practical zero-knowledge applications on Stellar.</p>
</div>

<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><em>The Blockchain Sector</em> newsletter goes out a few times a month when there is breaking news or interesting developments to discuss. All the content I produce is free, if you&#8217;d like to help please share this content on social media.</p>



<p class="has-text-align-center wp-block-paragraph">Thank you.</p>

<p class="has-text-color has-small-font-size wp-block-paragraph" style="color:#828689"><strong>Disclaimer</strong>: Not a financial advisor, not financial advice. The content I create is to document my journey and for educational and entertainment purposes only. It is not under any circumstances investment advice. I am not an investment or trading professional and am learning myself while still making plenty of mistakes along the way. Any code published is experimental and not production ready to be used for financial transactions. Do your own research and do not play with funds you do not want to lose. </p>

<!-- Substack Overlay -->
<script type="a794ff430a4e6afb4eb0f1f3-text/javascript">
const showOverlay = function() {
  document.body.innerHTML += `<div id="overlay-substack" style='display: block; z-index: 9999999; position: fixed; left: 0; right: 0; top: 0; bottom: 0; background: #111111; text-align: center;'><div style='width: 100%; text-align: right;'><div id='overlay-close' style='font-size: 26px; color: #555; cursor: pointer; padding: 20px;'>X</div></div><h1>Get The Latest Insider Blockchain News</h1><iframe data-src='https://bachini.substack.com/embed' width='300' height='320' style='border:1px solid #EEE; background:white; margin: 10px auto;' frameborder='0' scrolling='no' src='data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==' class='lazyload' data-load-mode='1'></iframe><div style='width: 320px; text-align: left; margin: 10px auto; line-height: 200%;'>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f4f0')} Be First To Hear Breaking News<br>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f50d')} Explainers For DeFi Protocols<br>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f4c8')} Independent Market Commentary<br>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f393')} Web3 & Solidity Dev Tutorials<br>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f4b0')} Free Newsletter, For Everyone<br><div style="font-size: 0.9em; width: 100%; text-align: center;"><a href="https://bachini.substack.com" target="_blank">https://bachini.substack.com</a></div></div></div>`;
  document.getElementById('overlay-close').onclick = function() {
    document.getElementById('overlay-substack').style.display = 'none';
  };
}
const myScrollFunc = function() {
  const y = window.scrollY;
  const lastOverlay = localStorage.lastOverlay || 0;
  const ts = new Date().getTime(); 
  if (y >= 3000 && lastOverlay < ts -  604800000) {
    localStorage.setItem('lastOverlay', ts);
    if (localStorage.getItem('lastOverlay') == ts) {
      showOverlay();
    }
  }
};
setTimeout(function() {
  window.addEventListener('scroll', myScrollFunc);
}, 30000);
</script>
<!-- End Substack Overlay -->


<!-- Google tag (gtag.js) -->
<script async src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=UA-48537439-11" type="a794ff430a4e6afb4eb0f1f3-text/javascript"></script>
<script type="a794ff430a4e6afb4eb0f1f3-text/javascript">
  window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];
  function gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments);}
  gtag('js', new Date());
  gtag('config', 'UA-48537439-11');
</script>

<script type="a794ff430a4e6afb4eb0f1f3-text/javascript">
window.lastScrollTime = false;
window.addEventListener('scroll', function() {
	window.lastScrollTime = new Date().getTime();
});
setTimeout(async function() {
	if (gtag && window.lastScrollTime) {
		gtag('event','timedScroll', {
			'event_category': 'blog'
		});
	}
}, 35000);
</script>

<style>
.single-post img {
  max-width: 100%;
}
.single-post a {
  word-break: break-all;
}
a, a:visited, a:active {
    color: #EEE !important;
}
a:hover {
    color: #FFF !important;
}
.wp-block-navigation__submenu-container {
    background: #111111EE !important;
}
.wp-block-navigation__responsive-container-content {
    background: #111111EE !important;
}
.table td, .table tr, .table th {
  padding: 5px;
  border: 1px solid #444;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #444 !important;
}
.table-center td, .table-center th {
  text-align: center !important;
}
.table  th {
  font-weight: 400;
  background: #23282d;
  color: #FFF00F;
}
.table tr {
  background: #111;
}
.table tr:nth-child(even) {
  background: #000;
}
.video-wrapper {
  position: relative;
  padding-top: 25px;
  width: 100%;
  padding-bottom: 380px;
}
.video-wrapper iframe {
  position: absolute;
  top: 80px;
  left: 0;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: 400px;
}
.video-header {
  background: #000;
  color: #EEE;
  width: 100%;
  height: 60px;
}
.video-channel {
  font-size: 1.3em;
  font-weight: bold;
  float: left;
  margin-left: 10px;
  margin-top: 15px;
}
.video-button {
  float: right;
  height: 40px;
  margin-right: 10px;
  margin-top: 10px;
  max-width: 33%;
  cursor: pointer
}
.entry-inner {
  word-wrap: break-word;
}
.responsive-image {
  max-width: 100%;
  max-height: 100%;
}  
.text-small {
  font-size: 0.8em;
}
.screenshot {
  border: 3px solid #444;
  border-radius: 5px;
  box-shadow: 0 0 5px #222;
  -webkit-box-shadow: 0 0 5px #222;
  -moz-box-shadow: 0 0 5px #222;
  box-sizing: border-box;
  -moz-box-sizing: border-box;
  -webkit-box-sizing: border-box;
}
.standout {
  background: #000;
  border-left: 5px solid #FFF00F;
  padding: 10px 10px 10px 20px;
  font-size: 1.1em;
}
.bullet {
  float: left;
  font-weight: bold;
  border-radius: 99px;
  background: #FFF00F;
  width: 27px;
  height: 27px;
  text-align: center;
  margin-right: 10px;
  color: #FFF;
}
code {
    background: #070707;
    color: #CCC;
    border: 3px solid #444;
    border-radius: 5px;
    padding: 10px;
    font-size: 0.8em;
}
.console {
  background: #000 !important;
  color: #6F0;
  border-radius: 3px;
  padding: 2px 4px;
}
.console-error {
  color: #F44;
}
.console code {
  background: #000 !important;
  color: #6F0;
}
.wp-block-post-title {
  font-weight: bold;
}
.wp-block-post-title, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 {
  background-image: linear-gradient(to right, #FFF00F, #f85050);
  color: transparent;
  background-clip: text;
  background: -webkit-linear-gradient(left, #FFF00F, #f85050);
  -webkit-background-clip: text;
  -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent;
}
@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
  .mobile-hidden {
    display: none;
  }
}
</style><script data-wp-router-options="{&quot;loadOnClientNavigation&quot;:true}" fetchpriority="low" id="@wordpress/block-library/navigation/view-js-module" src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/js/dist/script-modules/block-library/navigation/view.min.js?ver=96a846e1d7b789c39ab9" type="a794ff430a4e6afb4eb0f1f3-module"></script>

tocMobile_ITEo"><button type="button" class="clean-btn tocCollapsibleButton_TO0P">On this page</button></div><div class="theme-doc-markdown markdown"><header><h1>Noir</h1></header><p>Noir is an open-source, domain-specific programming language for writing privacy-preserving programs leveraging zero-knowledge proofs, requiring no previous knowledge on the underlying mathematics or cryptography.</p>
<p>A zero-knowledge proof is a cryptographic protocol that enables a party (the prover) to share outputs of certain computations with another party (the verifier), without revealing the inputs used in such computations, hence its zero-knowledge and privacy-preserving property.</p>
<img src="/docs/assets/images/banner-ef06d89b5fc2cda9c24be3ad44df5ce4.png" style="display:block;margin:2rem auto" alt="Noir banner">
<h3 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="quickstart">Quickstart<a href="#quickstart" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Quickstart" title="Direct link to Quickstart" translate="no">​</a></h3>
<h4 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="learning">Learning<a href="#learning" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Learning" title="Direct link to Learning" translate="no">​</a></h4>
<p>Paste this into your AI agent for asking questions:</p>
<div class="language-text codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color:#393A34;--prism-background-color:#f6f8fa"><div class="codeBlockContent_QJqH"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-text codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color:#393A34;background-color:#f6f8fa"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><div class="token-line" style="color:#393A34"><span class="token plain">Read https://noir-lang.org/docs/llms.txt and tell me about Noir</span><br></div></code></pre></div></div>
<h4 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="building">Building<a href="#building" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Building" title="Direct link to Building" translate="no">​</a></h4>
<p>Paste this into your AI agent to create a new project:</p>
<div class="language-text codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color:#393A34;--prism-background-color:#f6f8fa"><div class="codeBlockContent_QJqH"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-text codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color:#393A34;background-color:#f6f8fa"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><div class="token-line" style="color:#393A34"><span class="token plain">Read https://noir-lang.org/docs/getting_started_manually and create a Noir project</span><br></div></code></pre></div></div></div><footer class="theme-doc-footer docusaurus-mt-lg"><div class="row margin-top--sm theme-doc-footer-edit-meta-row"><div class="col noPrint_WFHX"><a itemprop="position" content="1"></li></ul></nav><span class="theme-doc-version-badge badge badge--secondary">Version: 3.0</span><div class="theme-doc-markdown markdown"><header><h1>Introduction</h1></header>
<p>Welcome to the <a href="https://risczero.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">RISC Zero</a> documentation!</p>
<p>RISC Zero is creating the infrastructure &amp; tooling necessary for
developers around the globe to build software that leverages <strong>verifiable computation</strong>. With verifiable computation, you can be cryptographically confident that a specific blob of data was produced by a specific algorithm.</p>
<p>This technology is poised to enable the finance and healthcare sectors to prove compliance while maintaining privacy, and enables the tech sector to address decades-long trust gaps in software supply chains.</p>
<p>Historically,
the only method for confirming the correct execution of a software application
was through redundant computation. ZK introduces a new option: <strong>verifiable
computation</strong>.</p>
<p>It&#x27;s now possible to pair a program&#x27;s output with a self-certifying <em>receipt</em>,
allowing a skeptical third party to verify correct execution — and the verifier
doesn&#x27;t need to repeat the original computation or even see the inputs to the
program!</p>
<p><strong>Ready to start building?</strong> <br>
Check out our <a href="/api/getting-started">Getting Started</a> page.</p></div><footer class="theme-doc-footer docusaurus-mt-lg"><div class="row margin-top--sm theme-doc-footer-edit-meta-row"><div .wp-block-quote{box-sizing:border-box;overflow-wrap:break-word}.wp-block-quote.is-large:where(:not(.is-style-plain)),.wp-block-quote.is-style-large:where(:not(.is-style-plain)){margin-bottom:1em;padding:0 1em}.wp-block-quote.is-large:where(:not(.is-style-plain)) p,.wp-block-quote.is-style-large:where(:not(.is-style-plain)) p{font-size:1.5em;font-style:italic;line-height:1.6}.wp-block-quote.is-large:where(:not(.is-style-plain)) cite,.wp-block-quote.is-large:where(:not(.is-style-plain)) footer,.wp-block-quote.is-style-large:where(:not(.is-style-plain)) cite,.wp-block-quote.is-style-large:where(:not(.is-style-plain)) footer{font-size:1.125em;text-align:right}.wp-block-quote>cite{display:block}
