<style id="wp-block-post-author-inline-css">
.wp-block-post-author{box-sizing:border-box;display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap}.wp-block-post-author__byline{font-size:.5em;margin-bottom:0;margin-top:0;width:100%}.wp-block-post-author__avatar{margin-right:1em}.wp-block-post-author__bio{font-size:.7em;margin-bottom:.7em}.wp-block-post-author__content{flex-basis:0;flex-grow:1}.wp-block-post-author__name{margin:0}
/*# sourceURL=https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/blocks/post-author/style.min.css */
</style>
<style id="wp-block-columns-inline-css">
.wp-block-columns{box-sizing:border-box;display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap!important}@media (min-width:782px){.wp-block-columns{flex-wrap:nowrap!important}}.wp-block-columns{align-items:normal!important}.wp-block-columns.are-vertically-aligned-top{align-items:flex-start}.wp-block-columns.are-vertically-aligned-center{align-items:center}.wp-block-columns.are-vertically-aligned-bottom{align-items:flex-end}@media (max-width:781px){.wp-block-columns:not(.is-not-stacked-on-mobile)>.wp-block-column{flex-basis:100%!important}}@media (min-width:782px){.wp-block-columns:not(.is-not-stacked-on-mobile)>.wp-block-column{flex-basis:0;flex-grow:1}.wp-block-columns:not(.is-not-stacked-on-mobile)>.wp-block-column[style*=flex-basis]{flex-grow:0}}.wp-block-columns.is-not-stacked-on-mobile{flex-wrap:nowrap!important}.wp-block-columns.is-not-stacked-on-mobile>.wp-block-column{flex-basis:0;flex-grow:1}.wp-block-columns.is-not-stacked-on-mobile>.wp-block-column[style*=flex-basis]{flex-grow:0}:where(.wp-block-columns){margin-bottom:1.75em}:where(.wp-block-columns.has-background){padding:1.25em 2.375em}.wp-block-column{flex-grow:1;min-width:0;overflow-wrap:break-word;word-break:break-word}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-top{align-self:flex-start}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-center{align-self:center}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-bottom{align-self:flex-end}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-stretch{align-self:stretch}.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-bottom,.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-center,.wp-block-column.is-vertically-aligned-top{width:100%}
/*# sourceURL=https://jamesbachini.com/wp-includes/blocks/columns/style.min.css */
</style>
<style id="wp-emoji-styles-inline-css">

	img.wp-smiley, img.emoji {
		display: inline !important;
		border: none !important;
		box-shadow: none !important;
		height: 1em !important;
		width: 1em !important;
		margin: 0 0.07em !important;
		vertical-align: -0.1em !important;
		background: none !important;
		padding: 0 !important;
	}
/*# sourceURL=wp-emoji-styles-inline-css */
</style>
<style id="wp-block-library-inline-css">
:root{--wp-block-synced-color:#7a00df;--wp-block-synced-color--rgb:122,0,223;--wp-bound-block-color:var(--wp-block-synced-color);--wp-editor-canvas-background:#ddd;--wp-admin-theme-color:#007cba;--wp-admin-theme-color--rgb:0,124,186;--wp-admin-theme-color-darker-10:#006ba1;--wp-admin-theme-color-darker-10--rgb:0,107,160.5;--wp-admin-theme-color-darker-20:#005a87;--wp-admin-theme-color-darker-20--rgb:0,90,135;--wp-admin-border-width-focus:2px}@media (min-resolution:192dpi){:root{--wp-admin-border-width-focus:1.5px}}.wp-element-button{cursor:pointer}:root .has-very-light-gray-background-color{background-color:#eee}:root .has-very-dark-gray-background-color{background-color:#313131}:root .has-very-light-gray-color{color:#eee}:root .has-very-dark-gray-color{color:#313131}:root .has-vivid-green-cyan-to-vivid-cyan-blue-gradient-background{background:linear-gradient(135deg,#00d084,#0693e3)}:root .has-purple-crush-gradient-background{background:linear-gradient(135deg,#34e2e4,#4721fb 50%,#ab1dfe)}:root .has-hazy-dawn-gradient-background{background:linear-gradient(135deg,#faaca8,#dad0ec)}:root .has-subdued-olive-gradient-background{background:linear-gradient(135deg,#fafae1,#67a671)}:root .has-atomic-cream-gradient-background{background:linear-gradient(135deg,#fdd79a,#004a59)}:root .has-nightshade-gradient-background{background:linear-gradient(135deg,#330968,#31cdcf)}:root .has-midnight-gradient-background{background:linear-gradient(135deg,#020381,#2874fc)}:root{--wp--preset--font-size--normal:16px;--wp--preset--font-size--huge:42px}.has-regular-font-size{font-size:1em}.has-larger-font-size{font-size:2.625em}.has-normal-font-size{font-size:var(--wp--preset--font-size--normal)}.has-huge-font-size{font-size:var(--wp--preset--font-size--huge)}:root .has-text-align-center{text-align:center}:root .has-text-align-left{text-align:left}:root .has-text-align-right{text-align:right}.has-fit-text{white-space:nowrap!important}#end-resizable-editor-section{display:none}.aligncenter{clear:both}.items-justified-left{justify-content:flex-start}.items-justified-center{justify-content:center}.items-justified-right{justify-content:flex-end}.items-justified-space-between{justify-content:space-between}.screen-reader-text{word-wrap:normal!important;border:0;clip-path:inset(50%);height:1px;margin:-1px;overflow:hidden;padding:0;position:absolute;width:1px}.screen-reader-text:focus{background-color:#ddd;clip-path:none;color:#444;display:block;font-size:1em;height:auto;left:5px;line-height:normal;padding:15px 23px 14px;text-decoration:none;top:5px;width:auto;z-index:100000}html :where(.has-border-color){border-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-color]){border-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-top-color]){border-top-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-right-color]){border-right-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-bottom-color]){border-bottom-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-left-color]){border-left-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-width]){border-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-top-width]){border-top-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-right-width]){border-right-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-bottom-width]){border-bottom-style:solid}html :where([style*=border-left-width]){border-left-style:solid}html :where(img[class*=wp-image-]){height:auto;max-width:100%}:where(figure){margin:0 0 1em}html :where(.is-position-sticky){--wp-admin--admin-bar--position-offset:var(--wp-admin--admin-bar--height,0px)}@media screen and (max-width:600px){html :where(.is-position-sticky){--wp-admin--admin-bar--position-offset:0px}}
/*# sourceURL=/wp-includes/css/dist/block-library/common.min.css */
</style>
<style id="ai_summarization-inline-css">
.ai-summarization-summary{--ai-summary-border-color:#1d2327;--ai-summary-background-color:rgba(29,35,39,.04);background-color:var(--ai-summary-background-color);border-left:4px solid var(--ai-summary-border-color);padding:1em 1.25em}.ai-summarization-summary>:last-child{margin-bottom:0}.ai-summarization-plugin-container .components-button.ai-summarization-plugin-button{justify-content:center;width:100%}.ai-summarization-plugin-container .description{color:#757575}.block-editor-block-popover .ai-summarization-block-controls-button{height:46px;margin-top:1px}

/*# sourceURL=https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/plugins/ai/build-scripts/experiments/summarization.css */
</style>
<style id="global-styles-inline-css">

				<style>
			.no-js img.lazyload {
				display: none;
			}

			figure.wp-block-image img.lazyloading {
				min-width: 150px;
			}

			.lazyload,
			.lazyloading {
				--smush-placeholder-width: 100px;
				--smush-placeholder-bg-max-width: 120px;
				--smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 1/1;

			}

						.lazyload, .lazyloading {

<main id="wp--skip-link--target" class="wp-block-group is-layout-flow wp-block-group-is-layout-flow" style="margin-top:var(--wp--preset--spacing--50)">
<div class="wp-block-group has-global-padding is-layout-constrained wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained"><h1 style="margin-bottom:var(--wp--preset--spacing--40)" class="wp-block-post-title has-large-font-size">Building Web3 Games With Stellar &amp; RISC Zero</h1>

<figure class="wp-block-post-featured-image"><img width="1645" height="700" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Stellar-x-RISC-Zero.png" class="has-border-color wp-post-image lazyload" alt="Stellar x RISC Zero" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 1645px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 1645/700;border-color:#212121;border-radius:33px;border-width:6px;object-fit:cover;" decoding="async" data-srcset="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Stellar-x-RISC-Zero.png 1645w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Stellar-x-RISC-Zero-300x128.png 300w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Stellar-x-RISC-Zero-1024x436.png 1024w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Stellar-x-RISC-Zero-768x327.png 768w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Stellar-x-RISC-Zero-1536x654.png 1536w" data-sizes="(max-width: 1645px) 100vw, 1645px" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" /></figure></div>


<div class="entry-content wp-block-post-content has-global-padding is-layout-constrained wp-block-post-content-is-layout-constrained">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The idea behind this tutorial was to create a on-chain game that demonstrated how to combine RISC Zero proofs with Stellar smart contracts.</p>



<div class="video-wrapper" onclick="if (!window.__cfRLUnblockHandlers) return false; window.open('https://www.youtube.com/c/JamesBachini?sub_confirmation=1');" data-cf-modified-2ad0f8b3f53b27a6cd908e12-="">
	<div class="video-header">
		<div class="video-channel">James On YouTube</div>
		<img decoding="async" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/04/youtubeSubscribeButton.png" class="video-button lazyload" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 500px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 500/111;" />
	</div>
	<!-- Copy & Pasted from YouTube -->
	<iframe data-src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/caPr0suJYAg?rel=0&amp;hd=1" allowfullscreen="" width="560" height="349" frameborder="0" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" class="lazyload" data-load-mode="1"></iframe>
</div>
<div class="text-center">Watch On YouTube: <a href="https://youtu.be/caPr0suJYAg" target="_blank" rel="noopener">https://youtu.be/caPr0suJYAg</a> | <a href="https://www.youtube.com/c/JamesBachini?sub_confirmation=1" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Subscribe</a> </div>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">All code is open source on github: <a href="https://github.com/jamesbachini/typezero">https://github.com/jamesbachini/typezero</a></p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The game is composed of four distinct components, all governed by deterministic rules to ensure that every participant computes the same result.</p>



<figure class="wp-block-table"><table class="has-fixed-layout"><tbody><tr><td>The frontend is responsible for capturing user keystrokes, encoding them into a compact replay format, computing preview statistics, and submitting proof results for verification. The user interface lives in <em>frontend/app.mjs</em>, while the deterministic replay encoding and scoring logic are implemented in <em>frontend/src/replay.mjs</em></td></tr><tr><td>The backend exposes a minimal HTTP API that acts as a proving service. Its role is to normalize prompts, validate replay encodings, and invoke the RISC Zero prover to generate cryptographic proofs. The main entry point for this service is <em>backend/server.js</em></td></tr><tr><td>The RISC Zero guest program is where verification and scoring are enforced. It deterministically replays the recorded events, applies timing constraints, computes the final statistics, and commits a fixed-size journal of public outputs. This logic is implemented in <em>risc0/typing_proof/methods/guest/src/main.rs</em></td></tr><tr><td>The Stellar Soroban smart contract verifies the generated proof using a Groth16 verifier contract and maintains the on-chain leaderboard. It treats all off-chain components as untrusted and only updates state after successful verification. The contract implementation lives in <em>contracts/leaderboard/src/lib.rs</em></td></tr></tbody></table></figure>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><a href="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-3.png"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="670" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-3-1024x670.png" alt="" class="wp-image-8160 lazyload" data-srcset="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-3-1024x670.png 1024w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-3-300x196.png 300w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-3-768x503.png 768w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-3.png 1288w" data-sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 1024px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 1024/670;" /></a></figure>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Application Flow</h2>



<ol class="wp-block-list">
<li>The frontend records keystrokes as <code>(dt_ms, key)</code> events and encodes them as bytes.</li>



<li>The backend validates the replay and runs the RISC Zero host to generate a proof.</li>



<li>The guest commits a small public journal containing:
<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>challenge ID</li>



<li>prompt hash</li>



<li>player public key</li>



<li>score, WPM, accuracy, duration</li>
</ul>
</li>



<li>The frontend submits the journal outputs and proof seal to the Soroban contract.</li>



<li>The contract verifies the proof and updates the leaderboard if the score is valid and superior.</li>
</ol>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Because the rules and arithmetic are deterministic, all parties compute identical results. The proof enforces that consistency.</p>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Generating RISC Zero Proofs</h3>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The guest program validates timing constraints, replays input events, computes stats and commits a fixed-sized journal.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><a href="https://github.com/jamesbachini/typezero/blob/main/risc0/typing_proof/methods/guest/src/main.rs" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">https://github.com/jamesbachini/typezero/blob/main/risc0/typing_proof/methods/guest/src/main.rs</a></p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Here is what the journal looks like:</p>



<pre class="wp-block-code"><code>let journal = encode_journal(
    challenge_id,
    &amp;player_pubkey,
    &amp;prompt_hash,
    score,
    wpm_x100,
    accuracy_bps,
    duration_ms as u32,
);</code></pre>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The host program executes the guest inside the zkVM and generates a proof that the guest code ran correctly and produced the committed journal outputs:</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><a href="https://github.com/jamesbachini/typezero/blob/main/risc0/typing_proof/host/src/lib.rs" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">https://github.com/jamesbachini/typezero/blob/main/risc0/typing_proof/host/src/lib.rs</a></p>



<blockquote class="wp-block-quote is-layout-flow wp-block-quote-is-layout-flow">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><strong>Note</strong>. that we need to output a groth16 receipt rather than a full receipt. Originally I was getting &#8220;Error 500: request body too large&#8221; from the RPC host. This was due to the seal size in the proof being too large for on-chain verification.</p>
</blockquote>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph"></p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">All anti-cheat guarantees live in this code and it is the trust layer for the entire application. If the replay violates constraints, no proof is produced. If this was going into production it would need further constraints and checks.</p>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Verifying RISC Zero On Stellar</h3>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The contract treats both frontend and backend as untrusted. It:</p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>Verifies the proof via a Groth16 verifier contract. This is based on the work by Nethermind which is available here: <a href="https://github.com/NethermindEth/stellar-risc0-verifier/tree/main/contracts/groth16-verifier">https://github.com/NethermindEth/stellar-risc0-verifier/tree/main/contracts/groth16-verifier</a></li>



<li>Checks challenge and prompt hashes</li>



<li>Updates leaderboard state only if the new score is valid and higher</li>
</ul>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Full code: <a href="https://github.com/jamesbachini/typezero/blob/main/contracts/leaderboard/src/lib.rs">https://github.com/jamesbachini/typezero/blob/main/contracts/leaderboard/src/lib.rs</a></p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Verification snippet:</p>



<pre class="wp-block-code"><code>fn verify_proof(
    env: &amp;Env,
    verifier_id: &amp;Address,
    journal_hash: &amp;BytesN&lt;32&gt;,
    image_id: &amp;BytesN&lt;32&gt;,
    seal: &amp;Bytes,
) {
    let mut args: Vec&lt;Val&gt; = Vec::new(env);
    args.push_back(seal.into_val(env));
    args.push_back(image_id.into_val(env));
    args.push_back(journal_hash.into_val(env));

    let result =
        env.try_invoke_contract::&lt;Val, InvokeError&gt;(verifier_id, &amp;Symbol::new(env, "verify"), args);
    match result {
        Ok(Ok(_)) =&gt; {}
        Ok(Err(_)) | Err(_) =&gt; panic_with_error!(env, Error::ProofVerificationFailed),
    }
}</code></pre>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Game Hub Integration</h2>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Stellar has an emerging web3 gaming ecosystem which follows a standardized format.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">This contract will call the mock gamehub contract on testnet at:</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><a href="https://stellar.expert/explorer/testnet/contract/CB4VZAT2U3UC6XFK3N23SKRF2NDCMP3QHJYMCHHFMZO7MRQO6DQ2EMYG?filter=interface" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">CB4VZAT2U3UC6XFK3N23SKRF2NDCMP3QHJYMCHHFMZO7MRQO6DQ2EMYG</a></p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">This includes the following interface:</p>



<pre class="wp-block-code"><code>#&#91;contractclient(name = "GameHubClient")]
pub trait GameHub {
    fn start_game(
        env: Env,
        game_id: Address,
        session_id: u32,
        player1: Address,
        player2: Address,
        player1_points: i128,
        player2_points: i128,
    );

    fn end_game(
        env: Env,
        session_id: u32,
        player1_won: bool
    );
}</code></pre>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">In this game player1 is the &#8220;house&#8221; and player2 is the user. We make a remote call to start_game() and end_game() in this external contract.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">For more information on building games on Stellar visit:</p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><a href="https://ohloss.com">https://ohloss.com</a></li>



<li><a href="https://jamesbachini.github.io/Stellar-Game-Studio/">https://jamesbachini.github.io/Stellar-Game-Studio/</a></li>
</ul>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Setup Instructions</h2>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The application relies on a backend and docker to generate the proofs. This isn&#8217;t in production so if you want to play with it you&#8217;ll have to clone it and set it up locally.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">You&#8217;ll need Rust and the Stellar cli installed to get this up and running.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Start by cloning the repo, starting dockerd and running the make file.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-code"><code>git clone https://github.com/jamesbachini/typezero.git
cd typezero
sudo dockerd &amp;
make dev</code></pre>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">This deploys the contract to testnet, writes frontend config, and starts backend and frontend services.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Open up a browser on localhost port 5173 (don&#8217;t try port 3000 which is the backend)</p>



<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><a href="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-scaled.png"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="505" data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-1024x505.png" alt="" class="wp-image-8157 lazyload" data-srcset="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-1024x505.png 1024w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-300x148.png 300w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-768x379.png 768w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-1536x758.png 1536w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-2048x1010.png 2048w, https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-scaled.png 1920w" data-sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 1024px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 1024/505;" /></a></figure>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-alpha-channel-opacity"/>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">TypeZERO demonstrates how devs can combine smart contract platforms with zero-knowledge technology.</p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Note the code is for demonstration purposes only.</p>
</div>

<div style="text-align: center; width: 280px; height: 320px; margin: 20px auto 0px auto; border:4px solid #444; border-radius: 4px;"><iframe data-src="https://bachini.substack.com/embed" scrolling="no" width="280" height="320" frameborder="0" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" class="lazyload" data-load-mode="1"></iframe></div>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><em>The Blockchain Sector</em> newsletter goes out a few times a month when there is breaking news or interesting developments to discuss. All the content I produce is free, if you&#8217;d like to help please share this content on social media.</p>



<p class="has-text-align-center wp-block-paragraph">Thank you.</p>



<figure class="wp-block-image aligncenter size-full is-resized"><a href="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/signature_grey.png"><img data-src="https://jamesbachini.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/signature_grey.png" alt="James Bachini" class="wp-image-5039 lazyload" width="150" height="68" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==" style="--smush-placeholder-width: 150px; --smush-placeholder-aspect-ratio: 150/68;" /></a></figure>



<p class="has-text-color has-small-font-size wp-block-paragraph" style="color:#828689"><strong>Disclaimer</strong>: Not a financial advisor, not financial advice. The content I create is to document my journey and for educational and entertainment purposes only. It is not under any circumstances investment advice. I am not an investment or trading professional and am learning myself while still making plenty of mistakes along the way. Any code published is experimental and not production ready to be used for financial transactions. Do your own research and do not play with funds you do not want to lose. </p>

<!-- Substack Overlay -->
<script type="2ad0f8b3f53b27a6cd908e12-text/javascript">
const showOverlay = function() {
  document.body.innerHTML += `<div id="overlay-substack" style='display: block; z-index: 9999999; position: fixed; left: 0; right: 0; top: 0; bottom: 0; background: #111111; text-align: center;'><div style='width: 100%; text-align: right;'><div id='overlay-close' style='font-size: 26px; color: #555; cursor: pointer; padding: 20px;'>X</div></div><h1>Get The Latest Insider Blockchain News</h1><iframe data-src='https://bachini.substack.com/embed' width='300' height='320' style='border:1px solid #EEE; background:white; margin: 10px auto;' frameborder='0' scrolling='no' src='data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==' class='lazyload' data-load-mode='1'></iframe><div style='width: 320px; text-align: left; margin: 10px auto; line-height: 200%;'>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f4f0')} Be First To Hear Breaking News<br>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f50d')} Explainers For DeFi Protocols<br>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f4c8')} Independent Market Commentary<br>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f393')} Web3 & Solidity Dev Tutorials<br>${String.fromCodePoint('0x1f4b0')} Free Newsletter, For Everyone<br><div style="font-size: 0.9em; width: 100%; text-align: center;"><a href="https://bachini.substack.com" target="_blank">https://bachini.substack.com</a></div></div></div>`;
  document.getElementById('overlay-close').onclick = function() {
    document.getElementById('overlay-substack').style.display = 'none';
  };
}
const myScrollFunc = function() {
  const y = window.scrollY;
  const lastOverlay = localStorage.lastOverlay || 0;
  const ts = new Date().getTime(); 
  if (y >= 3000 && lastOverlay < ts -  604800000) {
    localStorage.setItem('lastOverlay', ts);
    if (localStorage.getItem('lastOverlay') == ts) {
      showOverlay();
    }
  }
};
setTimeout(function() {
  window.addEventListener('scroll', myScrollFunc);
}, 30000);
</script>
<!-- End Substack Overlay -->

  window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];
  function gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments);}
  gtag('js', new Date());
  gtag('config', 'UA-48537439-11');
</script>

<script type="2ad0f8b3f53b27a6cd908e12-text/javascript">
window.lastScrollTime = false;
window.addEventListener('scroll', function() {
	window.lastScrollTime = new Date().getTime();
});
setTimeout(async function() {
	if (gtag && window.lastScrollTime) {
		gtag('event','timedScroll', {
			'event_category': 'blog'
		});
	}
}, 35000);
</script>

<style>
.single-post img {
  max-width: 100%;
}
.single-post a {
  word-break: break-all;
}
a, a:visited, a:active {
    color: #EEE !important;
}
a:hover {
    color: #FFF !important;
}
.wp-block-navigation__submenu-container {
    background: #111111EE !important;
}
.wp-block-navigation__responsive-container-content {
    background: #111111EE !important;
}
.table td, .table tr, .table th {
  padding: 5px;
  border: 1px solid #444;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #444 !important;
}
.table-center td, .table-center th {
  text-align: center !important;
}
.table  th {
  font-weight: 400;
  background: #23282d;
  color: #FFF00F;
}
.table tr {
  background: #111;
}
.table tr:nth-child(even) {
  background: #000;
}
.video-wrapper {
  position: relative;
  padding-top: 25px;
  width: 100%;
  padding-bottom: 380px;
}
.video-wrapper iframe {
  position: absolute;
  top: 80px;
  left: 0;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: 400px;
}
.video-header {
  background: #000;
  color: #EEE;
  width: 100%;
  height: 60px;
}
.video-channel {
  font-size: 1.3em;
  font-weight: bold;
  float: left;
  margin-left: 10px;
  margin-top: 15px;
}
.video-button {
  float: right;
  height: 40px;
  margin-right: 10px;
  margin-top: 10px;
  max-width: 33%;
  cursor: pointer
}
.entry-inner {
  word-wrap: break-word;
}
.responsive-image {
  max-width: 100%;
  max-height: 100%;
}  
.text-small {
  font-size: 0.8em;
}
.screenshot {
  border: 3px solid #444;
  border-radius: 5px;
  box-shadow: 0 0 5px #222;
  -webkit-box-shadow: 0 0 5px #222;
  -moz-box-shadow: 0 0 5px #222;
  box-sizing: border-box;
  -moz-box-sizing: border-box;
  -webkit-box-sizing: border-box;
}
.standout {
  background: #000;
  border-left: 5px solid #FFF00F;
  padding: 10px 10px 10px 20px;
  font-size: 1.1em;
}
.bullet {
  float: left;
  font-weight: bold;
  border-radius: 99px;
  background: #FFF00F;
  width: 27px;
  height: 27px;
  text-align: center;
  margin-right: 10px;
  color: #FFF;
}
code {
    background: #070707;
    color: #CCC;
    border: 3px solid #444;
    border-radius: 5px;
    padding: 10px;
    font-size: 0.8em;
}
.console {
  background: #000 !important;
  color: #6F0;
  border-radius: 3px;
  padding: 2px 4px;
}
.console-error {
  color: #F44;
}
.console code {
  background: #000 !important;
  color: #6F0;
}
.wp-block-post-title {
  font-weight: bold;
}
.wp-block-post-title, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 {
  background-image: linear-gradient(to right, #FFF00F, #f85050);
  color: transparent;
  background-clip: text;
  background: -webkit-linear-gradient(left, #FFF00F, #f85050);
  -webkit-background-clip: text;
  -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent;
}
@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
  .mobile-hidden {
    display: none;
  }
}

class="clean-btn theme-back-to-top-button backToTopButton_sjWU" type="button"></button><div class="docRoot_UBD9"><main class="docMainContainer_TBSr docMainContainerEnhanced_lQrH"><div class="container padding-top--md padding-bottom--lg"><div class="row"><div class="col docItemCol_VOVn"><div class="docItemContainer_Djhp"><article><div class="tocCollapsible_ETCw theme-doc-toc-mobile tocMobile_ITEo"><button type="button" class="clean-btn tocCollapsibleButton_TO0P">On this page</button></div><div class="theme-doc-markdown markdown"><header><h1>Key Terminology</h1></header>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="assumption">Assumption<a href="#assumption" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Assumption" title="Direct link to Assumption">​</a></h3>
<p>An assumption is <a href="#receipt-claim">receipt claim</a> upon which a <a href="#receipt">receipt</a> is <a href="#conditional">conditional</a>.
When the <a href="#guest-program">guest</a> uses <a href="#composition">composition</a> to verify a receipt in the <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a>, an assumption is added to the <a href="#receipt">receipt</a>.
This assumption can be proven and resolved later, either through <a href="#recursion">recursion</a> or by providing proof of the assumption to the verifier.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="circuit">Circuit<a href="#circuit" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Circuit" title="Direct link to Circuit">​</a></h3>
<p>A mathematical construct that acts as the &quot;CPU&quot; of the <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a> in a manner that enables creating <a href="#validity-proof">proofs</a>. <br>
See also: <a href="/reference-docs/about-arithmetic-circuits">Arithmetic Circuits</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="clock-cycles">Clock Cycles<a href="#clock-cycles" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Clock Cycles" title="Direct link to Clock Cycles">​</a></h3>
<p>The smallest unit of compute in the zkVM <a href="#circuit">circuit</a>, analogous to a clock cycle on a physical CPU.
The complexity of a <a href="#guest-program">guest program</a>&#x27;s <a href="#execute">execution</a> is measured in clock cycles as they directly affect the memory, proof size, and time performance of the <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a>.</p>
<p>Generally, a single cycle corresponds to a single <a href="#risc-v">RISC-V</a> operation. However, some operations require two cycles.</p>
<p>See the <a href="/api/zkvm/optimization">Optimization Guide</a> for more information about the zkVM cycles and performance.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="commit">Commit<a href="#commit" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Commit" title="Direct link to Commit">​</a></h3>
<p>Append data to the <a href="#journal">journal</a>. <br>
See also: <a href="https://docs.rs/risc0-zkvm/*/risc0_zkvm/guest/env/fn.commit.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">env::commit()</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="composition">Composition<a href="#composition" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Composition" title="Direct link to Composition">​</a></h3>
<p>As of our 0.20 release, the <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a> supports verification of RISC Zero receipts inside the RISC Zero <a href="#guest-program">guest</a> through a feature known as composition.
With this, multiple zkVM programs can be <em>composed</em> and produce a single receipt that verifies all computation done to reach the final result.</p>
<p>Some use cases for composition include:</p>
<ul>
<li>Splitting a program into multiple parts, proven by different parties, to preserve privacy and data ownership of each party.
<ul>
<li>E.g. Produce a proof that a ciphertext is a correct encryption of some value to a valid public key.</li>
<li>E.g. Produce a proof for a database query by joining receipts from the query over each privately held shard.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Aggregating many proofs into one for efficient batch verification.
<ul>
<li>E.g. Produce a proof for a block of transactions, where each transaction is itself verified by a receipt.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Creating a single receipt for a workflow that might be split into many different operations.
<ul>
<li>E.g. Produce a single receipt for the result of an image processing pipeline, where different filters are in their own guests.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="conditional">Conditional<a href="#conditional" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Conditional" title="Direct link to Conditional">​</a></h3>
<p>A <a href="#receipt">receipt</a> can be described as &quot;conditional&quot; if it is only true when one or more unproven <a href="#assumption">assumptions</a> are also true.
When the <a href="#assumption">assumptions</a> are not proven to be true, a conditional receipt does not provide any guarantees, and so verifiers will reject conditional receipts.
A conditional receipt can be made unconditional by providing receipts proving all <a href="#assumption">assumptions</a>.
Conditional receipts are a part of the <a href="#composition">composition</a> feature.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="continuations">Continuations<a href="#continuations" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Continuations" title="Direct link to Continuations">​</a></h3>
<p>As of our 0.15 release, the <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a> uses continuations to enable proof generation for very large programs without blowing up memory requirements.
Large proof generation is made possible by splitting the execution trace for a single <a href="#session">session</a> into a number of <a href="#segment">segments</a>, and proving those segments independently. <br>
See also: <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=v4HIwaqmIxk&amp;list=PLcPzhUaCxlCirUkJY0ltpjdtzWcz5U_6y&amp;index=1" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Continuations study club</a>, <a href="https://www.risczero.com/news/continuations" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Continuations blog</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="control-id">Control ID<a href="#control-id" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Control ID" title="Direct link to Control ID">​</a></h3>
<p>The control ID is the Merkle hash of the contents of the control columns, which are assumed to be known to the verifier as part of the circuit definition.</p>
<p>The control ID is the first entry in the <a href="#seal">seal</a> and plays a key role in defining the operations of the circuit. Verifying a <a href="#receipt">receipt</a> involves:</p>
<ul>
<li>Checking the control ID on the receipt against the control ID for the expected version of the zkVM.</li>
<li>Verifying the validity of the Merkle branches for various entries of the Control columns.</li>
</ul>
<p>For a closer look at how the control columns are used to manage constraint enforcement, check out our <a href="/proof-system/stark-by-hand">STARK by Hand</a> explainer.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="control-root">Control Root<a href="#control-root" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Control Root" title="Direct link to Control Root">​</a></h3>
<p>The control root is the Merkle hash of a set of <a href="#control-id">control ID</a>s.</p>
<p>The control root plays a key role in enforcing zkVM version control. Each <a href="#control-id">control ID</a> identifies one <a href="#recursion-program">recursion program</a>; the control root identifies the list of allowed recursion programs. When the RISC-V circuit or recursion programs change, the control root is updated to reflect the new set of allowed functions.</p>
<p>This design allows for updates to the <a href="#risc-v-circuit">RISC-V circuit</a> without necessitating a new trusted setup for the <a href="#groth16-circuit">Groth16 circuit</a>. <br>
See also: <a href="https://github.com/risc0/risc0/blob/v0.21.0/risc0/circuit/recursion/src/control_id.rs#L16" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Code reference for control root</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="deterministic-builds">Deterministic Builds<a href="#deterministic-builds" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Deterministic Builds" title="Direct link to Deterministic Builds">​</a></h3>
<p>A compilation process is called &quot;deterministic&quot; (or &quot;reproducible&quot;) if it reliably produces the same binary file, on a byte-by-byte level.</p>
<p>In the context of RISC Zero application development, deterministic builds are necessary to ensure a clear linkage between the source code for the <a href="#guest-program">guest program</a> and the resulting <a href="#image-id">Image ID</a>.</p>
<p>To access deterministic builds for your zkVM application, use <a href="https://docs.rs/crate/cargo-risczero/latest" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><code>cargo risczero build</code></a>. Deterministic builds are made possible by running the <code>rustc</code> compiler inside a Docker container.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="elf-binary">ELF Binary<a href="#elf-binary" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to ELF Binary" title="Direct link to ELF Binary">​</a></h3>
<p>The executable format for the <a href="#risc-v">RISC-V</a> instruction set.
Each <a href="#method">method</a> in a <a href="#guest-program">guest program</a> is written in Rust and compiled into an ELF binary before execution begins. Then, the ELF binary is <a href="#executor">executed</a> and <a href="#prover">proven</a>.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="execute">Execute<a href="#execute" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Execute" title="Direct link to Execute">​</a></h3>
<p>Run a <a href="#method">method</a> inside the <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a> and produce an <a href="#execution-trace">execution trace</a> which can be used by the <a href="#prover">prover</a> to create a <a href="#validity-proof">proof</a> of correct execution.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="executor">Executor<a href="#executor" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Executor" title="Direct link to Executor">​</a></h3>
<p>The portion of the <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a> responsible for generating the <a href="#execution-trace">execution trace</a>. <br>
See also: <a href="https://docs.rs/risc0-zkvm/*/risc0_zkvm/trait.Executor.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Executor documentation</a>, <a href="#prover">Prover</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="execution-trace">Execution Trace<a href="#execution-trace" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Execution Trace" title="Direct link to Execution Trace">​</a></h3>
<p>A complete record of a computation.
The execution trace is organized as a rectangular array, where each row describes a complete snapshot of the state of the <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a> at a given moment in time.
The width of the execution trace relates to the number of registers/components in the machine, and the length of the execution trace relates to the number of <a href="#clock-cycles">clock cycles</a> of the program&#x27;s execution.</p>
<p>The execution trace is generated by the <a href="#executor">executor</a> and checked for validity by the <a href="#prover">prover</a>.
A valid trace means that the <a href="#elf-binary">ELF binary</a> was faithfully executed according to the rules of the <a href="#risc-v">RISC-V</a> instruction set architecture.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="groth16">Groth16<a href="#groth16" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Groth16" title="Direct link to Groth16">​</a></h3>
<p>A proof system that offers a very small proof size, suitable for on-chain verification.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="groth16-circuit">Groth16 Circuit<a href="#groth16-circuit" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Groth16 Circuit" title="Direct link to Groth16 Circuit">​</a></h3>
<p>RISC Zero&#x27;s Groth16 Circuit is used to generate <a href="#receipt">receipts</a> that are small enough to support on-chain verification.
The Groth16 Circuit is a circom implementation of the verifier for the <a href="#recursion-circuit">Recursion Circuit</a>.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="groth16-receipt">Groth16 Receipt<a href="#groth16-receipt" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Groth16 Receipt" title="Direct link to Groth16 Receipt">​</a></h3>
<p>A Groth16 Receipt is a very small <a href="#validity-proof">validity proof</a>, used primarily for on-chain verification.
The Groth16 Receipt is constructed using the <a href="#groth16-circuit">Groth16 Circuit</a>.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="guest">Guest<a href="#guest" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Guest" title="Direct link to Guest">​</a></h3>
<p>The system running inside the <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a>. <br>
See also: <a href="https://docs.rs/risc0-zkvm/*/risc0_zkvm/guest" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Rust crate for zkVM guest</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="guest-program">Guest Program<a href="#guest-program" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Guest Program" title="Direct link to Guest Program">​</a></h3>
<p>The portion of a <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a> application that gets proven.
The guest program receives inputs from an (untrusted) <a href="#host">host</a>.
Depending on the application, the guest program can <a href="#commit">commit</a> results to the <a href="#journal">journal</a> and/or send them to the host. <br>
See also: <a href="https://docs.rs/risc0-zkvm/*/risc0_zkvm/guest" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Rust crate for zkVM guest</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="host">Host<a href="#host" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Host" title="Direct link to Host">​</a></h3>
<p>The system the <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a> runs on.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="host-program">Host Program<a href="#host-program" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Host Program" title="Direct link to Host Program">​</a></h3>
<p>The <a href="#host">host</a>-native, untrusted portion of a <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a> application.
The host program loads the <a href="#guest-program">guest program</a> and provides inputs to the guest program as necessary.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="image-id">Image ID<a href="#image-id" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Image ID" title="Direct link to Image ID">​</a></h3>
<p>A small cryptographic identifier that indicates the <a href="#method">method</a> or boot image for zkVM <a href="#execute">execution</a>. <br>
See also: <a href="https://docs.rs/risc0-zkvm/*/risc0_zkvm/struct.SystemState.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ImageID documentation</a>, <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QwzrBHHkzFE&amp;list=PLcPzhUaCxlCirUkJY0ltpjdtzWcz5U_6y&amp;index=4" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ImageID excerpt from Study Club</a>, <a href="#segment">segment</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="journal">Journal<a href="#journal" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Journal" title="Direct link to Journal">​</a></h3>
<p>The portion of the <a href="#receipt">receipt</a> that contains the public outputs of a <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a> application. <br>
See also: <a href="#commit">commit</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="method">Method<a href="#method" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Method" title="Direct link to Method">​</a></h3>
<p>A single &#x27;main&#x27; entry point for code that runs inside the <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a>.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="prove">Prove<a href="#prove" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Prove" title="Direct link to Prove">​</a></h3>
<p>Generate a <a href="#receipt">receipt</a> that serves as <a href="#validity-proof">proof</a> of correct execution of a <a href="#guest-program">guest program</a>.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="prover">Prover<a href="#prover" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Prover" title="Direct link to Prover">​</a></h3>
<p>The portion of the <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a> that <a href="#execute">executes</a> and <a href="#prove">proves</a> a <a href="#guest-program">guest program</a>, thereby constructing a <a href="#receipt">receipt</a>. <br>
See also: <a href="https://docs.rs/risc0-zkvm/*/risc0_zkvm/trait.Prover.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Prover documentation</a>, <a href="#executor">Executor</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="receipt">Receipt<a href="#receipt" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Receipt" title="Direct link to Receipt">​</a></h3>
<p>A receipt attests to valid <a href="#execute">execution</a> of a <a href="#guest-program">guest program</a>.
<a href="#verify">Verifying</a> the receipt provides cryptographic assurance that the <a href="#journal">journal</a> was indeed constructed using the expected <a href="#circuit">circuit</a> and the expected <a href="#image-id">imageID</a>.
The receipt consists of the <a href="#receipt-claim">receipt claim</a> and the <a href="#seal">seal</a>.
The <a href="#receipt-claim">receipt claim</a> contains the <a href="#journal">journal</a> along with other important details, and constitutes to the public outputs of the program.
The <a href="#seal">seal</a> is the opaque blob that cryptographically attests to the validity of the <a href="#receipt-claim">receipt claim</a>.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="receipt-claim">Receipt Claim<a href="#receipt-claim" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Receipt Claim" title="Direct link to Receipt Claim">​</a></h3>
<p>Every receipt has an associated &quot;receipt claim&quot; or &quot;claim&quot;, also known as the &quot;public output&quot;.
The claim is the statement that the receipt provides proof of.
It contains the <a href="#journal">journal</a>, and it additionally includes information about the <a href="#image-id">imageID</a>, exit status (e.g. if the program exits successfully or encountered an error), and the memory state at the end of execution.
A simple example of a claim is &quot;I ran the Fibonacci sequence calculator with input &quot;21&quot; and got output &quot;10946&quot;.
RISC Zero provides a formal system for defining and proving claims.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="recursion">Recursion<a href="#recursion" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Recursion" title="Direct link to Recursion">​</a></h3>
<p>When a zero-knowledge proof is verified within a zero-knowledge proof, this is referred to as &quot;recursion&quot;.
RISC Zero uses recursion to underpin features such as <a href="#continuations">continuations</a> and <a href="#composition">composition</a>.
Through recursion, RISC Zero can take two or more <a href="#receipt">receipts</a> and compress them into one receipt.
By repeating this compression, an arbitrary number of related <a href="#receipt">receipts</a> (e.g. all the <a href="#segment">Segments</a> of a <a href="#session">Session</a>) can be compressed into a single receipt.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="recursion-circuit">Recursion Circuit<a href="#recursion-circuit" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Recursion Circuit" title="Direct link to Recursion Circuit">​</a></h3>
<p>The recursion circuit is used to aggregate and compose <a href="#validity-proof">proofs</a>.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="recursion-program">Recursion Program<a href="#recursion-program" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Recursion Program" title="Direct link to Recursion Program">​</a></h3>
<p>The <a href="#recursion-circuit">recursion circuit</a> is capable of efficiently evaluating polynomial constraints, and was specifically designed to verify STARK proofs. Programs written for this circuit are loaded into the control columns. Each recursion program is identified by a <a href="#control-id">Control ID</a>.</p>
<p>For a list of all supported recursion programs, see the documentation for the <a href="https://docs.rs/risc0-zkvm/*/risc0_zkvm/struct.ApiClient.html#" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">zkVM API Client</a>.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="risc-v">RISC-V<a href="#risc-v" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to RISC-V" title="Direct link to RISC-V">​</a></h3>
<p>The 5th edition of UC Berkeley&#x27;s reduced instruction set computer architecture.
RISC Zero uses RISC-V, specifically the <a href="https://riscv.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/12/riscv-spec-20191213.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">rv32im instruction set</a>, as the basis for the RISC Zero zkVM.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="risc-v-circuit">RISC-V Circuit<a href="#risc-v-circuit" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to RISC-V Circuit" title="Direct link to RISC-V Circuit">​</a></h3>
<p>The RISC-V circuit is the core of the <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a>.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="seal">Seal<a href="#seal" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Seal" title="Direct link to Seal">​</a></h3>
<p>The portion of the <a href="#receipt">receipt</a> that cryptographically attests to the <a href="#receipt-claim">receipt claim</a> and the correct execution of the <a href="#guest-program">guest program</a>.
Concretely, the seal is a <a href="#stark">zk-STARK</a> and is generated by the <a href="#prover">prover</a>. <br>
See also: <a href="#validity-proof">validity proof</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="segment">Segment<a href="#segment" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Segment" title="Direct link to Segment">​</a></h3>
<p>The <a href="#execution-trace">execution trace</a> of a portion of a <a href="#guest-program">guest program</a>.
The execution of a segment begins at some initial memory image (identified by the <a href="#image-id">ImageID</a>) and proceeds until terminated by the system or user.
This represents a chunk of execution work that will be proven in a single call to the zero-knowledge proof system. <br>
See also: <a href="#session">Session</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="session">Session<a href="#session" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Session" title="Direct link to Session">​</a></h3>
<p>The <a href="#execution-trace">execution trace</a> of a <a href="#guest-program">guest program</a>.
The session&#x27;s execution starts from an initial memory image (which includes the starting program counter) and proceeds until either a <code>sys_halt</code> or a <code>sys_pause</code> syscall is encountered.
This record is stored as a vector of <a href="#segment">Segments</a>.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="stark">STARK<a href="#stark" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to STARK" title="Direct link to STARK">​</a></h3>
<p>A STARK (scalable, transparent argument of knowledge) serves as proof of validity for a <a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a> program. <br>
See also: <a href="/reference-docs/about-starks">About STARKs</a>, <a href="/proof-system/proof-system-sequence-diagram">Sequence Diagram for RISC Zero&#x27;s STARK</a>, <a href="https://risczero.com/proof-system-in-detail.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">RISC Zero&#x27;s ZKP Whitepaper</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="snark">SNARK<a href="#snark" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to SNARK" title="Direct link to SNARK">​</a></h3>
<p>A SNARK (succinct non-interactive argument of knowledge) serves as proof of validity for Blockchain applications due to their small size compared to <a href="#stark">STARK</a> proofs. <br>
See also: <a href="https://ethereum.org/en/developers/docs/scaling/zk-rollups#validity-proofs" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">About SNARKs</a></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="validity-proof">Validity Proof<a href="#validity-proof" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Validity Proof" title="Direct link to Validity Proof">​</a></h3>
<p>A validity proof is a cryptographic argument that attests to the validity of an <a href="#execution-trace">execution trace</a>.
The <a href="#seal">seal</a> on the <a href="#receipt">receipt</a> serves as a validity proof for the RISC-V instruction set.
A validity proof is often referred to as a &quot;zero-knowledge proof&quot;, or simply a &quot;proof&quot;.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="verifier">Verifier<a href="#verifier" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Verifier" title="Direct link to Verifier">​</a></h3>
<p>A program on the <a href="#host">host</a> that <a href="#verify">verifies</a> a <a href="#receipt">receipt</a>.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="verify">Verify<a href="#verify" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Verify" title="Direct link to Verify">​</a></h3>
<p>Check that the <a href="#receipt">receipt</a> is valid.
Verifying <a href="#segment">segment</a> receipts requires checking that the <a href="#seal">seal</a> is valid and was generated by the expected <a href="#image-id">ImageID</a>.
Verifying <a href="#session">session</a> receipts requires checking the validity of each <a href="#segment">segment</a> and checking that the initial/final memory snapshots for each segment align appropriately.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">Zero-Knowledge Virtual Machine (zkVM)<a href="#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Zero-Knowledge Virtual Machine (zkVM)" title="Direct link to Zero-Knowledge Virtual Machine (zkVM)">​</a></h3>
<p>A virtual machine that runs trusted code and generates proofs.
RISC Zero&#x27;s zkVM implements the RISC-V instruction set architecture and uses a <a href="#stark">STARK</a>-based proof system.</p></div></article><nav class="pagination-nav docusaurus-mt-lg" 13v10h-21v-19h12v2h-10v15h17v-8h2zm3-12h-10.988l4.035 4-6.977 7.07 2.828 2.828 6.977-7.07 4.125 4.172v-11z"></path></svg></a></li></ul></nav></div></div></aside><main class="docMainContainer_TBSr"><div class="container padding-top--md padding-bottom--lg"><div class="row"><div class="col docItemCol_VOVn"><div class="docItemContainer_Djhp"><article><nav class="theme-doc-breadcrumbs breadcrumbsContainer_Z_bl" aria-label="Breadcrumbs"><ul class="breadcrumbs" itemscope="" itemtype="https://schema.org/BreadcrumbList"><li class="breadcrumbs__item"><a aria-label="Home page" class="breadcrumbs__link" href="/"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="breadcrumbHomeIcon_YNFT"><path d="M10 19v-5h4v5c0 .55.45 1 1 1h3c.55 0 1-.45 1-1v-7h1.7c.46 0 .68-.57.33-.87L12.67 3.6c-.38-.34-.96-.34-1.34 0l-8.36 7.53c-.34.3-.13.87.33.87H5v7c0 .55.45 1 1 1h3c.55 0 1-.45 1-1z" fill="currentColor"></path></svg></a></li><li class="breadcrumbs__item"><span class="breadcrumbs__link">What is a zkVM Application?</span><meta itemprop="position" content="1"></li><li itemscope="" itemprop="itemListElement" itemtype="https://schema.org/ListItem" class="breadcrumbs__item breadcrumbs__item--active"><span class="breadcrumbs__link" itemprop="name">Overview</span><meta itemprop="position" content="2"></li></ul></nav><span class="theme-doc-version-badge badge badge--secondary">Version: 3.0</span><div class="tocCollapsible_ETCw theme-doc-toc-mobile tocMobile_ITEo"><button type="button" class="clean-btn tocCollapsibleButton_TO0P">On this page</button></div><div class="theme-doc-markdown markdown"><header><h1>zkVM Overview</h1></header>
<p>The RISC Zero zero-knowledge virtual machine <a href="/terminology#zero-knowledge-virtual-machine-zkvm">zkVM</a> (zkVM) lets you <a href="/terminology#validity-proof">prove</a>
correct execution of arbitrary Rust code. By allowing users to build
zero-knowledge applications that <strong>leverage existing Rust packages</strong>, the RISC
Zero zkVM makes it quick and easy to build powerful verifiable software
applications.</p>
<p>To learn more about what this enables, check out our <a href="/api/use-cases">use cases</a> page.</p>
<p>If you&#x27;re ready to start building, check out our <a href="/api/zkvm/quickstart">zkVM Quick Start</a>
and our <a href="/api/zkvm/examples">zkVM example applications</a>.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="what-are-the-components-of-a-zkvm-application">What are the components of a zkVM application?<a href="#what-are-the-components-of-a-zkvm-application" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to What are the components of a zkVM application?" title="Direct link to What are the components of a zkVM application?">​</a></h2>
<p>The core of a zkVM application is the <a href="/api/zkvm/guest-code-101">guest program</a>.
The guest program is the part of the application that gets proven.</p>
<p>To prove correct execution of the <a href="/api/zkvm/guest-code-101">guest program</a>:</p>
<ol>
<li>The <a href="/api/zkvm/guest-code-101">guest program</a> is compiled to an <a href="/terminology#elf-binary">ELF binary</a>. <br></li>
<li>The <a href="/terminology#executor">executor</a> runs the <a href="/terminology#elf-binary">ELF binary</a> and records the <a href="/terminology#session">session</a>.</li>
<li>The <a href="/terminology#prover">prover</a> checks and proves the validity of the <a href="/terminology#session">session</a>, outputting a
<a href="/api/zkvm/receipts">receipt</a>.</li>
</ol>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Note:</strong> You only need to worry about the details of step 2 &amp; step 3 if
you&#x27;re running the zkVM on your own hardware. See <a href="/api/zkvm/host-code-101">Host Code 101</a> for more details on these steps. If you&#x27;re using <a href="https://boundless.network" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Boundless</a> for remote
proving, you won&#x27;t need to write host code.</p>
</blockquote>
<p><img decoding="async" loading="lazy" alt="From Guest Code to Receipt" src="/assets/images/from-rust-to-receipt-23117368c4f46d78c8cac3b753245a5a.png" width="1778" height="892" class="img_ev3q"></p>
<p>Anyone with a copy of the <a href="/api/zkvm/receipts">receipt</a> can <a href="/terminology#verify">verify</a> the <a href="/api/zkvm/guest-code-101">guest program</a>&#x27;s execution
and read its <a href="/terminology#journal">publicly shared outputs</a>. The verification algorithm
receives an <a href="/terminology#image-id">ImageID</a> as a parameter; the ImageID serves as a cryptographic
identifier for the expected ELF binary.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="deploying-a-zkvm-application">Deploying a zkVM Application<a href="#deploying-a-zkvm-application" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Deploying a zkVM Application" title="Direct link to Deploying a zkVM Application">​</a></h2>
<p>Once you&#x27;ve built a zkVM application, you can generate proofs on your own
machine(s) or you can make requests to <a href="https://boundless.network" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Boundless</a> to generate proofs as needed.</p>
<p>For local proof generation, we recommend a machine with at least 16GB of RAM.
Options for generating proofs using GPU acceleration and skipping proof
generation are documented in the <a href="https://github.com/risc0/risc0#feature-flags" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">feature flags</a>.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="next-steps">Next Steps<a href="#next-steps" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Next Steps" title="Direct link to Next Steps">​</a></h2>
<p><em>Ready to start building zkVM applications?</em> <br>
Check out <a href="/api/zkvm/quickstart">zkVM Quickstart</a> &amp; <a href="/api/zkvm/guest-code-101">Guest Code 101</a>.</p>
<p><em>Want to browse some examples?</em> <br>
Check out the <a href="/api/zkvm/examples">zkVM example applications</a>.</p>
<p><em>Curious about performance?</em> <br>
Check the <a href="/api/zkvm/benchmarks">performance benchmarks</a> page.</p>
<p><em>Looking for information about remote proving?</em> <br>
Check out <a href="https://boundless.network" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Boundless</a>.</p>
<p><em>Looking for information about blockchain integration?</em> <br>
Check out our <a href="/api/blockchain-integration/risc-zero-on-eth">Risc Zero on Eth</a> page.</p></div><footer class="theme-doc-footer docusaurus-mt-lg"><div class="row margin-top--sm theme-doc-footer-edit-meta-row"><div class="col"><a href="https://github.com/risc0/risc0/edit/main/website/api/zkvm/zkvm-overview.md" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="row"><div class="col docItemCol_VOVn"><div class="docItemContainer_Djhp"><article><nav class="theme-doc-breadcrumbs breadcrumbsContainer_Z_bl" aria-label="Breadcrumbs"><ul class="breadcrumbs" class="breadcrumbHomeIcon_YNFT"><path d="M10 19v-5h4v5c0 .55.45 1 1 1h3c.55 0 1-.45 1-1v-7h1.7c.46 0 .68-.57.33-.87L12.67 3.6c-.38-.34-.96-.34-1.34 0l-8.36 7.53c-.34.3-.13.87.33.87H5v7c0 .55.45 1 1 1h3c.55 0 1-.45 1-1z" fill="currentColor"></path></svg></a></li><li class="breadcrumbs__item"><span class="breadcrumbs__link">Developing your zkVM Application</span><meta itemprop="position" itemprop="name">Quick Start</span><meta itemprop="position" content="2"></li></ul></nav><span class="theme-doc-version-badge badge badge--secondary">Version: 3.0</span><div class="tocCollapsible_ETCw theme-doc-toc-mobile tocMobile_ITEo"><button type="button" class="clean-btn tocCollapsibleButton_TO0P">On this page</button></div><div class="theme-doc-markdown markdown"><header><h1>zkVM Quick Start</h1></header>
<p>Welcome to the zkVM Quick Start page! Here are the steps to create your first proof:</p>
<ol>
<li>Install the RISC Zero toolchain with <code>rzup</code>.</li>
<li>Create a new project using the <code>cargo-risczero</code> tool.</li>
<li>Familiarize yourself with the project&#x27;s structure.</li>
<li>Run your project in dev-mode.</li>
<li>Run your project locally to generate a zk-proof.</li>
</ol>
<h2 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="concept-break">Concept break<a href="#concept-break" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Concept break" title="Direct link to Concept break">​</a></h2>
<p>The zkVM provides a zero-knowledge proof of the correct execution of Rust-based
program. The <strong><a href="/terminology#host-program">host</a></strong> is responsible for launching the zkVM (or a
<strong><a href="/terminology#prover">prover</a></strong>), and then the <strong><a href="/terminology#guest-program">guest</a></strong> is the program that runs inside the
zkVM. A proof of execution is a <strong><a href="/terminology#receipt">receipt</a></strong>; it contains a public part ― a
<strong><a href="/terminology#journal">journal</a></strong> and an encryption part ― a <strong><a href="/terminology#seal">seal</a></strong>.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="1-install-the-risc-zero-toolchain">1. Install the RISC Zero Toolchain<a href="#1-install-the-risc-zero-toolchain" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to 1. Install the RISC Zero Toolchain" title="Direct link to 1. Install the RISC Zero Toolchain">​</a></h2>
<p>Install <code>rzup</code> by running the following command:</p>
<div class="language-bash codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color:#F8F8F2;--prism-background-color:#282A36"><div class="codeBlockContent_biex"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-bash codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color:#F8F8F2;background-color:#282A36"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><span class="token-line" style="color:#F8F8F2"><span class="token function" style="color:rgb(80, 250, 123)">curl</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token parameter variable" style="color:rgb(189, 147, 249);font-style:italic">-L</span><span class="token plain"> https://risczero.com/install </span><span class="token operator">|</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token function" style="color:rgb(80, 250, 123)">bash</span><br></span></code></pre><div class="buttonGroup__atx"><button type="button" aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" title="Copy" class="clean-btn"><span class="copyButtonIcons_eSgA" aria-hidden="true"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonIcon_y97N"><path fill="currentColor" d="M19,21H8V7H19M19,5H8A2,2 0 0,0 6,7V21A2,2 0 0,0 8,23H19A2,2 0 0,0 21,21V7A2,2 0 0,0 19,5M16,1H4A2,2 0 0,0 2,3V17H4V3H16V1Z"></path></svg><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonSuccessIcon_LjdS"><path fill="currentColor" d="M21,7L9,19L3.5,13.5L4.91,12.09L9,16.17L19.59,5.59L21,7Z"></path></svg></span></button></div></div></div>
<p>Run <code>rzup</code> to install the RISC Zero toolchain and <code>cargo-risczero</code>.</p>
<div class="language-bash codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color:#F8F8F2;--prism-background-color:#282A36"><div class="codeBlockContent_biex"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-bash codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color:#F8F8F2;background-color:#282A36"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><span class="token-line" style="color:#F8F8F2"><span class="token plain">rzup </span><span class="token function" style="color:rgb(80, 250, 123)">install</span><br></span></code></pre><div class="buttonGroup__atx"><button type="button" aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" title="Copy" class="clean-btn"><span class="copyButtonIcons_eSgA" aria-hidden="true"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonIcon_y97N"><path fill="currentColor" d="M19,21H8V7H19M19,5H8A2,2 0 0,0 6,7V21A2,2 0 0,0 8,23H19A2,2 0 0,0 21,21V7A2,2 0 0,0 19,5M16,1H4A2,2 0 0,0 2,3V17H4V3H16V1Z"></path></svg><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonSuccessIcon_LjdS"><path fill="currentColor" d="M21,7L9,19L3.5,13.5L4.91,12.09L9,16.17L19.59,5.59L21,7Z"></path></svg></span></button></div></div></div>
<h2 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="2-create-a-new-project">2. Create a New Project<a href="#2-create-a-new-project" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to 2. Create a New Project" title="Direct link to 2. Create a New Project">​</a></h2>
<p>The <code>cargo-risczero</code> tool takes <code>--guest-name</code> parameter, a <a href="/terminology#guest-program">guest</a> program that
the zkVM runs, to generate a proof of its execution:</p>
<div class="language-bash codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color:#F8F8F2;--prism-background-color:#282A36"><div class="codeBlockContent_biex"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-bash codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color:#F8F8F2;background-color:#282A36"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><span class="token-line" style="color:#F8F8F2"><span class="token function" style="color:rgb(80, 250, 123)">cargo</span><span class="token plain"> risczero new my_project --guest-name guest_code_for_zk_proof</span><br></span></code></pre><div class="buttonGroup__atx"><button type="button" aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" title="Copy" class="clean-btn"><span class="copyButtonIcons_eSgA" aria-hidden="true"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonIcon_y97N"><path fill="currentColor" d="M19,21H8V7H19M19,5H8A2,2 0 0,0 6,7V21A2,2 0 0,0 8,23H19A2,2 0 0,0 21,21V7A2,2 0 0,0 19,5M16,1H4A2,2 0 0,0 2,3V17H4V3H16V1Z"></path></svg><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonSuccessIcon_LjdS"><path fill="currentColor" d="M21,7L9,19L3.5,13.5L4.91,12.09L9,16.17L19.59,5.59L21,7Z"></path></svg></span></button></div></div></div>
<p>There is a list of options in the <a href="https://github.com/risc0/risc0#feature-flags" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">feature flags</a>.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="3-project-structure">3. Project Structure<a href="#3-project-structure" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to 3. Project Structure" title="Direct link to 3. Project Structure">​</a></h2>
<p>The <a href="/api/zkvm/tutorials/hello-world">Hello World tutorial</a> contains step-by-step instructions on
how to:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="/api/zkvm/tutorials/hello-world#step-2-host-share-private-data-as-input-with-the-guest">Share private data</a> between the host &amp; guest.</li>
<li><a href="/api/zkvm/tutorials/hello-world#step-3-guest-read-input-and-commit-output">Share the guest&#x27;s results</a> with the host publicly.</li>
<li><a href="/api/zkvm/tutorials/hello-world#step-4-host-generate-a-receipt-and-read-its-journal-contents">Generate a receipt</a> and read its journal contents.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="4-quick-development-leveraging-dev-mode">4. Quick Development: Leveraging dev-mode<a href="#4-quick-development-leveraging-dev-mode" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to 4. Quick Development: Leveraging dev-mode" title="Direct link to 4. Quick Development: Leveraging dev-mode">​</a></h2>
<p>During the development of your project, running your code can take a long time
due to the proof generation process. To address this issue and allow for faster
iterations of your code, we suggest utilizing <a href="/api/generating-proofs/dev-mode">dev-mode</a>. This mode bypasses the
time-consuming proof generation process. To activate dev-mode, set the
environment variable <code>RISC0_DEV_MODE=1</code> when executing your project:</p>
<div class="language-bash codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color:#F8F8F2;--prism-background-color:#282A36"><div class="codeBlockContent_biex"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-bash codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color:#F8F8F2;background-color:#282A36"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><span class="token-line" style="color:#F8F8F2"><span class="token assign-left variable" style="color:rgb(189, 147, 249);font-style:italic">RISC0_DEV_MODE</span><span class="token operator">=</span><span class="token number">1</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token function" style="color:rgb(80, 250, 123)">cargo</span><span class="token plain"> run </span><span class="token parameter variable" style="color:rgb(189, 147, 249);font-style:italic">--release</span><br></span></code></pre><div class="buttonGroup__atx"><button type="button" aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" title="Copy" class="clean-btn"><span class="copyButtonIcons_eSgA" aria-hidden="true"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonIcon_y97N"><path fill="currentColor" d="M19,21H8V7H19M19,5H8A2,2 0 0,0 6,7V21A2,2 0 0,0 8,23H19A2,2 0 0,0 21,21V7A2,2 0 0,0 19,5M16,1H4A2,2 0 0,0 2,3V17H4V3H16V1Z"></path></svg><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonSuccessIcon_LjdS"><path fill="currentColor" d="M21,7L9,19L3.5,13.5L4.91,12.09L9,16.17L19.59,5.59L21,7Z"></path></svg></span></button></div></div></div>
<h2 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="5-real-proof-generation">5. Real Proof Generation<a href="#5-real-proof-generation" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to 5. Real Proof Generation" title="Direct link to 5. Real Proof Generation">​</a></h2>
<p>Once you&#x27;ve reached a point where you&#x27;re ready to generate real proofs, you can
do so by setting <code>RISC0_DEV_MODE=0</code>. Generating proofs locally would be achieved
by running the following:</p>
<div class="language-bash codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color:#F8F8F2;--prism-background-color:#282A36"><div class="codeBlockContent_biex"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-bash codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color:#F8F8F2;background-color:#282A36"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><span class="token-line" style="color:#F8F8F2"><span class="token assign-left variable" style="color:rgb(189, 147, 249);font-style:italic">RISC0_DEV_MODE</span><span class="token operator">=</span><span class="token number">0</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token function" style="color:rgb(80, 250, 123)">cargo</span><span class="token plain"> run </span><span class="token parameter variable" style="color:rgb(189, 147, 249);font-style:italic">--release</span><br></span></code></pre><div class="buttonGroup__atx"><button type="button" aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" title="Copy" class="clean-btn"><span class="copyButtonIcons_eSgA" aria-hidden="true"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonIcon_y97N"><path fill="currentColor" d="M19,21H8V7H19M19,5H8A2,2 0 0,0 6,7V21A2,2 0 0,0 8,23H19A2,2 0 0,0 21,21V7A2,2 0 0,0 19,5M16,1H4A2,2 0 0,0 2,3V17H4V3H16V1Z"></path></svg><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonSuccessIcon_LjdS"><path fill="currentColor" d="M21,7L9,19L3.5,13.5L4.91,12.09L9,16.17L19.59,5.59L21,7Z"></path></svg></span></button></div></div></div>
<p>Note that since proofs are now being generated, the execution time will be
significantly longer than when running in dev-mode. To create a proof with the
zkVM on your own machine, we recommend at least 16 GB of RAM. To avoid these
hardware requirements, consider using <a href="https://boundless.network" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Boundless</a> to generate proofs remotely, as
it will be significantly faster than running proofs locally.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorWithStickyNavbar_LWe7" id="executor-statistics">Executor Statistics<a href="#executor-statistics" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Executor Statistics" title="Direct link to Executor Statistics">​</a></h3>
<p>To gain insights into your application&#x27;s performance, you can obtain executor
statistics by setting the <code>RUST_LOG</code> environment variable to
<code>info</code>.</p>
<p>Setting this filter will print statistics about the execution before proof
generation, so you can understand how computationally expensive your application
is. Since the statistics concern only the executor phase, it is recommended to
run your application in dev-mode to avoid the overhead of proof generation:</p>
<div class="language-bash codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color:#F8F8F2;--prism-background-color:#282A36"><div class="codeBlockContent_biex"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-bash codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color:#F8F8F2;background-color:#282A36"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><span class="token-line" style="color:#F8F8F2"><span class="token assign-left variable" style="color:rgb(189, 147, 249);font-style:italic">RISC0_DEV_MODE</span><span class="token operator">=</span><span class="token number">1</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token assign-left variable" style="color:rgb(189, 147, 249);font-style:italic">RUST_LOG</span><span class="token operator">=</span><span class="token plain">info </span><span class="token assign-left variable" style="color:rgb(189, 147, 249);font-style:italic">RISC0_INFO</span><span class="token operator">=</span><span class="token number">1</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token function" style="color:rgb(80, 250, 123)">cargo</span><span class="token plain"> run </span><span class="token parameter variable" style="color:rgb(189, 147, 249);font-style:italic">--release</span><br></span></code></pre><div class="buttonGroup__atx"><button type="button" aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" title="Copy" class="clean-btn"><span class="copyButtonIcons_eSgA" aria-hidden="true"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonIcon_y97N"><path fill="currentColor" d="M19,21H8V7H19M19,5H8A2,2 0 0,0 6,7V21A2,2 0 0,0 8,23H19A2,2 0 0,0 21,21V7A2,2 0 0,0 19,5M16,1H4A2,2 0 0,0 2,3V17H4V3H16V1Z"></path></svg><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonSuccessIcon_LjdS"><path fill="currentColor" d="M21,7L9,19L3.5,13.5L4.91,12.09L9,16.17L19.59,5.59L21,7Z"></path></svg></span></button></div></div></div>
<p>The statistics include:</p>
<ul>
<li>Total Cycles</li>
<li>Session Cycle</li>
<li>Segments Count</li>
<li>Execution time</li>
</ul>
<p>Knowing these statistics is useful for estimating the cost of your application
before submitting real workloads to Boundless, as the cost of proof generation is
proportional to the number of cycles and segments used.</p>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Congratulations!</strong></p>
<p><em>That&#x27;s all it takes to build and run a minimal RISC Zero application.</em></p>
</blockquote></div><footer class="theme-doc-footer docusaurus-mt-lg"><div class="row margin-top--sm theme-doc-footer-edit-meta-row"><div class="col"><a href="https://github.com/risc0/risc0/edit/main/website/api/zkvm/quickstart.md" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="theme-edit-this-page"><svg fill="currentColor" height="20" width="20" viewBox="0 0 40 40" class="iconEdit_Z9Sw" aria-hidden="true"><g><path d="m34.5 11.7l-3 3.1-6.3-6.3 3.1-3q0.5-0.5 1.2-0.5t1.1 0.5l3.9 3.9q0.5 0.4 0.5 1.1t-0.5 1.2z m-29.5 17.1l18.4-18.5 6.3 6.3-18.4 18.4h-6.3v-6.2z"></path></g></svg>Edit this page</a></div><div class="col lastUpdated_JAkA"></div></div></footer></article><nav class="pagination-nav docusaurus-mt-lg" aria-label="Docs pages"><a class="pagination-nav__link pagination-nav__link--prev" href="/api/zkvm/install"><div class="pagination-nav__sublabel">Previous</div><div class="pagination-nav__label">Installation</div></a><a class="pagination-nav__link pagination-nav__link--next" href="/api/zkvm/tutorials/hello-world"><div class="pagination-nav__sublabel">Next</div><div class="pagination-nav__label">Hello World Tutorial</div></a></nav></div></div><div class="col col--3"><div class="tableOfContents_bqdL thin-scrollbar theme-doc-toc-desktop"><ul class="table-of-contents table-of-contents__left-border"><li><a href="#concept-break" class="table-of-contents__link toc-highlight">Concept break</a></li><li><a href="#1-install-the-risc-zero-toolchain" class="table-of-contents__link toc-highlight">1.
