    <div id="onboarding-modal" class="hidden fixed inset-0 z-50" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="onboarding-title">
        <div class="absolute inset-0 bg-black/70"></div>
        <div class="relative min-h-full flex items-center justify-center p-4">
            <div class="w-full max-w-3xl bg-dark-900 border border-dark-700 rounded-xl shadow-xl">
                <div class="px-5 py-4 border-b border-dark-700 flex items-center justify-between gap-3">
                    <h2 id="onboarding-title" class="text-lg font-semibold text-dark-100">Set up PoolStellar</h2>
                    <button id="onboarding-close-btn" type="button" class="p-1 text-dark-400 hover:text-dark-200 transition-colors" aria-label="Close onboarding">
                        <svg class="w-5 h-5" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"><line x1="18" y1="6" x2="6" y2="18"/><line x1="6" y1="6" x2="18" y2="18"/></svg>

                            <span class="w-5 h-5 rounded-full border border-current flex items-center justify-center text-[10px] font-semibold">3</span>

                    <p id="onboarding-error" class="hidden mt-4 text-sm text-red-400"></p>

<iframe src="/-/storage-change-detection.html" width="0" height="0" style="display: none"></iframe><a class="skip-main-content" href="#main-content">Skip to main content</a><!--[if lte IE 11]><div class="warning">This old browser is unsupported and will most likely display funky things.</div><![endif]--><rustdoc-topbar><h2><a href="#">Module v25_bn254</a></h2></rustdoc-topbar><nav class="sidebar"><div class="sidebar-crate"><h2><a href="../../../soroban_sdk/index.html">soroban_<wbr>sdk</a><span class="version">26.1.0</span></h2></div><div class="sidebar-elems"><section id="rustdoc-toc"><h2 class="location"><a href="#">Module v25_<wbr>bn254</a></h2><h3><a href="#">Sections</a></h3><ul class="block top-toc"><li><a href="#new-types" title="New Types">New Types</a></li><li><a href="#new-operations" title="New Operations">New Operations</a></li><li><a href="#example-basic-g1-operations" title="Example: Basic G1 Operations">Example: Basic G1 Operations</a></li><li><a href="#example-point-negation" title="Example: Point Negation">Example: Point Negation</a></li><li><a href="#example-pairing-check" title="Example: Pairing Check">Example: Pairing Check</a></li></ul></section><div id="rustdoc-modnav"><h2><a href="../index.html">In soroban_<wbr>sdk::<wbr>_<wbr>migrating</a></h2></div></div></nav><div class="sidebar-resizer" title="Drag to resize sidebar"></div><main><div class="width-limiter"><section id="main-content" class="content" tabindex="-1"><div class="main-heading"><div class="rustdoc-breadcrumbs"><a href="../../index.html">soroban_sdk</a>::<wbr><a href="../index.html">_migrating</a></div><h1>Module <span>v25_<wbr>bn254</span>&nbsp;<button id="copy-path" title="Copy item path to clipboard">Copy item path</button></h1><rustdoc-toolbar></rustdoc-toolbar><span class="sub-heading"><a class="src" href="../../../src/soroban_sdk/_migrating/v25_bn254.rs.html#1-138">Source</a> </span></div><details class="toggle top-doc" open><summary class="hideme"><span>Expand description</span></summary><div class="docblock"><p>BN254 (alt_bn128) elliptic curve support.</p>
<p>Support for BN254 (also known as alt_bn128), a pairing-friendly elliptic curve commonly used in
zero-knowledge proof systems.</p>
<p>The BN254 functionality is accessed via <code>env.crypto().bn254()</code>.</p>
<h3 id="new-types"><a class="doc-anchor" href="#new-types">§</a>New Types</h3>
<ul>
<li><a href="../../crypto/bn254/struct.Bn254G1Affine.html" title="struct soroban_sdk::crypto::bn254::Bn254G1Affine"><code>Bn254G1Affine</code></a> - A point in the G1 group (64 bytes, Ethereum-compatible format)</li>
<li><a href="../../crypto/bn254/struct.Bn254G2Affine.html" title="struct soroban_sdk::crypto::bn254::Bn254G2Affine"><code>Bn254G2Affine</code></a> - A point in the G2 group (128 bytes, Ethereum-compatible format)</li>
<li><a href="../../crypto/bn254/struct.Bn254Fr.html" title="struct soroban_sdk::crypto::bn254::Bn254Fr"><code>Bn254Fr</code></a> - A scalar field element (32 bytes, internally a <code>U256</code>)</li>
<li><a href="../../crypto/bn254/struct.Bn254Fp.html" title="struct soroban_sdk::crypto::bn254::Bn254Fp"><code>Bn254Fp</code></a> - A base field element (32 bytes)</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="new-operations"><a class="doc-anchor" href="#new-operations">§</a>New Operations</h3>
<ul>
<li><code>g1_add</code> - Add two G1 points</li>
<li><code>g1_mul</code> - Multiply a G1 point by a scalar</li>
<li><code>pairing_check</code> - Multi-pairing check for ZK proof verification</li>
</ul>
<p>G1 points also support arithmetic operations via Rust traits:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>Add</code> - Add two G1 points</li>
<li><code>Mul&lt;Bn254Fr&gt;</code> - Multiply a G1 point by a scalar</li>
<li><code>Neg</code> - Negate a G1 point</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="example-basic-g1-operations"><a class="doc-anchor" href="#example-basic-g1-operations">§</a>Example: Basic G1 Operations</h3>
<div class="example-wrap"><pre class="rust rust-example-rendered"><code><span class="kw">use </span>soroban_sdk::{Env, BytesN, U256};
<span class="kw">use </span>soroban_sdk::crypto::bn254::{Bn254Fr, Bn254G1Affine};

<span class="kw">let </span>env = Env::default();
<span class="kw">let </span>bn254 = env.crypto().bn254();

<span class="comment">// The generator point G1 for BN254
// G1 = (1, 2)
</span><span class="kw">let </span>g1_bytes: [u8; <span class="number">64</span>] = [
    <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>,
    <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">1</span>,  <span class="comment">// x = 1
    </span><span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>,
    <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">2</span>,  <span class="comment">// y = 2
</span>];
<span class="kw">let </span>g1 = Bn254G1Affine::from_array(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>g1_bytes);

<span class="comment">// Add the generator to itself: G + G = 2G
</span><span class="kw">let </span>g1_doubled = bn254.g1_add(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>g1, <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>g1);

<span class="comment">// Or use the Add trait
</span><span class="kw">let </span>g1_doubled_alt = g1.clone() + g1.clone();
<span class="macro">assert_eq!</span>(g1_doubled, g1_doubled_alt);

<span class="comment">// Scalar multiplication: 2 * G should equal G + G
</span><span class="kw">let </span>scalar: Bn254Fr = U256::from_u32(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="number">2</span>).into();
<span class="kw">let </span>g1_times_2 = bn254.g1_mul(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>g1, <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>scalar);
<span class="macro">assert_eq!</span>(g1_doubled, g1_times_2);

<span class="comment">// Or use the Mul trait
</span><span class="kw">let </span>g1_times_2_alt = g1.clone() * scalar;
<span class="macro">assert_eq!</span>(g1_doubled, g1_times_2_alt);</code></pre></div><h3 id="example-point-negation"><a class="doc-anchor" href="#example-point-negation">§</a>Example: Point Negation</h3>
<div class="example-wrap"><pre class="rust rust-example-rendered"><code><span class="kw">use </span>soroban_sdk::{Env, BytesN};
<span class="kw">use </span>soroban_sdk::crypto::bn254::Bn254G1Affine;

<span class="kw">let </span>env = Env::default();
<span class="kw">let </span>bn254 = env.crypto().bn254();

<span class="comment">// G1 generator point (1, 2)
</span><span class="kw">let </span>g1_bytes: [u8; <span class="number">64</span>] = [
    <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>,
    <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">1</span>,
    <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>,
    <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">0</span>, <span class="number">2</span>,
];
<span class="kw">let </span>g1 = Bn254G1Affine::from_array(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>g1_bytes);

<span class="comment">// Negate the point: -G has same x but negated y
</span><span class="kw">let </span>neg_g1 = -g1.clone();

<span class="comment">// G + (-G) = point at infinity (all zeros)
</span><span class="kw">let </span>sum = bn254.g1_add(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>g1, <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>neg_g1);
<span class="kw">let </span>infinity = Bn254G1Affine::from_array(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>[<span class="number">0u8</span>; <span class="number">64</span>]);
<span class="macro">assert_eq!</span>(sum, infinity);</code></pre></div><h3 id="example-pairing-check"><a class="doc-anchor" href="#example-pairing-check">§</a>Example: Pairing Check</h3>
<div class="example-wrap"><pre class="rust rust-example-rendered"><code><span class="kw">use </span>soroban_sdk::{Env, vec, Vec};
<span class="kw">use </span>soroban_sdk::crypto::bn254::{Bn254G1Affine, Bn254G2Affine};

<span class="kw">let </span>env = Env::default();
<span class="kw">let </span>bn254 = env.crypto().bn254();

<span class="comment">// Example G1 and G2 points from Ethereum test vectors - https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/blob/master/core/vm/testdata/precompiles/bn256Pairing.json
</span><span class="kw">let </span>g1_bytes: [u8; <span class="number">64</span>] = [
    <span class="number">0x1c</span>, <span class="number">0x76</span>, <span class="number">0x47</span>, <span class="number">0x6f</span>, <span class="number">0x4d</span>, <span class="number">0xef</span>, <span class="number">0x4b</span>, <span class="number">0xb9</span>, <span class="number">0x45</span>, <span class="number">0x41</span>, <span class="number">0xd5</span>, <span class="number">0x7e</span>, <span class="number">0xbb</span>, <span class="number">0xa1</span>, <span class="number">0x19</span>, <span class="number">0x33</span>,
    <span class="number">0x81</span>, <span class="number">0xff</span>, <span class="number">0xa7</span>, <span class="number">0xaa</span>, <span class="number">0x76</span>, <span class="number">0xad</span>, <span class="number">0xa6</span>, <span class="number">0x64</span>, <span class="number">0xdd</span>, <span class="number">0x31</span>, <span class="number">0xc1</span>, <span class="number">0x60</span>, <span class="number">0x24</span>, <span class="number">0xc4</span>, <span class="number">0x3f</span>, <span class="number">0x59</span>,
    <span class="number">0x30</span>, <span class="number">0x34</span>, <span class="number">0xdd</span>, <span class="number">0x29</span>, <span class="number">0x20</span>, <span class="number">0xf6</span>, <span class="number">0x73</span>, <span class="number">0xe2</span>, <span class="number">0x04</span>, <span class="number">0xfe</span>, <span class="number">0xe2</span>, <span class="number">0x81</span>, <span class="number">0x1c</span>, <span class="number">0x67</span>, <span class="number">0x87</span>, <span class="number">0x45</span>,
    <span class="number">0xfc</span>, <span class="number">0x81</span>, <span class="number">0x9b</span>, <span class="number">0x55</span>, <span class="number">0xd3</span>, <span class="number">0xe9</span>, <span class="number">0xd2</span>, <span class="number">0x94</span>, <span class="number">0xe4</span>, <span class="number">0x5c</span>, <span class="number">0x9b</span>, <span class="number">0x03</span>, <span class="number">0xa7</span>, <span class="number">0x6a</span>, <span class="number">0xef</span>, <span class="number">0x41</span>,
];
<span class="kw">let </span>g1 = Bn254G1Affine::from_array(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>g1_bytes);

<span class="kw">let </span>g2_bytes: [u8; <span class="number">128</span>] = [
    <span class="number">0x20</span>, <span class="number">0x9d</span>, <span class="number">0xd1</span>, <span class="number">0x5e</span>, <span class="number">0xbf</span>, <span class="number">0xf5</span>, <span class="number">0xd4</span>, <span class="number">0x6c</span>, <span class="number">0x4b</span>, <span class="number">0xd8</span>, <span class="number">0x88</span>, <span class="number">0xe5</span>, <span class="number">0x1a</span>, <span class="number">0x93</span>, <span class="number">0xcf</span>, <span class="number">0x99</span>,
    <span class="number">0xa7</span>, <span class="number">0x32</span>, <span class="number">0x96</span>, <span class="number">0x36</span>, <span class="number">0xc6</span>, <span class="number">0x35</span>, <span class="number">0x14</span>, <span class="number">0x39</span>, <span class="number">0x6b</span>, <span class="number">0x4a</span>, <span class="number">0x45</span>, <span class="number">0x20</span>, <span class="number">0x03</span>, <span class="number">0xa3</span>, <span class="number">0x5b</span>, <span class="number">0xf7</span>,
    <span class="number">0x04</span>, <span class="number">0xbf</span>, <span class="number">0x11</span>, <span class="number">0xca</span>, <span class="number">0x01</span>, <span class="number">0x48</span>, <span class="number">0x3b</span>, <span class="number">0xfa</span>, <span class="number">0x8b</span>, <span class="number">0x34</span>, <span class="number">0xb4</span>, <span class="number">0x35</span>, <span class="number">0x61</span>, <span class="number">0x84</span>, <span class="number">0x8d</span>, <span class="number">0x28</span>,
    <span class="number">0x90</span>, <span class="number">0x59</span>, <span class="number">0x60</span>, <span class="number">0x11</span>, <span class="number">0x4c</span>, <span class="number">0x8a</span>, <span class="number">0xc0</span>, <span class="number">0x40</span>, <span class="number">0x49</span>, <span class="number">0xaf</span>, <span class="number">0x4b</span>, <span class="number">0x63</span>, <span class="number">0x15</span>, <span class="number">0xa4</span>, <span class="number">0x16</span>, <span class="number">0x78</span>,
    <span class="number">0x2b</span>, <span class="number">0xb8</span>, <span class="number">0x32</span>, <span class="number">0x4a</span>, <span class="number">0xf6</span>, <span class="number">0xcf</span>, <span class="number">0xc9</span>, <span class="number">0x35</span>, <span class="number">0x37</span>, <span class="number">0xa2</span>, <span class="number">0xad</span>, <span class="number">0x1a</span>, <span class="number">0x44</span>, <span class="number">0x5c</span>, <span class="number">0xfd</span>, <span class="number">0x0c</span>,
    <span class="number">0xa2</span>, <span class="number">0xa7</span>, <span class="number">0x1a</span>, <span class="number">0xcd</span>, <span class="number">0x7a</span>, <span class="number">0xc4</span>, <span class="number">0x1f</span>, <span class="number">0xad</span>, <span class="number">0xbf</span>, <span class="number">0x93</span>, <span class="number">0x3c</span>, <span class="number">0x2a</span>, <span class="number">0x51</span>, <span class="number">0xbe</span>, <span class="number">0x34</span>, <span class="number">0x4d</span>,
    <span class="number">0x12</span>, <span class="number">0x0a</span>, <span class="number">0x2a</span>, <span class="number">0x4c</span>, <span class="number">0xf3</span>, <span class="number">0x0c</span>, <span class="number">0x1b</span>, <span class="number">0xf9</span>, <span class="number">0x84</span>, <span class="number">0x5f</span>, <span class="number">0x20</span>, <span class="number">0xc6</span>, <span class="number">0xfe</span>, <span class="number">0x39</span>, <span class="number">0xe0</span>, <span class="number">0x7e</span>,
    <span class="number">0xa2</span>, <span class="number">0xcc</span>, <span class="number">0xe6</span>, <span class="number">0x1f</span>, <span class="number">0x0c</span>, <span class="number">0x9b</span>, <span class="number">0xb0</span>, <span class="number">0x48</span>, <span class="number">0x16</span>, <span class="number">0x5f</span>, <span class="number">0xe5</span>, <span class="number">0xe4</span>, <span class="number">0xde</span>, <span class="number">0x87</span>, <span class="number">0x75</span>, <span class="number">0x50</span>,
];
<span class="kw">let </span>g2 = Bn254G2Affine::from_array(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>g2_bytes);

<span class="comment">// Create vectors of G1 and G2 points
</span><span class="kw">let </span>g1_vec: Vec&lt;Bn254G1Affine&gt; = <span class="macro">vec!</span>[<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, g1];
<span class="kw">let </span>g2_vec: Vec&lt;Bn254G2Affine&gt; = <span class="macro">vec!</span>[<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, g2];

<span class="comment">// Perform pairing check
// Returns true if e(G1[0], G2[0]) * e(G1[1], G2[1]) * ... = 1
</span><span class="kw">let </span>result = bn254.pairing_check(g1_vec, g2_vec);
<span class="comment">// result will be true or false depending on the pairing equation</span></code></pre></div></div></details></section></div></main></div></body></html>

</div><div class="rustdoc mod container-rustdoc" id="rustdoc_body_wrapper" tabindex="-1"><script async src="/-/static/menu.js?0-0-0-886bc7a22b1adc4b387ce1343447fe3af98d9ce5-2026-06-14"></script>

<iframe src="/-/storage-change-detection.html" width="0" height="0" style="display: none"></iframe><a class="skip-main-content" href="#main-content">Skip to main content</a><!--[if lte IE 11]><div class="warning">This old browser is unsupported and will most likely display funky things.</div><![endif]--><rustdoc-topbar><h2><a href="#">Module v25_poseidon</a></h2></rustdoc-topbar><nav class="sidebar"><div class="sidebar-crate"><h2><a href="../../../soroban_sdk/index.html">soroban_<wbr>sdk</a><span class="version">26.1.0</span></h2></div><div class="sidebar-elems"><section id="rustdoc-toc"><h2 class="location"><a href="#">Module v25_<wbr>poseidon</a></h2><h3><a href="#">Sections</a></h3><ul class="block top-toc"><li><a href="#-hazardous-materials-warning" title="⚠️ Hazardous Materials Warning">⚠️ Hazardous Materials Warning</a></li><li><a href="#enabling-the-feature" title="Enabling the Feature">Enabling the Feature</a></li><li><a href="#poseidon-permutation" title="Poseidon Permutation">Poseidon Permutation</a></li><li><a href="#poseidon2-permutation" title="Poseidon2 Permutation">Poseidon2 Permutation</a></li><li><a href="#example" title="Example">Example</a></li></ul></section><div id="rustdoc-modnav"><h2><a href="../index.html">In soroban_<wbr>sdk::<wbr>_<wbr>migrating</a></h2></div></div></nav><div class="sidebar-resizer" title="Drag to resize sidebar"></div><main><div class="width-limiter"><section id="main-content" class="content" tabindex="-1"><div class="main-heading"><div class="rustdoc-breadcrumbs"><a href="../../index.html">soroban_sdk</a>::<wbr><a href="../index.html">_migrating</a></div><h1>Module <span>v25_<wbr>poseidon</span>&nbsp;<button id="copy-path" title="Copy item path to clipboard">Copy item path</button></h1><rustdoc-toolbar></rustdoc-toolbar><span class="sub-heading"><a class="src" href="../../../src/soroban_sdk/_migrating/v25_poseidon.rs.html#1-100">Source</a> </span></div><details class="toggle top-doc" open><summary class="hideme"><span>Expand description</span></summary><div class="docblock"><p>Poseidon and Poseidon2 permutation functions.</p>
<p>Protocol 25 exposes low-level Poseidon and Poseidon2 permutation host functions
for advanced cryptographic use cases. These are available through the <code>CryptoHazmat</code>
interface under the <code>hazmat-crypto</code> feature.</p>
<p>Higher level non-hazmat functionality will be released in a separate crate.</p>
<h3 id="-hazardous-materials-warning"><a class="doc-anchor" href="#-hazardous-materials-warning">§</a>⚠️ Hazardous Materials Warning</h3>
<p>These are low-level cryptographic primitives. Most users should use higher-level
constructions built on top of these permutations. Incorrect usage can lead to
security vulnerabilities.</p>
<h3 id="enabling-the-feature"><a class="doc-anchor" href="#enabling-the-feature">§</a>Enabling the Feature</h3>
<p>Add the <code>hazmat-crypto</code> feature to your <code>Cargo.toml</code>:</p>
<div class="example-wrap"><pre class="language-toml"><code>[dependencies]
soroban-sdk = { version = &quot;25&quot;, features = [&quot;hazmat-crypto&quot;] }</code></pre></div><h3 id="poseidon-permutation"><a class="doc-anchor" href="#poseidon-permutation">§</a>Poseidon Permutation</h3>
<p>The <code>poseidon_permutation</code> function performs the standard Poseidon permutation
with a full MDS matrix.</p>
<p>Parameters:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>input</code> - State vector of <code>U256</code> field elements</li>
<li><code>field</code> - Field identifier (e.g., <code>"BN254"</code>)</li>
<li><code>t</code> - State width (number of elements)</li>
<li><code>d</code> - S-box exponent (typically 5)</li>
<li><code>rounds_f</code> - Number of full rounds (must be even)</li>
<li><code>rounds_p</code> - Number of partial rounds</li>
<li><code>mds</code> - MDS matrix (<code>t × t</code> matrix of <code>U256</code> elements)</li>
<li><code>round_constants</code> - Round constants (one vector per round)</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="poseidon2-permutation"><a class="doc-anchor" href="#poseidon2-permutation">§</a>Poseidon2 Permutation</h3>
<p>The <code>poseidon2_permutation</code> function performs the Poseidon2 permutation with an
optimized internal matrix representation.</p>
<p>Parameters:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>input</code> - State vector of <code>U256</code> field elements</li>
<li><code>field</code> - Field identifier (e.g., <code>"BN254"</code>)</li>
<li><code>t</code> - State width (number of elements)</li>
<li><code>d</code> - S-box exponent (typically 5)</li>
<li><code>rounds_f</code> - Number of full rounds (must be even)</li>
<li><code>rounds_p</code> - Number of partial rounds</li>
<li><code>mat_internal_diag_m_1</code> - Diagonal of internal matrix minus identity</li>
<li><code>round_constants</code> - Round constants (one vector per round)</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="example"><a class="doc-anchor" href="#example">§</a>Example</h3>
<div class="example-wrap ignore"><a href="#" class="tooltip" title="This example is not tested">ⓘ</a><pre class="rust rust-example-rendered"><code><span class="kw">use </span>soroban_sdk::{bytesn, vec, Env, Symbol, U256};
<span class="kw">use </span>soroban_sdk::crypto::CryptoHazmat;

<span class="kw">let </span>env = Env::default();

<span class="comment">// Define MDS matrix (2x2 for t=2)
</span><span class="kw">let </span>mds = <span class="macro">vec!</span>[
    <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env,
    <span class="macro">vec!</span>[<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env,
        U256::from_be_bytes(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span><span class="macro">bytesn!</span>(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="number">0x066f6f85d6f68a85ec10345351a23a3aaf07f38af8c952a7bceca70bd2af7ad5</span>).into()),
        U256::from_be_bytes(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span><span class="macro">bytesn!</span>(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="number">0x2b9d4b4110c9ae997782e1509b1d0fdb20a7c02bbd8bea7305462b9f8125b1e8</span>).into()),
    ],
    <span class="macro">vec!</span>[<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env,
        U256::from_be_bytes(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span><span class="macro">bytesn!</span>(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="number">0x0cc57cdbb08507d62bf67a4493cc262fb6c09d557013fff1f573f431221f8ff9</span>).into()),
        U256::from_be_bytes(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span><span class="macro">bytesn!</span>(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="number">0x1274e649a32ed355a31a6ed69724e1adade857e86eb5c3a121bcd147943203c8</span>).into()),
    ],
];

<span class="comment">// Define round constants
</span><span class="kw">let </span>rc = <span class="macro">vec!</span>[
    <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env,
    <span class="macro">vec!</span>[<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, U256::from_u32(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="number">1</span>), U256::from_u32(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="number">2</span>)],
    <span class="macro">vec!</span>[<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, U256::from_u32(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="number">3</span>), U256::from_u32(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="number">4</span>)],
    <span class="macro">vec!</span>[<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, U256::from_u32(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="number">5</span>), U256::from_u32(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="number">6</span>)],
];

<span class="kw">let </span>input = <span class="macro">vec!</span>[<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, U256::from_u32(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="number">0</span>), U256::from_u32(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="number">1</span>)];

<span class="kw">let </span>hazmat = CryptoHazmat::new(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env);
<span class="kw">let </span>result = hazmat.poseidon_permutation(
    <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>input,
    Symbol::new(<span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>env, <span class="string">"BN254"</span>),
    <span class="number">2</span>,  <span class="comment">// t: state width
    </span><span class="number">5</span>,  <span class="comment">// d: s-box exponent
    </span><span class="number">2</span>,  <span class="comment">// rounds_f: full rounds
    </span><span class="number">1</span>,  <span class="comment">// rounds_p: partial rounds
    </span><span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>mds,
    <span class="kw-2">&amp;</span>rc,
);

<span class="macro">assert_eq!</span>(result.len(), <span class="number">2</span>);</code></pre></div></div></details></section></div></main></div></body></html>

class="breadcrumbs__item breadcrumbs__item--active"><span class="breadcrumbs__link">Stellar CLI</span></li></ul></nav><div class="theme-doc-markdown markdown"><div class="row"><div class="col col--12 markdown"><header><h1>Stellar CLI</h1></header>
<p>The command line interface to Soroban smart contracts. It allows you to build, deploy, and interact with smart contracts; configure identities; generate key pairs; manage networks; and more.</p>
<p>Install Stellar CLI as explained in <a class="" href="/docs/build/smart-contracts/getting-started/setup#install-the-stellar-cli">Setup</a>. For examples on how to use the Stellar CLI, please see <a class="" href="/docs/tools/cli/cookbook">Stellar CLI Guides</a>.</p>
<p>The auto-generated comprehensive reference documentation is available <a class="" href="/docs/tools/cli/stellar-cli">here</a>.</p></div></div></div><div class="row"><div class="col col--12"><footer class="theme-doc-footer docusaurus-mt-lg"><div class="row margin-top--sm theme-doc-footer-edit-meta-row"><div class="col noPrint_vwug"><a menu__list-item"><a class="menu__link" href="/docs/platforms/stellar-disbursement-platform"><span title="Stellar Disbursement Platform" class="linkLabel_ls5s">Stellar Disbursement Platform</span></a></li></ul></nav></div></div></aside><main class="docMainContainer_ZZ3i"><div class="container padding-top--md padding-bottom--lg"><div class="row"><div class="col docItemCol_lRRE"><div class="docItemContainer_O0yx"><article><nav class="theme-doc-breadcrumbs breadcrumbsContainer_Kc6E" aria-label="Breadcrumbs"><ul class="breadcrumbs"><li class="breadcrumbs__item"><a aria-label="Home page" class="breadcrumbs__link" href="/"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="breadcrumbHomeIcon_SeIs"><path d="M10 19v-5h4v5c0 .55.45 1 1 1h3c.55 0 1-.45 1-1v-7h1.7c.46 0 .68-.57.33-.87L12.67 3.6c-.38-.34-.96-.34-1.34 0l-8.36 7.53c-.34.3-.13.87.33.87H5v7c0 .55.45 1 1 1h3c.55 0 1-.45 1-1z" fill="currentColor"></path></svg></a></li><li class="breadcrumbs__item breadcrumbs__item--active"><span class="breadcrumbs__link">Lab</span></li></ul></nav><div class="tocCollapsible_lP1m theme-doc-toc-mobile tocMobile_k7Dh"><button type="button" class="clean-btn tocCollapsibleButton_fC7B">On this page</button></div><div class="theme-doc-markdown markdown"><div class="row"><div class="col col--12 markdown"><header><h1>Lab</h1></header>
<h3 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_GmKl" id="stellar-lab"><a href="https://lab.stellar.org" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Stellar Lab</a><a href="#stellar-lab" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to stellar-lab" title="Direct link to stellar-lab" translate="no">​</a></h3>
<p>Stellar Lab is our go-to tool for development, experimenting, and testing, as well as <a href="https://lab.stellar.org/endpoints" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">exploring APIs</a> developers use to interact with the Stellar network. Whether you&#x27;re a developer seeking to <a href="https://lab.stellar.org/transaction/build" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">test transactions</a>, <a href="https://lab.stellar.org/smart-contracts/deploy-contract" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">deploy</a> and <a href="https://lab.stellar.org/smart-contracts/contract-explorer" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">invoke smart contracts</a>, or explore RPC methods or Horizon endpoints, Stellar Lab provides a modern and user-friendly interface that makes the process smooth and intuitive.</p>
<p><img decoding="async" loading="lazy" alt="Lab: Homepage" src="/assets/images/lab-01232026-efa1620520d6b6f141a436387845896f.png" width="1500" height="934" class="img_ARY8"></p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_GmKl" id="features-of-stellar-lab">Features of Stellar Lab<a href="#features-of-stellar-lab" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Features of Stellar Lab" title="Direct link to Features of Stellar Lab" translate="no">​</a></h3>
<ul>
<li class="">
<p><strong><a href="https://lab.stellar.org/account/create" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Easily Create Accounts</a></strong>: Create Accounts on Mainnet, Testnet, and Futurenet using a web UI. You can use <a href="https://lab.stellar.org/account/fund?$=network$id=testnet&amp;label=Testnet&amp;horizonUrl=https:////horizon-testnet.stellar.org&amp;passphrase=Test%20SDF%20Network%20/;%20September%202015;;" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Friendbot</a> to fund those accounts directly on Lab for Testnet and Futurenet. You can also add a trustline to USDC and EURC on Testnet.</p>
</li>
<li class="">
<p><strong><a href="https://lab.stellar.org/smart-contracts/deploy-contract" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Smart Contract Deployment</a></strong>: Deploy smart contracts directly from the Lab interface without needing command-line tools. Upload your contract WASM files and deploy them to the network with a user-friendly workflow.</p>
</li>
<li class="">
<p><strong><a href="https://lab.stellar.org/smart-contracts/contract-explorer" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Invoke Smart Contracts</a></strong>: Interact with deployed smart contracts through an intuitive interface. Select contract functions, fill in parameters with type hints, simulate transactions, and invoke contract methods — all from your browser.</p>
</li>
<li class="">
<p><strong><a href="https://lab.stellar.org/transaction-dashboard" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Transaction Dashboard</a></strong>: View comprehensive transaction details including XDR, operations, results, and metadata. Explore transaction history, examine individual operations, and debug transaction failures with detailed error information.</p>
</li>
<li class="">
<p><strong><a href="https://lab.stellar.org/endpoints" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Access RPC Methods and Horizon Endpoints</a></strong>: Leverage powerful Stellar RPC methods and Stellar Horizon endpoints in a web UI to interact with the Stellar network and obtain crucial data. Try RPC methods to get states from the ledger like accounts, trustlines, contract WASM, and more.</p>
</li>
<li class="">
<p><strong><a href="https://lab.stellar.org/transaction/simulate" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Simulate</a> and <a href="https://lab.stellar.org/transaction/submit" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Submit</a></strong>: Lab supports the ability to use custom RPC providers so that you can simulate transactions, save transactions, and submit transactions directly using Lab. You can also submit transactions using Horizon.</p>
</li>
<li class="">
<p><strong><a href="https://lab.stellar.org/endpoints/saved" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Save and Share API Requests</a></strong>: Easily save your requests and transactions for the future or share them with teammates to build faster together.</p>
</li>
<li class="">
<p><strong><a href="https://lab.stellar.org/xdr/view" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">XDR ⇔ JSON Support</a></strong>: We have introduced a canonical <a href="https://www.npmjs.com/package/@stellar/stellar-xdr-json-web" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">XDR to JSON mapping</a> which is used on Stellar Lab (also used in the <a href="https://github.com/stellar/stellar-cli" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Stellar CLI</a>). You can see this at work in the Lab where <a href="https://lab.stellar.org/xdr/view" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">XDR can be converted to JSON</a>.</p>
</li>
<li class="">
<p><strong>Mobile Friendly</strong>: We heard that you use Lab on mobile, so we optimized the Lab to have a mobile responsive layout.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_GmKl" id="video-tutorial-for-stellar-lab">Video Tutorial for Stellar Lab<a href="#video-tutorial-for-stellar-lab" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Video Tutorial for Stellar Lab" title="Direct link to Video Tutorial for Stellar Lab" translate="no">​</a></h3>
<ul>
<li class="">Video Tutorials for Lab are available on <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sv2gV13q7FI&amp;list=PLj3KReXFn3x73pPlgSun-2FYTrP4tZVPL" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Lumen Loop&#x27;s YouTube Channel</a></li>
</ul>
<h3 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_GmKl" id="help-us-improve">Help Us Improve!<a href="#help-us-improve" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Help Us Improve!" title="Direct link to Help Us Improve!" translate="no">​</a></h3>
<p>We’re committed to making Stellar Lab even better. If you have any feature requests, please submit them on <a href="https://github.com/stellar/laboratory/issues" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Github</a>. Your feedback is important to us with product iterations and in shaping the future of Stellar Lab.</p></div></div></div><div class="row"><div class="col col--12"><footer class="theme-doc-footer docusaurus-mt-lg"><div class="row margin-top--sm theme-doc-footer-edit-meta-row"><div class="col noPrint_vwug"><a href="https://github.com/stellar/stellar-docs/edit/main/docs/tools/lab/README.mdx" markdown"><div class="row"><div class="col col--12 markdown"><header><h1>Quickstart</h1></header>
<p>Quickstart is a local Stellar network environment (node) that allows developers to run a local version of the Stellar network for development and testing. Quickstart runs a local version of Stellar Core, Horizon, RPC, and everything else needed to replicate the public network.</p>
<p>For more information about running Stellar services in production, see the documentation for the individual services here:</p>
<ul>
<li class=""><a class="" href="/docs/validators">How to run Stellar Core in production</a></li>
<li class=""><a class="" href="/docs/data/apis/horizon/admin-guide/overview">How to run Horizon in production</a></li>
<li class=""><a class="" href="/docs/data/apis/rpc/admin-guide">How to run RPC in production</a></li>
</ul>
<p>Quickstart is intended for use in development, not in production, although running it in public mode will cause it to join the public network.</p>
<p><strong>Run Quickstart</strong></p>
<p>Ready to get started? The Getting Started section of the Quickstart documentation will show how to run Quickstart, but there are some configurations to decide on first. Most importantly, the network mode Quickstart will run in (pubnet, testnet, futurenet, local). Read about the modes in the Network Modes section.</p>
<div class="theme-admonition theme-admonition-info admonition_seM4 alert alert--info"><div class="admonitionHeading_vjpE"><span class="admonitionIcon_kMma"><svg viewBox="0 0 14 16"><path fill-rule="evenodd" d="M7 2.3c3.14 0 5.7 2.56 5.7 5.7s-2.56 5.7-5.7 5.7A5.71 5.71 0 0 1 1.3 8c0-3.14 2.56-5.7 5.7-5.7zM7 1C3.14 1 0 4.14 0 8s3.14 7 7 7 7-3.14 7-7-3.14-7-7-7zm1 3H6v5h2V4zm0 6H6v2h2v-2z"></path></svg></span>info</div><div class="admonitionContent_kvCb"><p>Quickstart can be deployed using the Getting Started guide, but the configurations used in the guide may not be ideal for your use case, so please familiarize yourself with the options to configure Quickstart to fit your use case.</p></div></div>
<div class="theme-admonition theme-admonition-caution admonition_seM4 alert alert--warning"><div class="admonitionHeading_vjpE"><span class="admonitionIcon_kMma"><svg viewBox="0 0 16 16"><path fill-rule="evenodd" d="M8.893 1.5c-.183-.31-.52-.5-.887-.5s-.703.19-.886.5L.138 13.499a.98.98 0 0 0 0 1.001c.193.31.53.501.886.501h13.964c.367 0 .704-.19.877-.5a1.03 1.03 0 0 0 .01-1.002L8.893 1.5zm.133 11.497H6.987v-2.003h2.039v2.003zm0-3.004H6.987V5.987h2.039v4.006z"></path></svg></span>caution</div><div class="admonitionContent_kvCb"><p>Please note: the quickstart image is not intended for production purposes. Information about setting up a production environment is available <a class="" href="/docs/validators">here</a>.</p></div></div></div></div></div><div class="row"><div class="col col--12"><footer markdown"><header><h1>SDKs</h1></header>
<!-- -->
<p>The SDK section is split into three categories:</p>
<ul>
<li class=""><a class="" href="/docs/tools/sdks/contract-sdks">Contract SDKs</a> that are used to build smart contracts that will be deployed to the Stellar network;</li>
<li class=""><a class="" href="/docs/tools/sdks/client-sdks">Client &amp; XDR SDKs</a> that are used by applications to interact with the network;</li>
<li class=""><a class="" href="/docs/tools/sdks/build-your-own">Build your own SDK</a> that details the minimum requirements for building your own SDK.</li>
</ul>
<section class="row"><article class="docCardListItem_YRRD col col--6"><a class="card padding--lg cardContainer_HfW5" href="/docs/tools/sdks/contract-sdks"><h2 class="text--truncate cardTitle_zXrx" title="Contract SDKs">📄️<!-- --> <!-- -->Contract SDKs</h2><p class="text--truncate cardDescription_cpcC" title="Contract SDKs are used to build smart contracts that will be deployed to the Stellar network.">Contract SDKs are used to build smart contracts that will be deployed to the Stellar network.</p></a></article><article class="docCardListItem_YRRD col col--6"><a class="card padding--lg cardContainer_HfW5" href="/docs/tools/sdks/client-sdks"><h2 class="text--truncate cardTitle_zXrx" title="Client &amp; XDR SDKs">📄️<!-- --> <!-- -->Client &amp; XDR SDKs</h2><p class="text--truncate cardDescription_cpcC" title="Explore the Stellar SDK library to simplify blockchain development. Leverage Rust, JavaScript, Python, and more to build on and integrate with the network.">Explore the Stellar SDK library to simplify blockchain development. Leverage Rust, JavaScript, Python, and more to build on and integrate with the network.</p></a></article><article class="docCardListItem_YRRD col col--6"><a class="card padding--lg cardContainer_HfW5" href="/docs/tools/sdks/build-your-own"><h2 class="text--truncate cardTitle_zXrx" title="Build Your Own Contract SDK">📄️<!-- --> <!-- -->Build Your Own Contract SDK</h2><p class="text--truncate cardDescription_cpcC" title="This is for building an SDK for writing smart contracts.">This is for building an SDK for writing smart contracts.</p></a></article></section></div></div></div><div .hs-form .form-columns-1,.hs-form .form-columns-2,.hs-form .form-columns-3{display:-webkit-box;display:-webkit-flex;display:-ms-flexbox;display:flex;gap:1.5rem;}/*!sc*/

@media screen and (min-width: 0px){.hs-form .form-columns-1,.hs-form .form-columns-2,.hs-form .form-columns-3{-webkit-flex-direction:column;-ms-flex-direction:column;flex-direction:column;margin:-0.5rem 0;gap:0rem;}}/*!sc*/
@media screen and (min-width: 768px){.hs-form .form-columns-1,.hs-form .form-columns-2,.hs-form .form-columns-3{-webkit-flex-direction:row;-ms-flex-direction:row;flex-direction:row;margin:-1rem 0;gap:1.5rem;}}/*!sc*/

@media screen and (min-width: 768px){.hs-form .hs_recaptcha{margin:2rem 0;}}/*!sc*/
.hs-form .input{line-height:0;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form input[type="text"],.hs-form input[type="tel"],.hs-form input[type="email"],.hs-form input[type="number"],.hs-form textarea,.hs-form select{font-family:"Inter",sans-serif;border:1px solid #969696;width:100%;border-radius:1.5rem;padding:0.625rem 1.5rem;margin-top:0.25rem;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form input[type="text"]:hover,.hs-form input[type="tel"]:hover,.hs-form input[type="email"]:hover,.hs-form input[type="number"]:hover,.hs-form textarea:hover,.hs-form select:hover{background-color:#f9f9f9;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form input[type="text"]:focus,.hs-form input[type="tel"]:focus,.hs-form input[type="email"]:focus,.hs-form input[type="number"]:focus,.hs-form textarea:focus,.hs-form select:focus{border-color:#0f0f0f;color:#0f0f0f;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form input[type="submit"]{background-color:#0f0f0f;font-size:1rem;color:#ffffff;padding:0.375rem 1.375rem;line-height:1.75rem;border:1px solid #0f0f0f;border-radius:6.25rem;font-weight:400;cursor:pointer;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form input[type="radio"]{cursor:pointer;width:1.5rem;height:1.5rem;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form input[type="radio"]:hover{border:1px solid #0f0f0f;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form input[type="radio"]:checked::before{background-color:#fdda24;border:6px solid #0f0f0f;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form input[type="radio"]::before{content:"";display:inline-block;width:1.5rem;height:1.5rem;margin-right:0.5rem;border-radius:50%;border:1px solid #969696;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form input[type="radio"]::before:hover{background-color:#f9f9f9;}/*!sc*/
.hs-field-desc{font-weight:400;font-size:0.75rem;line-height:2;margin:0.5rem 0;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form-checkbox,.hs-form-booleancheckbox,.hs-form-booleancheckbox-display{display:-webkit-box;display:-webkit-flex;display:-ms-flexbox;display:flex;-webkit-align-items:flex-end;-webkit-box-align:flex-end;-ms-flex-align:flex-end;align-items:flex-end;-webkit-box-pack:start;-ms-flex-pack:start;-webkit-justify-content:flex-start;justify-content:flex-start;-webkit-box-flex-wrap:nowrap;-webkit-flex-wrap:nowrap;-ms-flex-wrap:nowrap;flex-wrap:nowrap;font-weight:400;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form-checkbox input[type="checkbox"],.hs-form-booleancheckbox input[type="checkbox"],.hs-form-booleancheckbox-display input[type="checkbox"]{-webkit-appearance:none;-moz-appearance:none;-ms-appearance:none;appearance:none;width:1.5rem;height:1.5rem;border:1px solid #969696;border-radius:0.25rem;background-repeat:no-repeat;-webkit-background-position:center;background-position:center;cursor:pointer;font-weight:400;min-width:1.5rem;margin:0;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form-checkbox input[type="checkbox"]:hover,.hs-form-booleancheckbox input[type="checkbox"]:hover,.hs-form-booleancheckbox-display input[type="checkbox"]:hover{background-color:#f2f2f2;border:1 solid #0f0f0f;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form-checkbox input[type="checkbox"]:hover:checked,.hs-form-booleancheckbox input[type="checkbox"]:hover:checked,.hs-form-booleancheckbox-display input[type="checkbox"]:hover:checked,.hs-form-checkbox input[type="checkbox"]:checked,.hs-form-booleancheckbox input[type="checkbox"]:checked,.hs-form-booleancheckbox-display input[type="checkbox"]:checked{border:1 solid #0f0f0f;background-color:#0f0f0f;background-image:url('/images/check-yellow.svg');}/*!sc*/
.hs-form-checkbox input[type="checkbox"]:checked:hover,.hs-form-booleancheckbox input[type="checkbox"]:checked:hover,.hs-form-booleancheckbox-display input[type="checkbox"]:checked:hover{border:1 solid #0f0f0f;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form-checkbox span,.hs-form-booleancheckbox span,.hs-form-booleancheckbox-display span{font-size:0.75rem;line-height:1.5rem;display:-webkit-box;display:-webkit-flex;display:-ms-flexbox;display:flex;-webkit-align-items:center;-webkit-box-align:center;-ms-flex-align:center;align-items:center;-webkit-box-pack:center;-ms-flex-pack:center;-webkit-justify-content:center;justify-content:center;font-weight:400;-webkit-flex-basis:fit-content;-ms-flex-preferred-size:fit-content;flex-basis:fit-content;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form-checkbox label,.hs-form-booleancheckbox label,.hs-form-booleancheckbox-display label{display:-webkit-box;display:-webkit-flex;display:-ms-flexbox;display:flex;-webkit-align-items:flex-start;-webkit-box-align:flex-start;-ms-flex-align:flex-start;align-items:flex-start;margin:0.5rem 0;gap:0.5rem;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form-booleancheckbox-display span{display:inline;}/*!sc*/
.hs-fieldtype-select select{-webkit-appearance:none;-moz-appearance:none;-ms-appearance:none;appearance:none;padding-right:2rem;}/*!sc*/
.hs-fieldtype-select .input{display:inline-block;width:100%;position:relative;}/*!sc*/
.hs-fieldtype-select .input:after{content:"";position:absolute;top:1rem;right:1rem;width:1rem;height:1rem;background-image:url(/images/chevron-down.svg);background-repeat:no-repeat;-webkit-background-position:center;background-position:center;pointer-events:none;cursor:pointer;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form-radio{margin:0.5rem 0;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form-radio span{font-size:0.75rem;line-height:1.5rem;display:-webkit-box;display:-webkit-flex;display:-ms-flexbox;display:flex;-webkit-align-items:center;-webkit-box-align:center;-ms-flex-align:center;align-items:center;-webkit-box-pack:center;-ms-flex-pack:center;-webkit-justify-content:center;justify-content:center;font-weight:400;-webkit-flex-basis:fit-content;-ms-flex-preferred-size:fit-content;flex-basis:fit-content;color:#000;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form-radio label{display:-webkit-box;display:-webkit-flex;display:-ms-flexbox;display:flex;gap:0.5rem;-webkit-align-items:center;-webkit-box-align:center;-ms-flex-align:center;align-items:center;}/*!sc*/
.hs-form-radio input{margin:0;width:1rem;height:1rem;}/*!sc*/
input[type=file]{margin-top:0.5rem;}/*!sc*/
.hs_error_rollup{border:0.125rem solid #ff3f00;border-radius:0.125rem;line-height:2rem;padding:0.75rem 1.5rem;margin-bottom:2.5rem;}/*!sc*/

:root{--mono-1:#f9f9f9;--mono-2:#00a7b5;--mono-3:#969696;--hue-1:#fdda24;--hue-2:#00a7b5;--hue-3:#b7ace8;--hue-4:#fdda24;--hue-5:#00a7b5;--hue-5-2:rgba(0, 167, 181, 0.8);--hue-6:#00a7b5;--syntax-fg:#b7ace8;--syntax-bg:#262626;--syntax-gutter:rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.2);--syntax-guide:rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1);--syntax-selection-color:rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1);--syntax-gutter-background-color-selected:#262626;--syntax-cursor-line:#ffffff;}/*!sc*/
code[class*="language-"],pre[class*="language-"]{background:var(--syntax-bg);color:var(--mono-1);direction:ltr;text-align:left;white-space:pre;word-spacing:normal;word-break:normal;font-size:0.875rem;line-height:1.125rem;-moz-tab-size:2;-o-tab-size:2;-moz-tab-size:2;tab-size:2;-webkit-hyphens:none;-moz-hyphens:none;-ms-hyphens:none;-webkit-hyphens:none;-moz-hyphens:none;-ms-hyphens:none;hyphens:none;}/*!sc*/
code[class*="language-"]::-moz-selection,code[class*="language-"] *::-moz-selection,pre[class*="language-"] *::-moz-selection{background:var(--syntax-selection-color);color:inherit;text-shadow:none;}/*!sc*/
code[class*="language-"]::selection,code[class*="language-"] *::selection,pre[class*="language-"] *::selection{background:var(--syntax-selection-color);color:inherit;text-shadow:none;}/*!sc*/
pre[class*="language-"]{padding:1em;margin:0.5em 0;overflow:auto;border-radius:0.3em;}/*!sc*/
:not(pre)>code[class*="language-"]{padding:0.2em 0.3em;border-radius:0.3em;white-space:normal;}/*!sc*/
@media print{code[class*="language-"],pre[class*="language-"]{text-shadow:none;}}/*!sc*/
.token.comment,.token.prolog,.token.cdata{color:var(--mono-3);}/*!sc*/
.token.doctype,.token.punctuation,.token.entity{color:var(--mono-1);}/*!sc*/
.token.attr-name,.token.class-name,.token.boolean,.token.constant,.token.number,.token.atrule{color:var(--hue-6);}/*!sc*/
.token.keyword{color:var(--hue-3);}/*!sc*/
.token.property,.token.tag,.token.symbol,.token.deleted,.token.important{color:var(--hue-5);}/*!sc*/
.token.selector,.token.string,.token.char,.token.builtin,.token.inserted,.token.regex,.token.attr-value,.token.attr-value>.token.punctuation{color:var(--hue-4);}/*!sc*/
.token.variable,.token.operator,.token.function{color:var(--hue-2);}/*!sc*/
.token.url{color:var(--hue-1);}/*!sc*/
.language-rust .token.attr-name{color:var(--hue-4);}/*!sc*/
.token.attr-value>.token.punctuation.attr-equals,.token.special-attr>.token.attr-value>.token.value.css{color:var(--mono-1);}/*!sc*/
.language-css .token.selector{color:var(--hue-5);}/*!sc*/
.language-css .token.property{color:var(--mono-1);}/*!sc*/
.language-css .token.function,.language-css .token.url>.token.function{color:var(--hue-1);}/*!sc*/
.language-css .token.url>.token.string.url{color:var(--hue-4);}/*!sc*/
.language-css .token.important,.language-css .token.atrule .token.rule{color:var(--hue-3);}/*!sc*/
.language-javascript .token.operator{color:var(--hue-3);}/*!sc*/
.language-javascript .token.template-string>.token.interpolation>.token.interpolation-punctuation.punctuation{color:var(--hue-5-2);}/*!sc*/
.language-json .token.operator{color:var(--mono-1);}/*!sc*/
.language-json .token.property{color:var(--hue-3);}/*!sc*/
.language-json .token.null.keyword{color:var(--hue-6);}/*!sc*/
.language-markdown .token.url,.language-markdown .token.url>.token.operator,.language-markdown .token.url-reference.url>.token.string{color:var(--mono-1);}/*!sc*/
.language-markdown .token.url>.token.content{color:var(--hue-2);}/*!sc*/
.language-markdown .token.url>.token.url,.language-markdown .token.url-reference.url{color:var(--hue-1);}/*!sc*/
.language-markdown .token.blockquote.punctuation,.language-markdown .token.hr.punctuation{color:var(--mono-3);font-style:italic;}/*!sc*/
.language-markdown .token.code-snippet{color:var(--hue-4);}/*!sc*/
.language-markdown .token.bold .token.content{color:var(--hue-6);}/*!sc*/
.language-markdown .token.italic .token.content{color:var(--hue-3);}/*!sc*/
.language-markdown .token.strike .token.content,.language-markdown .token.strike .token.punctuation,.language-markdown .token.list.punctuation,.language-markdown .token.title.important>.token.punctuation{color:var(--hue-5);}/*!sc*/
.token.bold{font-weight:bold;}/*!sc*/
.token.italic{font-style:italic;}/*!sc*/
.token.entity{cursor:help;}/*!sc*/
.token.namespace{opacity:0.8;}/*!sc*/
.token.token.tab:not(:empty):before,.token.token.cr:before,.token.token.lf:before,.token.token.space:before{color:var(--syntax-guide);text-shadow:none;}/*!sc*/
div.code-toolbar>.toolbar.toolbar>.toolbar-item{margin-right:0.4em;}/*!sc*/
div.code-toolbar>.toolbar.toolbar>.toolbar-item>button,div.code-toolbar>.toolbar.toolbar>.toolbar-item>a,div.code-toolbar>.toolbar.toolbar>.toolbar-item>span{background:var(--syntax-gutter-background-color-selected);color:var(--mono-2);padding:0.5em 1em;border-radius:0.3em;cursor:pointer;}/*!sc*/
div.code-toolbar>.toolbar.toolbar>.toolbar-item>button:hover,div.code-toolbar>.toolbar.toolbar>.toolbar-item>button:focus,div.code-toolbar>.toolbar.toolbar>.toolbar-item>a:hover,div.code-toolbar>.toolbar.toolbar>.toolbar-item>a:focus,div.code-toolbar>.toolbar.toolbar>.toolbar-item>span:hover,div.code-toolbar>.toolbar.toolbar>.toolbar-item>span:focus{background:var(--syntax-selection-color);color:var(--mono-1);}/*!sc*/
.line-highlight.line-highlight{background:var(--syntax-cursor-line);}/*!sc*/
.line-highlight.line-highlight:before,.line-highlight.line-highlight[data-end]:after{background:var(--syntax-gutter-background-color-selected);color:var(--mono-1);padding:0.1em 0.6em;border-radius:0.3em;box-shadow:0 2px 0 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.2);}/*!sc*/

.line-numbers.line-numbers .line-numbers-rows,.command-line .command-line-prompt{border-right-color:var(--syntax-guide);}/*!sc*/
.line-numbers .line-numbers-rows>span:before,.command-line .command-line-prompt>span:before{color:var(--syntax-gutter);}/*!sc*/
.rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-1,.rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-5,.rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-9{color:var(--hue-5);}/*!sc*/
.rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-2,.rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-6,.rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-10{color:var(--hue-4);}/*!sc*/

pre.diff-highlight>code .token.token.deleted:not(.prefix)::-moz-selection,pre.diff-highlight>code .token.token.deleted:not(.prefix) *::-moz-selection,pre>code.diff-highlight .token.token.deleted:not(.prefix)::-moz-selection,pre>code.diff-highlight .token.token.deleted:not(.prefix) *::-moz-selection{background-color:hsla(353,95%,66%,0.25);}/*!sc*/
pre.diff-highlight>code .token.token.deleted:not(.prefix)::selection,pre.diff-highlight>code .token.token.deleted:not(.prefix) *::selection,pre>code.diff-highlight .token.token.deleted:not(.prefix)::selection,pre>code.diff-highlight .token.token.deleted:not(.prefix) *::selection{background-color:hsla(353,95%,66%,0.25);}/*!sc*/
pre.diff-highlight>code .token.token.inserted:not(.prefix),pre>code.diff-highlight .token.token.inserted:not(.prefix){background-color:hsla(137,100%,55%,0.15);}/*!sc*/
pre.diff-highlight>code .token.token.inserted:not(.prefix)::-moz-selection,pre.diff-highlight>code .token.token.inserted:not(.prefix) *::-moz-selection,pre>code.diff-highlight .token.token.inserted:not(.prefix)::-moz-selection,pre>code.diff-highlight .token.token.inserted:not(.prefix) *::-moz-selection{background-color:hsla(135,73%,55%,0.25);}/*!sc*/
pre.diff-highlight>code .token.token.inserted:not(.prefix)::selection,pre.diff-highlight>code .token.token.inserted:not(.prefix) *::selection,pre>code.diff-highlight .token.token.inserted:not(.prefix)::selection,pre>code.diff-highlight .token.token.inserted:not(.prefix) *::selection{background-color:hsla(135,73%,55%,0.25);}/*!sc*/
.prism-previewer.prism-previewer:before,.prism-previewer-gradient.prism-previewer-gradient div{border-color:hsl(224,13%,17%);}/*!sc*/
.prism-previewer-color.prism-previewer-color:before,.prism-previewer-gradient.prism-previewer-gradient div,.prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing:before{border-radius:0.3em;}/*!sc*/
.prism-previewer.prism-previewer:after{border-top-color:hsl(224,13%,17%);}/*!sc*/
.prism-previewer-flipped.prism-previewer-flipped.after{border-bottom-color:hsl(224,13%,17%);}/*!sc*/
.prism-previewer-angle.prism-previewer-angle:before,.prism-previewer-time.prism-previewer-time:before,.prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing{background:hsl(219,13%,22%);}/*!sc*/
.prism-previewer-angle.prism-previewer-angle circle,.prism-previewer-time.prism-previewer-time circle{stroke:var(--mono-1);stroke-opacity:1;}/*!sc*/
.prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing circle,.prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing path,.prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing line{stroke:var(--mono-1);}/*!sc*/
.prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing circle{fill:transparent;}/*!sc*/
pre[class*="language-"].line-numbers{position:relative;padding-left:3.8em;counter-reset:linenumber;}/*!sc*/
pre[class*="language-"].line-numbers>code{position:relative;white-space:inherit;}/*!sc*/
.line-numbers .line-numbers-rows{position:absolute;pointer-events:none;top:0;font-size:100%;left:-3.8em;width:3em;letter-spacing:-1px;border-right:1px solid;-webkit-user-select:none;-moz-user-select:none;-ms-user-select:none;-webkit-user-select:none;-moz-user-select:none;-ms-user-select:none;user-select:none;}/*!sc*/
.line-numbers-rows>span{display:block;counter-increment:linenumber;}/*!sc*/
.line-numbers-rows>span:before{content:counter(linenumber);color:red;display:block;padding-right:0.8em;text-align:right;}/*!sc*/
data-styled.g135[id="sc-global-eMkfHm1"]{content:"sc-global-eMkfHm1,"}/*!sc*/
.katex{font-family:'lora','lora Fallback',serif;line-height:1.2;text-indent:0;text-rendering:auto;font-size:1.5rem;}/*!sc*/
.katex *{-ms-high-contrast-adjust:none!important;}/*!sc*/
.katex *{border-color:currentColor;}/*!sc*/
.katex .katex-version::after{content:"0.16.8";}/*!sc*/
.katex .katex-mathml{position:absolute;clip:rect(1px,1px,1px,1px);padding:0;border:0;height:1px;width:1px;overflow:hidden;}/*!sc*/
.katex .katex-html>.newline{display:block;}/*!sc*/
.katex .base{position:relative;display:inline-block;white-space:nowrap;width:-webkit-min-content;width:-moz-min-content;width:-webkit-min-content;width:-moz-min-content;width:min-content;}/*!sc*/
.katex .strut{display:inline-block;}/*!sc*/
.katex .textbf{font-weight:bold;}/*!sc*/
.katex .textit{font-style:italic;}/*!sc*/
.katex .mathnormal{font-style:italic;}/*!sc*/
.katex .mathit{font-style:italic;}/*!sc*/
.katex .mathrm{font-style:normal;}/*!sc*/
.katex .mathbf{font-weight:bold;}/*!sc*/
.katex .boldsymbol{font-weight:bold;font-style:italic;}/*!sc*/
.katex .mathboldsf,.katex .textboldsf{font-weight:bold;}/*!sc*/
.katex .mathitsf,.katex .textitsf{font-style:italic;}/*!sc*/
.katex .mainrm{font-style:normal;}/*!sc*/
.katex .vlist-t{display:inline-table;table-layout:fixed;border-collapse:collapse;}/*!sc*/
.katex .vlist-r{display:table-row;}/*!sc*/
.katex .vlist{display:table-cell;vertical-align:bottom;position:relative;}/*!sc*/
.katex .vlist>span{display:block;height:0;position:relative;}/*!sc*/
.katex .vlist>span>span{display:inline-block;}/*!sc*/
.katex .vlist>span>.pstrut{overflow:hidden;width:0;}/*!sc*/
.katex .vlist-t2{margin-right:-2px;}/*!sc*/
.katex .vlist-s{display:table-cell;vertical-align:bottom;font-size:1px;width:2px;min-width:2px;}/*!sc*/
.katex .vbox{display:-webkit-inline-box;display:-webkit-inline-flex;display:-ms-inline-flexbox;display:inline-flex;-webkit-flex-direction:column;-ms-flex-direction:column;flex-direction:column;-webkit-align-items:baseline;-webkit-box-align:baseline;-ms-flex-align:baseline;align-items:baseline;}/*!sc*/
.katex .hbox{display:-webkit-inline-box;display:-webkit-inline-flex;display:-ms-inline-flexbox;display:inline-flex;-webkit-flex-direction:row;-ms-flex-direction:row;flex-direction:row;width:100%;}/*!sc*/
.katex .thinbox{display:-webkit-inline-box;display:-webkit-inline-flex;display:-ms-inline-flexbox;display:inline-flex;-webkit-flex-direction:row;-ms-flex-direction:row;flex-direction:row;width:0;max-width:0;}/*!sc*/
.katex .msupsub{text-align:left;}/*!sc*/
.katex .mfrac>span>span{text-align:center;}/*!sc*/
.katex .mfrac .frac-line{display:inline-block;width:100%;border-bottom-style:solid;}/*!sc*/

.katex .mspace{display:inline-block;}/*!sc*/
.katex .llap,.katex .rlap,.katex .clap{width:0;position:relative;}/*!sc*/
.katex .llap>.inner,.katex .rlap>.inner,.katex .clap>.inner{position:absolute;}/*!sc*/
.katex .llap>.fix,.katex .rlap>.fix,.katex .clap>.fix{display:inline-block;}/*!sc*/
.katex .llap>.inner{right:0;}/*!sc*/
.katex .rlap>.inner,.katex .clap>.inner{left:0;}/*!sc*/
.katex .clap>.inner>span{margin-left:-50%;margin-right:50%;}/*!sc*/
.katex .rule{display:inline-block;border:solid 0;position:relative;}/*!sc*/
.katex .overline .overline-line,.katex .underline .underline-line,.katex .hline{display:inline-block;width:100%;border-bottom-style:solid;}/*!sc*/
.katex .hdashline{display:inline-block;width:100%;border-bottom-style:dashed;}/*!sc*/

sc-94e76d4b-0 bA-dJbf XDajr">Developers</p><h1 class="sc-579c22d1-2 sc-94e76d4b-1 bGKdIx jXwbls">Your Paycheck Is Private. So, Why Isn’t Your Blockchain Payment?</h1></div><div class="sc-hAqnzo sc-94e76d4b-2 hitExr gSSQkd"><figure width="100%" height="100%" class="sc-iKTbCL sc-gnpauu sc-hxyrwO jJeuXN fIRjaX dKpNEv"><picture><source srcSet="data:image/png;base64,"/><img srcSet="data:image/png;base64," loading="eager" class="sc-xuTxv bCFiyR"/></picture></figure></div><div class="sc-hAqnzo sc-94e76d4b-4 cJLrxc kJtwul"><div class="sc-94e76d4b-6 dWYGiK"><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-4 eLhqkv bPMqN">Author</p><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-4 fnthtp bPMqN">James Bachini</p></div><div class="sc-94e76d4b-6 dWYGiK"><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-4 eLhqkv bPMqN">Publishing date</p><time dateTime="2026-01-15T00:00:00.000Z" class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-4 fnthtp bPMqN"></time></div><div class="sc-94e76d4b-6 gSEQgI"><div class="sc-94e76d4b-5 gfnMuN"></div></div></div></div></div></section></div><div class="sc-iKTbCL sc-gnpauu sc-dkiZCX sc-8960526-1 fIRjaX gISiLb cdsFTG"><section id="852e8c0a467d" class="sc-8960526-0 hktVrm"><div class="sc-iKTbCL sc-gnpauu sc-fLVvQH sc-320d54e1-0 sc-a236a696-0 jUhiFf fIRjaX cHQWYL evLKQF jjrsBs" width="100%"><div class="sc-e90eaf70-3 emCDng"><div class="sc-e90eaf70-7 haqGDy"><div class="sc-577d91a0-3 erWDBq"><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 fnthtp czmbPC sc-577d91a0-9 gSqEit">In traditional finance, privacy is expected. It’s baked in as default, from banking services to payroll.</p><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 fnthtp czmbPC sc-577d91a0-9 gSqEit">Yet the moment you move money on a public blockchain, anyone with a block explorer can view a transaction trail that could make an accountant blush.</p><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 fnthtp czmbPC sc-577d91a0-9 gSqEit">There is a historical precedent here where the first attempts to create a peer-to-peer electronic cash focused on transparency above all else. In pioneering an open financial system based on digital scarcity, users&#x27; privacy was overlooked. Complete blockchain transparency became a feature, not a bug, that grew throughout the industry.</p><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 fnthtp czmbPC sc-577d91a0-9 gSqEit">Pseudonymity became the default, where a blockchain address served as a thin alias rather than true confidentiality. This worked well enough in the early days, when transactions were small, users were few, and nobody imagined the forensic tools that would later tie those aliases back to real identities, spending habits, counterparties, and business relationships.</p><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 fnthtp czmbPC sc-577d91a0-9 gSqEit">Over time, the industry learned the hard way that pseudonymity isn’t the same as financial privacy. It’s a temporary mask that slips the moment someone looks closely enough, leaving transactions exposed in ways no traditional financial system would ever permit.</p><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 fnthtp czmbPC sc-577d91a0-9 gSqEit">Enterprises taking their first steps into decentralized finance require data protection. Payroll, supplier payment, and internal treasury finance all demand confidential financial transactions. Without privacy, it is impossible to meet compliance requirements, protect competitive advantage, or maintain basic end-user expectations. This is the next step forward on a road that can lead to mainstream adoption of digital assets.</p></div></div></div></div></section></div><div class="sc-iKTbCL sc-gnpauu sc-dkiZCX sc-8960526-1 fIRjaX gISiLb cdsFTG"><section id="c2b06bf3deea" class="sc-8960526-0 hktVrm"><div class="sc-iKTbCL sc-gnpauu sc-fLVvQH sc-320d54e1-0 jUhiFf fIRjaX cHQWYL evLKQF" width="100%"><div class="sc-iKTbCL sc-gnpauu sc-flUrZP sc-99a47be6-0 fIRjaX bxIUGr llHrbm"><div class="sc-hAqnzo gEgBns"><div class="sc-e90eaf70-3 emCDng"><h2 class="sc-579c22d1-1 gjILUk sc-e90eaf70-6 kmSstj">In 2026 and beyond</h2></div></div><div class="sc-hAqnzo gEgBns"><div class="sc-e90eaf70-3 emCDng"><div class="sc-e90eaf70-7 UyiPm"><div class="sc-577d91a0-3 erWDBq"><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 fnthtp czmbPC sc-577d91a0-9 gSqEit">We will see the emergence of new financial privacy-focused technologies on Stellar.</p><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 fnthtp czmbPC sc-577d91a0-9 gSqEit"><a aria-label="The Protocol X-Ray upgrade" class="sc-fpSqyW sc-3f996000-5 bVxYjp jGieIs" href="https://stellar.org/blog/developers/announcing-stellar-x-ray-protocol-25"><span class="sc-3f996000-6 eKbMIk"><span class="sc-3f996000-0 cDlpCt">The Protocol X-Ray upgrade</span></span></a> (<em class="sc-577d91a0-16 dIIeyU">expected on Testnet Jan 7th, then Mainnet Jan 22nd<a href="https://x.com/StellarOrg/status/1993012871757021345" aria-label="*" class="sc-gJhJfT irqYQk sc-fpSqyW sc-3f996000-5 bVxYjp jGieIs" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><span class="sc-3f996000-6 eKbMIk"><span class="sc-3f996000-0 cDlpCt">*</span></span></a></em>) will introduce cryptographic primitives such as BN254 curves and Poseidon hashing to Soroban. For developers, this means we can verify zero-knowledge proofs within a Stellar smart contract!</p><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 fnthtp czmbPC sc-577d91a0-9 gSqEit">At a fundamental level, this will allow us to prove attributes of data without revealing the data itself.</p><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 fnthtp czmbPC sc-577d91a0-9 gSqEit">Privacy and transparency aren’t going to be opposites; we will use these tools to create new systems that provide personal financial privacy while retaining the advantages and transparency of a public blockchain.</p></div></div></div></div></div></div></section></div><div class="sc-iKTbCL sc-gnpauu sc-dkiZCX sc-8960526-1 fIRjaX gISiLb cdsFTG"><section id="20b827a35300" class="sc-8960526-0 hktVrm"><div class="sc-iKTbCL sc-gnpauu sc-fLVvQH sc-320d54e1-0 sc-a236a696-0 jUhiFf fIRjaX cHQWYL evLKQF jjrsBs" width="100%"><div class="sc-e90eaf70-3 emCDng"><h2 class="sc-579c22d1-1 gjILUk sc-e90eaf70-6 kmSstj">Privacy with verifiable integrity</h2><div class="sc-e90eaf70-7 haqGDy"><div class="sc-577d91a0-3 erWDBq"><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 fnthtp czmbPC sc-577d91a0-9 gSqEit">Consider a practical example in which we want to prove that an address has sufficient funds to complete a transaction without revealing the balance. While doing this, we still need complete verifiable transparency over the total amount of funds issued across the network to ensure that the transaction did not affect the total supply.</p><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 fnthtp czmbPC sc-577d91a0-9 gSqEit">With zero-knowledge tooling becoming accessible to Stellar developers, we are finally entering a phase where financial privacy can match real-world expectations without sacrificing the integrity of an open ledger.</p><p class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 fnthtp czmbPC sc-577d91a0-9 gSqEit">The next generation of decentralized applications will adopt a model in which individual financial records remain shielded, compliance checks are verifiable, and public transparency remains intact where it matters. When that happens, the future of finance will feel as private as payroll.</p></div></div></div></div></section></div><div class="sc-iKTbCL sc-gnpauu sc-dkiZCX sc-8960526-1 fIRjaX gISiLb cdsFTG"><section id="e0079d9068c5" data-theme="light" class="sc-8960526-0 hktVrm"><div class="sc-iKTbCL sc-gnpauu sc-la-DyAJ fIRjaX jiMAvT"><div class="sc-iKTbCL sc-gnpauu sc-fLVvQH sc-320d54e1-0 class="sc-beef955c-2 jBGUCR" href="/es/blog/desarrolladores/privacidad-financiera"><span class="sc-4251f05a-0 sc-4251f05a-3 sc-beef955c-0 elUAL czmbPC dvFKTW">Español</span></a></div></div></div></footer></div></div></div></div><script id="__NEXT_DATA__" type="application/json">{"props":{"pageProps":{"preventIndexing":false,"_createdAt":"2026-01-14T14:55:24Z","_id":"f4a6e509-23d1-419f-9d87-db356d0992b5","_rev":"sO6pEa5iOp8NEYlrYONKhm","_system":{"base":{"id":"f4a6e509-23d1-419f-9d87-db356d0992b5","rev":"dY33eTGN3lr264DYE3xwHK"}},"_type":"blogPage","_updatedAt":"2026-01-16T17:13:49Z","author":"James Bachini","blogType":"Developers","content":[{"_key":"852e8c0a467d","_type":"oneColumnHtmlSection","backgroundImage":{"blur":false,"image":null,"scrim":false},"body":[{"_key":"f5fefe6ca87d","_type":"richText","children":[{"_key":"4d9602626ba8","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":"In traditional finance, privacy is expected. It’s baked in as default, from banking services to payroll."}],"markDefs":[],"style":"normal"},{"_key":"e4a6c1d2fb77","_type":"richText","children":[{"_key":"f1cb39cf5bed","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":"Yet the moment you move money on a public blockchain, anyone with a block explorer can view a transaction trail that could make an accountant blush."}],"markDefs":[],"style":"normal"},{"_key":"191d04480103","_type":"richText","children":[{"_key":"2c216afefc84","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":"There is a historical precedent here where the first attempts to create a peer-to-peer electronic cash focused on transparency above all else. In pioneering an open financial system based on digital scarcity, users' privacy was overlooked. Complete blockchain transparency became a feature, not a bug, that grew throughout the industry."}],"markDefs":[],"style":"normal"},{"_key":"c63b226ba55d","_type":"richText","children":[{"_key":"52305fbad0f7","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":"Pseudonymity became the default, where a blockchain address served as a thin alias rather than true confidentiality. This worked well enough in the early days, when transactions were small, users were few, and nobody imagined the forensic tools that would later tie those aliases back to real identities, spending habits, counterparties, and business relationships."}],"markDefs":[],"style":"normal"},{"_key":"190d903b7542","_type":"richText","children":[{"_key":"65133566cb50","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":"Over time, the industry learned the hard way that pseudonymity isn’t the same as financial privacy. It’s a temporary mask that slips the moment someone looks closely enough, leaving transactions exposed in ways no traditional financial system would ever permit."}],"markDefs":[],"style":"normal"},{"_key":"78c61d620cba","_type":"richText","children":[{"_key":"48de82a048a0","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":"Enterprises taking their first steps into decentralized finance require data protection. Payroll, supplier payment, and internal treasury finance all demand confidential financial transactions. Without privacy, it is impossible to meet compliance requirements, protect competitive advantage, or maintain basic end-user expectations. This is the next step forward on a road that can lead to mainstream adoption of digital assets."}],"markDefs":[],"style":"normal"}],"ctas":null,"eventPageLumaEvent":null,"hasBackgroundImage":false,"showHostedBy":null,"slug":null,"theme":"lightTheme"},{"_key":"c2b06bf3deea","_type":"twoColumnHtmlSection","backgroundImage":{"blur":false,"image":null,"scrim":false},"body":[{"_key":"a26b63ba87d3","_type":"richText","children":[{"_key":"c3058181d1e2","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":"We will see the emergence of new financial privacy-focused technologies on Stellar."}],"markDefs":[],"style":"normal"},{"_key":"d418cdec4516","_type":"richText","children":[{"_key":"2fcc7c38a132","_type":"span","marks":["a168ff7fa8b8"],"text":"The Protocol X-Ray upgrade"},{"_key":"e645c3b11130","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":" ("},{"_key":"b21490277ef5","_type":"span","marks":["em"],"text":"expected on Testnet Jan 7th, then Mainnet Jan 22nd"},{"_key":"df38ee8b1d75","_type":"span","marks":["896b9df326e3","em"],"text":"*"},{"_key":"4834881773bb","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":") will introduce cryptographic primitives such as BN254 curves and Poseidon hashing to Soroban. For developers, this means we can verify zero-knowledge proofs within a Stellar smart contract!"}],"markDefs":[{"_key":"a168ff7fa8b8","_type":"link","href":"https://stellar.org/blog/developers/announcing-stellar-x-ray-protocol-25"},{"_key":"896b9df326e3","_type":"link","href":"https://x.com/StellarOrg/status/1993012871757021345"}],"style":"normal"},{"_key":"cc753cfd60b5","_type":"richText","children":[{"_key":"ee90601ffa13","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":"At a fundamental level, this will allow us to prove attributes of data without revealing the data itself."}],"markDefs":[],"style":"normal"},{"_key":"f94d8eebf72b","_type":"richText","children":[{"_key":"101cedb371f3","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":"Privacy and transparency aren’t going to be opposites; we will use these tools to create new systems that provide personal financial privacy while retaining the advantages and transparency of a public blockchain."}],"markDefs":[],"style":"normal"}],"ctas":null,"hasBackgroundImage":false,"multiColumn":true,"slug":null,"theme":"lightTheme","title":"In 2026 and beyond"},{"_key":"20b827a35300","_type":"oneColumnHtmlSection","backgroundImage":{"blur":false,"image":null,"scrim":false},"body":[{"_key":"06f00f9ce512","_type":"richText","children":[{"_key":"0520006585b8","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":"Consider a practical example in which we want to prove that an address has sufficient funds to complete a transaction without revealing the balance. While doing this, we still need complete verifiable transparency over the total amount of funds issued across the network to ensure that the transaction did not affect the total supply."}],"markDefs":[],"style":"normal"},{"_key":"d8b91a2b05bc","_type":"richText","children":[{"_key":"d8e75f56f354","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":"With zero-knowledge tooling becoming accessible to Stellar developers, we are finally entering a phase where financial privacy can match real-world expectations without sacrificing the integrity of an open ledger."}],"markDefs":[],"style":"normal"},{"_key":"dd9a74155a23","_type":"richText","children":[{"_key":"65f66d488331","_type":"span","marks":[],"text":"The next generation of decentralized applications will adopt a model in which individual financial records remain shielded, compliance checks are verifiable, and public transparency remains intact where it matters. When that happens, the future of finance will feel as private as payroll."}],"markDefs":[],"style":"normal"}],"ctas":null,"eventPageLumaEvent":null,"hasBackgroundImage":false,"showHostedBy":null,"slug":null,"theme":"lightTheme","title":"Privacy with verifiable integrity"},{"_createdAt":"2025-09-11T02:22:07Z","_id":"7461c08b-948c-4eba-83d3-cc8a3891cd88","_key":"e0079d9068c5","_ref":"7461c08b-948c-4eba-83d3-cc8a3891cd88","_rev":"sO6pEa5iOp8NEYlrYONCFA","_system":{"base":{"id":"7461c08b-948c-4eba-83d3-cc8a3891cd88","rev":"vvx7DZzhGQsYVmHDPEyoy9"}},"_type":"dynamicCardCarouselSection","_updatedAt":"2026-01-16T17:13:35Z","amountOfCards":5,"aspectRatios":["16:9"],"blogType":{"_ref":"blog-type-developers","_type":"reference"},"body":null,"cardSize":"medium","collectionType":"blogPage","cta":null,"ctaLabel":"View","eventCategory":null,"eventStatus":null,"eventType":"all","items":[{"_id":"50562d11-f90e-4e4d-9634-e2a47daa5aed","_type":"blogPage","author":"Nicolas @media screen and (min-width: 768px){.hs-form .form-columns-1,.hs-form .form-columns-2,.hs-form .form-columns-3{-webkit-flex-direction:row;-ms-flex-direction:row;flex-direction:row;margin:-1rem 0;gap:1.5rem;}}/*!sc*/
